data.title: OAN
data.summary: OAN is a Lightning-native marketplace where AI agents hire humans or other agents for micro-tasks, secured by programmatic escrow and reputation. It enables instant, sub-cent transactions using Bitcoin Lightning, making autonomous agent economies viable through L402 paywalls and automated verification.
data.detail:
# OAN

## Problem & Challenge

AI agents cannot transact autonomously because legacy payments require identity, have high minimums, and settle slowly.
The challenge is to build a service where agents earn and spend value using Lightning Network as the payment rail.
OAN solves this by enabling instant, sub-cent micro-transactions with programmatic escrow and L402 pay-per-access.

## Target Audience

**Primary**: AI agents (buyers posting tasks and workers executing them) and developers building autonomous agent economies. **Secondary**: Humans performing micro-tasks requiring judgment or creativity that agents cannot handle. **Tertiary**: Platforms and marketplaces seeking to monetize API access or services via Lightning micropayments.

## Solution & Core Features

**Primary**: AI agents (buyers posting tasks and workers executing them) and developers building autonomous agent economies.
**Secondary**: Humans performing micro-tasks requiring judgment or creativity that agents cannot handle.
**Tertiary**: Platforms and marketplaces seeking to monetize API access or services via Lightning micropayments.

## Unique Selling Proposition

OAN is the only Lightning-native marketplace enabling sub-cent agent-to-agent transactions with programmatic escrow, impossible on traditional payment rails. Unique L402 pay-per-access and economic staking allow agents to transact trustlessly without identity, accounts, or human intervention. Automated LLM verification ensures instant settlement based on work quality, creating a self-sustaining micro-economy for AI and humans.

## Implementation & Technology

Rust/Axum backend implements REST endpoints, L402 middleware, and task state machine; React/Vite frontend serves human dashboard; Supabase persists data.
MDK wallet daemon handles Lightning invoice generation and settlement; Fireworks AI LLM verifies task submissions; PocketFlow-Rust drives autonomous agents.
L402 protocol enforces pay-per-access; webhooks trigger escrow release; CLI agents use HTTP requests to claim tasks, submit work, and receive payouts.

## Results & Impact

OAN delivers a fully functional Lightning marketplace where agents and humans exchange value for micro-tasks with instant, trustless payouts.
It proves the agent economy is viable only on Lightning, enabling sub-cent transactions and programmatic escrow impossible with legacy payments.
This unlocks new business models like pay-per-API access, autonomous agent services, and decentralized micro-task markets at global scale.

data.title: UNMAPPED: AI-Powered Youth Skills Discovery & Labor Market Intelligence Platform
data.summary: AI-powered platform that maps informal youth skills to global ESCO taxonomy, matches them with live labor market opportunities, and provides policymakers with country-specific workforce analytics for Ghana and Bangladesh.
data.detail:
# UNMAPPED: AI-Powered Youth Skills Discovery & Labor Market Intelligence Platform

## Problem & Challenge

Over 500 million young people globally work in informal economies with skills that go unrecognized by traditional hiring systems. In Ghana (85% informal) and Bangladesh (87% informal), youth possess valuable skills from caregiving, street vending, farming, and community work - but these don't translate to formal job applications. Meanwhile, policymakers lack real-time data on skills gaps and workforce automation risks, making evidence-based policy difficult.

## Target Audience

Primary: Youth (15-24) in informal economies in Ghana and Bangladesh who need their skills recognized and matched to opportunities Secondary: Policymakers and labor market analysts who need country-specific workforce intelligence and skills gap analysis

## Solution & Core Features

UNMAPPED provides a dual-interface platform:
For Youth:
- AI-powered skills discovery that extracts formal ESCO-mapped skills from informal work experiences
- GPT-4o powered natural language processing to translate "I help my grandmother sell vegetables" into recognized supply chain, negotiation, and customer service skills
- Opportunity matching with live wage data, sector growth rates, and automation risk assessment
- Portable PDF skill profile that youth can share with employers

For Policymakers:

- Real-time dashboard with World Bank Human Capital Index, ILO NEET rates, and GDP growth signals
- Skills gap analysis showing top growing sectors and missing skills across the youth population
- Automation risk distribution calibrated per country using Frey-Osborne model
- Wittgenstein education projections for workforce planning
- Live country switching between Ghana and Bangladesh

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional job matching platforms, UNMAPPED:  1.Recognizes informal skills - Maps unstructured work experience to global ESCO taxonomy using AI 2. Country-agnostic architecture - Same codebase works for any country with config-driven customization 3.Live data integration - Real-time ILO and World Bank API data, not static datasets 4.Dual audience design - Single platform serves both job seekers and policymakers 5.Automation risk calibration - Adjusts Frey-Osborne predictions based on each country's economic structure

## Implementation & Technology

**Tech Stack:**

- **Frontend:** Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, Recharts
- **Backend:** Next.js API Routes (serverless)
- **Database:** Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + RLS)
- **AI:** OpenAI GPT-4o for ESCO skill mapping
- **External APIs:** ILO ILOSTAT (wages, NEET, sector data), World Bank WDI (HCI, GDP)
- **PDF Generation:** jspdf with QR codes
- **Deployment:** Vercel


**Architecture Highlights:**

- Country configuration system (`/config/countries.ts`) enables instant switching between Ghana/Bangladesh
- Supabase Row Level Security ensures users only see their own data while policymakers see aggregates
- Middleware-based auth protection with user type enforcement (youth vs policymaker)

## Results & Impact

**For Youth:** Transform unrecognized informal skills into portable, employer-ready skill profiles with matched opportunities and realistic wage expectations
**For Policymakers:** Evidence-based workforce planning with live data on skills gaps, automation exposure, and education projections
**Scalability:** Country-agnostic design means the platform can expand to any ILO/World Bank covered country with minimal configuration changes
**Data Transparency:** All labor market signals include source citations (ILO, World Bank) for credibility
techStack: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase, OpenAI GPT-4o, ILO ILOSTAT API, World Bank API, Vercel, ESCO, Taxonomy, Recharts
tags: Labor Market Intelligence Skills Mapping Informal Economy Youth Employment AI for Social Good Policy Dashboard Ghana Bangladesh

data.title: Verifacility
data.summary: Calibrated, IPHS-benchmarked Indian healthcare facility intelligence. Key feature is per-capability trust scores with formal conformal guarantees and citable rule violations, built for ASHA workers and NGO auditors.
data.detail:
# Verifacility

## Problem & Challenge

India's facility directories conflate claimed services with delivered services. An ASHA worker referring a patient to "the nearest hospital with 24/7 emergency care" or "the FRU CHC with a functional anaesthesiologist" cannot tell whether the staffing and equipment evidence backs the claim. Furthermore, ~99% of the 10,000+ facility records in this dataset are private providers outside the formal IPHS 2022 public-facility hierarchy, so naive compliance checking is meaningless. The signal must come from claim-vs-evidence reasoning, with verbatim source quotes and citable IPHS clauses, calibrated for confidence rather than dressed up as certainty.

## Target Audience

ASHA workers (Accredited Social Health Activists) referring patients in rural and tribal blocks - NGO planners and field staff (Virtue Foundation and similar) doing facility-mapping audits at scale - District- and state-level government auditors checking IPHS compliance and PMJAY empanelment - Public-health researchers and journalists investigating medical-desert and access gaps - Software platforms (e.g., ABDM HFR consumers) needing trust signals on third-party facility data

## Solution & Core Features

A six-phase pipeline that turns messy facility text into auditable, citable trust signals:

- Pydantic-typed structured extraction with N=5 self-consistency, evidence-quote grounding via rapidfuzz (≥0.95 substring match) — every capability carries a verbatim source quote and char offset
- Conformal-calibrated trust scorer (IsotonicRegression blended with MAPIE SplitConformalClassifier, LAC method at α=0.10) producing per-capability prediction sets with formal coverage guarantees
- IPHS-grounded validator with 17 machine-checkable rules distilled from IPHS 2022 Volumes I–IV, emitting per-capability iphs_alignment scores plus volume/section citations
- MLflow 3 step-level tracing with OpenTelemetry semantic conventions (CHAIN / LLM / TOOL / AGENT / RETRIEVER spans, token usage on every model call)
- Streamlit citation UI that highlights evidence quotes in source text alongside per-capability cards (badge, alignment score, expandable rule citations)
- FastAPI /trace/{source_record_id} route surfacing the full audit chain (claim + trust + validations + rules index + MLflow trace tree) for production frontends

## Unique Selling Proposition

Per-capability discrimination, not facility-level scoring. Inside a single facility record, three capabilities can be flagged for IPHS rule violations while three sibling capabilities stay clean. On top of that, every flag carries a verbatim source quote next to a citable IPHS clause. Combined with formal statistical guarantees (0.9286 empirical coverage on 90% conformal prediction sets), an open-weight inference path (Qwen 2.5 7B AWQ on a Colab T4 for $0 marginal cost at 10K-record scale), and an asset-based product stance (the system augments ASHA workers, it does not replace clinical judgment), the result is a triage-grade audit tool an NGO or state health team can actually deploy without per-token frontier-API economics.

## Implementation & Technology

- Runtime: Python 3.11, pinned via .python-version
- Extraction: Pydantic 2.13 + Instructor 1.15 + Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 (30-record reference slice) + Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct AWQ via vLLM on Colab Pro T4 (production batch path), rapidfuzz 3.13 for evidence grounding
- Calibration: scikit-learn 1.8 IsotonicRegression wrapped as a binary classifier, MAPIE 1.3 SplitConformalClassifier with LAC conformity score and 20/10 record-level split
- Validator: PyYAML 6.0, deterministic rule engine over data/iphs_rules.yaml (17 rules from IPHS 2022 Vols I–IV)
- Tracing: MLflow 3.11 with explicit mlflow.anthropic.autolog(), OpenTelemetry 1.41 semantic conventions, SQLite tracking store configurable to UC Delta via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI for the production deployment path
- API/UI: FastAPI 0.136, Streamlit 1.56, uvicorn[standard] 0.46

## Results & Impact

On the 30-record human-verified gold set:

- 0.9286 empirical coverage on 90% conformal prediction sets (nominal target 0.90, master-prompt floor 0.85). 
- 0.877 capability recall and 0.714 precision against gold after symmetric alias canonicalization
- 1.000 category accuracy after Phase 4 boundary fixes
- 8 of 101 capabilities triggered ≥1 IPHS rule violation; max single-rule fire rate is 4%. Well below the 50% threshold for over-broad rules
- 57-span MLflow traces per record with token usage on every CHAT_MODEL span. The audit chain is observable end-to-end
- $1.85 total API spend across all six development phases; production 10K batch path is $0 marginal cost via Qwen-AWQ on rented GPU time

Main record (c616c9769f2afbd7, Dr. Pratik Dhabalia Joint Replacement): three claimed surgical capabilities flagged at iphs_alignment=0.00 with R01/R02 violations and IPHS Vol II citations, next to three sibling capabilities at iphs_alignment=1.00 with no violations. That per-capability signal claim, plus evidence, plus citation, is what makes the output usable for an ASHA worker or auditor in the field, rather than a single facility-level number that obscures the substantive disagreements.

- API/UI: FastAPI 0.136, Streamlit 1.56, uvicorn[standard] 0.46
structured.targetAudience: ASHA workers (Accredited Social Health Activists) referring patients in rural and tribal blocks - NGO planners and field staff (Virtue Foundation and similar) doing facility-mapping audits at scale - District- and state-level government auditors checking IPHS compliance and PMJAY empanelment - Public-health researchers and journalists investigating medical-desert and access gaps - Software platforms (e.g., ABDM HFR consumers) needing trust signals on third-party facility data

data.title: AI Scientist Assistant
data.summary: An 8-stage AI pipeline that takes a scientific hypothesis in plain language and generates a complete, operationally realistic experiment plan in under 65 seconds — protocol, materials with catalog numbers, budget, timeline, validation, and design critique.
data.detail:
# AI Scientist Assistant

## Problem & Challenge

Turning a scientific hypothesis into a runnable experiment takes weeks of manual work. A senior scientist can scope a protocol in hours. A junior one may take days, and a wrong chemical concentration or unrealistic timeline can send a lab down the wrong path for weeks. This is not a knowledge problem — it is an operations problem. The execution planning layer between hypothesis and experiment is where science slows down, and no existing tool solves it with the depth and reliability a real lab requires.

## Target Audience

Researchers and principal investigators who need to move quickly from hypothesis to executable plan, particularly junior researchers who lack the domain experience to scope protocols reliably. Secondary users include Contract Research Organisations processing large volumes of incoming briefs, and research funders evaluating feasibility of proposed studies.

## Solution & Core Features

AI Scientist Assistant is an 8-stage pipeline, not a chatbot. It decomposes experiment planning into independent stages: literature quality control via Europe PMC with real DOIs and a component-level novelty score, protocol generation grounded in protocols.io via parallel writer calls with context isolation, materials sourcing with real catalog numbers and explicit gap flags, budget estimation with verified vs estimated confidence on every line item, timeline derivation computed from ISO 8601 step durations, validation criteria back-linked to the producing procedure, a dedicated LLM design critique that audits the full plan before the user sees it, and a researcher-ready summary. A hypothesis quality checker scores the input on four criteria before generation. A learning loop stores scientist corrections and injects them as few-shot examples into future plans of the same type without retraining.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Every stage has defined inputs, outputs, and grounding logic — this is not one monolithic prompt. Uncertainty is made visible through TODO_for_researcher markers rather than confident hallucination. The hypothesis quality checker validates the input before generation, a feature no existing tool provides. The learning loop means every scientist correction compounds into better future plans, making the system more valuable the more it is used. The difference between a tool and a platform.

## Implementation & Technology

Python Flask API with three endpoints orchestrates the 8-stage pipeline. LLM calls use Anthropic Claude Sonnet via the Anthropic SDK and Gemini 2.5 Flash via OpenRouter as a dual-provider fallback. Literature QC queries the Europe PMC REST API with LLM-rewritten search queries. Protocol grounding uses the protocols.io REST API. The frontend is built in React TypeScript via Lovable and deployed on Vercel. Persistent storage and the pgvector semantic retrieval foundation use Supabase Postgres. The system includes 179 unit tests covering hallucinatio

## Results & Impact

Complete operational experiment plans generate in approximately 65 seconds end to end. A complex CRP biosensor hypothesis produces 6 procedures, 62 steps, 86 materials, and 47 TODO_for_researcher markers. A cryoprotection study produces 5 procedures, 42 steps, 57 materials, and 9 documented protocol deviations. All four sample hypotheses from the Fulcrum Science brief were tested across diagnostics, gut health, cell biology, and climate domains. The learning loop demo shows visible plan correction without re-prompting. By compressing weeks of operations planning into 65 seconds, the system gives every researcher access to the output that previously only the most experienced senior scientists could produce.

data.title: JECK City AI
data.summary: JECK is a real-time, AI-powered city wallet that connects users with hyper-relevant local offers based on their immediate context such as weather, location, time, and nearby merchant activity. Instead of static coupons, JECK dynamically generates personalized offers at the right moment, helping users discover nearby opportunities while enabling small merchants to attract customers during key moments. The experience is seamless from discovery to redemption through a mobile wallet and QR-based checkout.
data.detail:
# JECK City AI

## Problem & Challenge

Problem
Local merchants rely on static, generic promotions that are poorly timed and often irrelevant to users’ immediate needs. As a result, even when potential customers are physically nearby, there is no mechanism to connect real-world context (weather, time, location, demand) with meaningful, actionable offers. This creates a gap between user intent and local business opportunities.
Challenge
Design a Generative City Wallet that bridges this gap by transforming real-time contextual signals into personalized, dynamic offers. The challenge is to build a system that understands situational context, generates relevant offers instantly using AI, and delivers them through a seamless user experience — while also providing merchants with an easy, low-effort way to participate and benefit from real-time demand.

## Target Audience

Urban Consumers (End Users) and Local Merchants (Small & Medium Businesses)

## Solution & Core Features

Solution
JECK is a Generative City Wallet that uses real-time AI to connect users with hyper-relevant local offers based on their immediate context (weather, location, time, and local demand signals). Instead of static coupons, the system dynamically generates personalized offers at the exact moment they are most relevant. It bridges the gap between user intent and merchant availability by turning city data into actionable, real-time opportunities.
Core Features
1. Real-Time Context Engine
Aggregates live signals such as weather, geolocation, time of day, and simulated merchant activity to detect high-opportunity moments.
2. AI Generative Offer System
Creates dynamic, personalized offers instantly using AI. Offers include tailored messaging, discount logic, and emotional framing based on context (e.g. “Cold outside? Grab a hot drink nearby”).

3. Merchant Rule-Based Setup
Merchants define simple goals (e.g. increase traffic during off-peak hours, limit discount to 20%), while AI handles execution and personalization automatically.

4. User Wallet & QR Redemption
Users can accept offers and store them in a digital wallet. Each offer generates a secure QR code for seamless in-store redemption.

5. Merchant Dashboard
Provides insights on offer performance, including acceptance rates, traffic impact, and peak/off-peak analytics.

6. Seamless UX Flow
Offers are delivered in a mobile-first interface designed for instant comprehension and 3-second decision-making.

7. Privacy by Design
Personalization is based on on-device processing, with only abstract intent signals shared externally to ensure GDPR compliance.

## Unique Selling Proposition

JECK is a real-time Generative City Wallet that creates hyper-personalized local offers instantly based on live context like weather, location, and demand signals. Unlike traditional coupon apps, it generates dynamic, situation-aware offers on the fly and enables seamless QR-based redemption in one unified experience.

## Implementation & Technology

Implementation & Technology
JECK is built using Lovable Cloud as the backend and database layer, enabling a fully integrated and scalable full-stack environment.
Real-time context signals (weather, location, time, and simulated merchant transaction data) are processed through edge functions in Lovable, which compute contextual scores and trigger offer generation when relevant conditions are met.
The Generative Offer Engine leverages AI to dynamically create personalized offers (content, discount, and framing) instead of relying on static templates. Merchant inputs are simplified to basic business rules, while the system handles the rest automatically.
On the frontend, JECK is implemented as a mobile-first application with a wallet interface, map view, and merchant dashboard, providing a seamless end-to-end experience from offer discovery to QR-based redemption.
The entire system is designed with a GDPR-friendly approach, ensuring that sensitive personalization is abstracted and handled securely within the Lovable ecosystem.
Overall, Lovable Cloud enables JECK to unify backend, database, and deployment into a single environment, making real-time generative city experiences fast to build, scalable, and production-ready.

## Results & Impact

JECK successfully demonstrates an end-to-end Generative City Wallet MVP that transforms real-time urban signals into actionable, personalized offers. The system is able to detect contextual moments (weather, time, location, and merchant activity) and generate relevant offers instantly, proving the feasibility of real-time contextual commerce.
On the user side, the experience significantly improves offer relevance and engagement, as promotions are no longer static but dynamically adapted to the user’s immediate situation. This leads to faster decision-making and a more intuitive, “right place, right time” experience.
On the merchant side, JECK shows how small businesses can benefit from automated demand stimulation during off-peak hours without needing marketing expertise. The simulated analytics dashboard highlights improved traffic potential and offer acceptance tracking.
Technically, the project validates a full pipeline: context ingestion → AI offer generation → QR-based redemption, all running on Lovable Cloud with real-time capabilities.
Overall, JECK proves that combining generative AI with live city data can bridge the gap between physical context and digital personalization, unlocking a new model for smart, responsive urban commerce.

data.title: Maarg — The Healthcare Truth Layer for Critical Care Discovery
data.summary: Maarg is an AI-powered healthcare reasoning system that converts unreliable, unstructured hospital data into verified, evidence-backed intelligence, helping people identify where real critical care exists, not just where it is claimed.
data.detail:
# Maarg — The Healthcare Truth Layer for Critical Care Discovery

## Problem & Challenge

In critical situations, people do not struggle to find hospitals. They struggle to trust them.

Healthcare data is fragmented, outdated, and often inaccurate. Hospitals list capabilities that may not reflect ground reality. In emergencies, families rely on guesswork, incomplete listings, or proximity instead of certainty.

This results in:
• Delays in accessing critical care
• Patients being redirected across facilities
• Preventable loss of life due to poor decision-making

The core challenge is not discovery. It is verification of real capability.

## Target Audience

• Patients and families making urgent care decisions • Emergency responders and coordinators • NGOs and public health organizations • Policy makers identifying infrastructure gaps

## Solution & Core Features

Maarg introduces a verification layer over healthcare data.

Instead of listing hospitals, it audits them.

Core features:
• Unstructured data processing from reports, PDFs, and facility data
• AI reasoning engine to extract and verify medical capabilities
• Confidence scoring for every claim
• Evidence-linked outputs for full transparency
• Detection of healthcare gaps and underserved regions

Users move from guessing to knowing.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Maarg does not find the nearest hospital. It finds the nearest hospital that is telling the truth. The core insight behind Maarg is that the problem with India's healthcare facility data is not incompleteness — it is unverified claims. A facility can list advanced surgery while having no anesthesiologist. It can claim a functional ICU while its notes reference a single oxygen cylinder. Knowing a facility exists is not the same as knowing it can help. Maarg is built around that distinction. The Equipment-to-Capability Inference Graph is the technical expression of this insight. Rather than asking whether a facility claims a capability, it asks whether the documented equipment and staff inventory logically supports that claim — and penalizes the trust score when it does not, citing the exact sentence in the source record that caused the contradiction. Every recommendation Maarg makes is backed by calibrated confidence intervals, not a binary yes or no. "Emergency obstetric care: likely yes, 78% confidence, 95% CI 64–89%" is a fundamentally more honest and useful output for someone making a life-or-death routing decision. This same standard of verification extends to the Facility Portal. When a hospital submits a correction — a new piece of equipment, an updated capability — they cannot simply type it in. They must photograph the physical evidence and, critically, they must do so with their device location turned on. Maarg captures their GPS coordinates at the moment the photo is taken and cross-validates them against the facility's registered address. If the person submitting the proof is not physically within the hospital premises when the photo is captured, the submission is flagged before it ever reaches a human reviewer. A hospital cannot claim a ventilator exists by photographing one from a warehouse across the city. The proof has to come from inside the building. This location-gating mechanism is what makes facility self-reporting trustworthy rather than just convenient. The system gets more accurate the more it is used — because every approved, location-verified correction feeds back into the extraction pipeline and improves the facility's trust score on the next run. And when a user needs more than a recommendation, the Caller AI Agent phones the top verified facilities, confirms bed availability in real time, and dispatches an ambulance on confirmation. Data to reasoning. Reasoning to action. Action to a verified path — because when finding the path saves a life, you can't afford to guess.

## Implementation & Technology

Maarg is built with a scalable and modular architecture.

Tech stack:
• Backend: FastAPI (Python)
• Frontend: Next.js (TypeScript)
• AI Layer: structured extraction and reasoning workflows
• Observability: MLflow for tracking reasoning and outputs
• Data: mock datasets with pipeline designed for large-scale ingestion

System flow:

Ingest unstructured healthcare data
Extract capabilities using AI
Validate claims and assign confidence scores
Link evidence to each output
Serve structured insights through APIs

## Results & Impact

Within the hackathon scope, Maarg successfully:
• Converted messy healthcare data into structured intelligence
• Demonstrated transparent reasoning with traceable outputs
• Identified gaps in healthcare accessibility

Impact:
• Reduces decision time in critical scenarios
• Improves trust in healthcare discovery
• Enables data-driven planning for healthcare systems

data.title: DSV-Gruppe
data.summary:
City Wallet is an AI-powered mobile wallet that generates hyper-personalised local offers in real time, connecting nearby customers with merchants who need them most.

When a user walks near a quiet café or bakery, the app senses the context — live weather, time of day, transaction density from Payone — and uses a large language model to generate a personalised discount offer. Not a template. Copy written by the model for that specific person, at that specific moment.

Every piece of personalisation — location, spending history, demographics, interests — is processed on-device or anonymised before it touches the network. The LLM never sees a name, a coordinate, or an exact amount. GDPR compliance is not an afterthought; it is the architecture.

Built with: FastAPI · Groq (Llama 3.1 8B) · OpenWeatherMap · Payone · plain HTML/JS demo with QR-code redemption flow.
data.detail:
# DSV-Gruppe

## Problem & Challenge

Raw GPS, transactions, and PII are processed on-device and stripped before anything crosses the network. The LLM only sees anonymised summaries — yet still generates offer copy tailored to the exact person, moment, and demographic. Same model, same café, same weather — completely different headlines for a 28-year-old designer and a 67-year-old doctor. Privacy is the enabler of personalisation, not the enemy of it.

## Target Audience

Merchants — local businesses (cafés, bakeries, bookstores) that lose revenue during quiet hours and want to attract nearby customers without broad, expensive advertising.  Wallet users — city shoppers who want relevant, timely discounts near them, without giving away their personal data to get them.

## Solution & Core Features

Solution: City Wallet senses real-time context and uses an LLM to generate a personalised offer for the right person at the right moment — not a template, written fresh each time.

Core Features:

Context sensing — live weather (OpenWeatherMap), Payone transaction density, time of day, and user movement combined into a situation summary
On-device anonymisation — raw GPS and transactions processed locally, only plain-text summaries sent to the backend
GDPR consent gate — explicit per-type consent before anything loads
LLM offer generation — Llama 3.1 reasons across user profile, current situation, and merchant rules to pick the best merchant and write personalised copy
Demographic tailoring — age group, sex, profession, and interests shape the tone and wording of every offer
QR-code redemption — user claims offer, merchant scans QR, loop closed
Merchant dashboard — live stats: transaction volume, offers generated, redemptions

## Unique Selling Proposition

GDPR compliance built into the architecture — not bolted on. Raw data never leaves the device. The LLM only sees anonymised summaries, yet still produces genuinely personalised offer copy tailored to each person's age, profession, interests, and moment. No other solution delivers both without compromise.

## Implementation & Technology

FastAPI backend, Groq (Llama 3.1 8B open-weight LLM), OpenWeatherMap for live weather, Payone transaction stubs for merchant density, on-device JavaScript for all raw signal processing, Next.js merchant dashboard, QR-code redemption flow.

## Results & Impact

A working end-to-end demo: consent → context sensing → LLM offer generation → QR redemption → merchant dashboard. The A/B comparison between Mia (28F) and Werner (67M) in the same café with the same weather proves the demographic tailoring works — the model writes genuinely different copy without any hardcoded rules. Merchants get customers during quiet hours. Users get relevant offers without sacrificing privacy.

data.title: Pehchaan
data.summary: Pehchaan (پہچان) is an open infrastructure platform designed to map informal, self-taught tech talent to formal economic opportunities. By replacing passive, easily faked portfolios with active, AI-evaluated coding challenges, it provides developers with a verifiable, objective ledger of their capabilities. Built for an AI-dominated landscape, the platform focuses on delivering relevant skills through an environment that is highly resistant to cheating, ensuring the experience remains educational and skill-building rather than purely adversarial.
data.detail:
# Pehchaan

## Problem & Challenge

Self-taught and unrecognized developers often lack the institutional credentials required to access formal economic opportunities, leaving their skills unverified and trapping them in the informal economy. Furthermore, in today’s AI-dominated landscape, traditional passive portfolios are easily bypassed with AI-generated code, making it difficult for employers to trust a candidate's true capabilities. There is a need for a validation method that is rigorous and cheat-proof, yet focused on genuine skill acquisition.

## Target Audience

The platform serves unrecognized, informal tech talent, split into two primary segments:      Novice Developers: Beginners who need a safe, zero-penalty environment to learn, receive hints, and build competence progressively.      Experienced Developers: Self-taught professionals looking to formally validate their skills, earn immutable credentials, and prove their capabilities to the broader gig economy.

## Solution & Core Features

Pehchaan acts as a proving ground that provides a verifiable ledger of a developer's skills. Core features include:

    The Sandbox: A training mode for novices featuring progressive difficulty and zero-penalty learning.

    The Arena: A testing ground for experienced developers featuring timed, active code debugging challenges.

    Pehchaan Trust Score (PTS): A dynamic, objective score updated via AI evaluation based on challenge performance.

    Immutable Badges: Verifiable credentials awarded for completing specific technical hurdles.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional coding assessments or static GitHub portfolios, Pehchaan is actively resistant to cheating. It uses live, in-browser code debugging evaluated by an advanced AI (Gemini) to verify actual problem-solving abilities. Most importantly, it bridges the gap between rigorous assessment and learning—providing a platform that is strictly objective in its grading, but fundamentally educational and supportive rather than purely adversarial.

## Implementation & Technology

Pehchaan is built on a highly scalable, modern web stack:

    Frontend: Next.js 16, React 19, and Tailwind CSS v3 for a fast, responsive user interface.

    Code Environment: Monaco Editor (the engine behind VS Code) for an authentic developer experience.

    Backend & Data: Neon (serverless PostgreSQL) for lightweight, instantaneous database management.

    AI & Evaluation: Google Gemini API (gemma-4-31b-it) for intelligent, automated code grading, with Tavily API integrated for optional profile enrichment.

## Results & Impact

Pehchaan directly maps informal talent to formal gig economy roles by providing a trusted metric (the Pehchaan Trust Score) to employers. Its impact strongly aligns with three UN Sustainable Development Goals:

    SDG 4 (Quality Education): Validates the skills of self-taught individuals without requiring traditional institutional gatekeeping.

    SDG 8 (Decent Work): Formalizes uncredentialed talent, opening doors to fair economic opportunities.

    SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities): Dismantles geographic, financial, and credential-based barriers to entry in the global tech industry.

data.title: CIty-Wallet Hack-Nation
data.summary: City Wallet is a hyper-local cashback platform using on-device AI (Phi-3) to deliver personalized offers without compromising user privacy. The project enables merchants to create targeted deals via templates, while customers generate secure QR code coupons for instant redemption. Powered by Supabase, the ecosystem manages profiles, products, and real-time transaction validation. Its core mission is to boost local commerce by ensuring sensitive movement data never leaves the user's smartphone.
data.detail:
# CIty-Wallet Hack-Nation

## Problem & Challenge

Privacy Invasion
Problem: Most apps track and sell your precise GPS location to give you rewards.
Solution: We use on-device AI. Your data stays on your phone; the cloud never knows exactly where you are.
Marketing Gap for Small Shops
Problem: Local merchants can't compete with big brands' high-tech marketing.
Solution: An easy Merchant Terminal that lets small shops create "Smart Rules" and targeted offers in seconds.
Coupon Fraud
Problem: Digital coupons are often shared, leaked, or used twice.
Solution: Single-use QR codes that expire after 15 minutes and are validated in real-time, making fraud impossible.

## Target Audience

Citizens

## Solution & Core Features

On-Device AI (Privacy): We run the Phi-3 model directly on your phone. It analyzes your location locally and only sends anonymous "interest tags" to the cloud, so your raw data never leaves your device.
Merchant Dashboard (Simplicity): We provide an intuitive terminal where local shops use templates and smart rules to launch professional marketing campaigns in seconds without tech skills.
Secure QR Handshake (Security): Every coupon is a unique, encrypted token. Merchants validate these in real-time via Supabase, ensuring they are single-use and expire after 15 minutes to prevent fraud.
Real-Time Backend (Sync): Using a live database ensures that as soon as a merchant updates a product or a customer redeems a token, everyone’s app stays perfectly in sync.

## Unique Selling Proposition

City Wallet stands out by choosing Privacy by Design over the "Data-Hungry" model of traditional apps. Here is why it’s better:   Zero-Tracking Personalization: Unlike competitors who sell your GPS history, we use Local AI (Phi-3). You get relevant deals while your actual movements stay 100% private on your device. Fraud-Proof Security: Most local shops lose money on shared screenshots or reused codes. Our dynamic, single-use QR tokens validated by Supabase make coupon fraud impossible. Pro-Tools for Local Shops: We give small merchants the same "Smart Targeting" power as Amazon (rules like weather or stock-based offers) through a dead-simple mobile terminal. Frictionless Experience: No paper, no plastic cards, and no complex sign-ups. It’s a clean, real-time "Digital Handshake" between the customer and the shop.

## Implementation & Technology

The Stack
Frontend: React Native (cross-platform mobile app).
Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL) for real-time data and security.
AI: Phi-3 running locally on the phone (Local Inference).
Local AI Execution
Instead of a server, the AI model runs on the device. It filters your location and habits to find matches locally. It only sends anonymous "interest tags" to the cloud, keeping your raw data invisible to us.
The Secure Handshake
The validation process uses an Atomic Transaction:
 
Generate: App creates a unique UUID token with a 15-minute expiration.
Scan: Merchant app reads the QR and queries Supabase.
Validate: The backend checks in one single step if the token is real, unused, and not expired.
Lock: Once verified, it’s instantly marked as redeemed to prevent any double-use.
Data Security (RLS)
We use Row Level Security (RLS). This ensures that a merchant can only see their own products and scans, while a user can only see their own wallet—no one can "peek" at other people's data.

## Results & Impact

The Generative City Wallet project revitalizes local commerce by transforming transactional and contextual data into hyper-relevant, real-time AI offers for the user.

Key Results

Functional End-to-End Prototype: Successfully validated the entire user journey, from secure role-based login to real-time transaction processing.

Real-Time Contextual Engine: Seamlessly integrated live weather, GPS location, business

data.title: Unmapped Voices: Making language skills visible and valuable.
data.summary: Unmapped Voices is a platform that identifies and verifies informal language skills through short assessment tasks, creating a trusted, portable skills profile. These validated skills can then be used to connect individuals to AI training opportunities and broader economic pathways, turning unseen abilities into real value.
data.detail:
# Unmapped Voices: Making language skills visible and valuable. 

## Problem & Challenge

Millions of people, particularly in low and middle-income regions, possess valuable language and cultural skills, but these skills are invisible to formal systems. They lack recognised credentials, are excluded from labour market opportunities, and have no structured way to participate in the growing AI economy.
Over 90% of online content is concentrated in just 10 languages, leaving thousands of languages underrepresented in the data used to train AI systems. As a result, AI performance is significantly weaker in low-resource languages. At the same time, modern AI relies heavily on human-labelled data and feedback for training and alignment, especially in language-based tasks. Despite this demand, millions of multilingual individuals in low- and middle-income countries have no formal way to verify or contribute their language skills to the AI economy.

## Target Audience

Young people in low- and middle-income countries with informal or unrecognised language skills

## Solution & Core Features

AI onboarding screener to quickly assess baseline ability and intent
Proof-of-skill assessments (language, reasoning, and AI judgment tasks) to verify real competencies
Skills Passport profile that aggregates verified performance into a portable talent record
Remote job matching system that connects users to relevant global opportunities based on skill fit
Performance-based ranking (global leaderboard) to reflect comparative skill strength and credibility
Personalised upskilling pathway that recommends tests and learning steps to improve employability over time

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unmapped Voices uniquely combines skill verification, AI data generation, and economic opportunity into a single system — turning informal language ability into a measurable, trusted, and income-generating asset. Unlike the traditional hiring platforms that rely on resumes, degrees, and prior experience. The unmapped voices uses AI-powered proof of skill assesment to connect overlooked talent with global remote work opportunities so the new trust system for hiring hiddent talent.

## Implementation & Technology

Unmapped voices can be implemented through partnerships with ministries of labor, public universities, vocational training centers, NGOs, and remote-first employers to help job seekers qualify for digital work opportunities.
Users complete AI-powered assessments in areas such as English communication, problem-solving, data annotation accuracy, AI response evaluation, and multilingual review. Based on their results, they receive a verified Skills Passport that shows job readiness, strengths, and recommended roles.
Employers can then use this verified talent pool to hire candidates for remote positions such as AI data labeling, content moderation, customer support, localization, quality assurance, and research assistance.
For developing countries like Kenya, Indonesia, and etc this creates a practical pathway to reduce youth unemployment, increase foreign income through remote work, strengthen digital workforce competitiveness, and shift talent from informal or low-wage jobs into higher-value online employment.
Unmapped voices is implemented as a web platform where users complete AI-powered skill assessments, receive verified Skills Passports, and get matched to suitable remote work opportunities based on demonstrated ability rather than traditional credentials.
The platform is built using Next.js for the frontend and Node.js backend services with PostgreSQL for secure data storage. OpenAI API powers adaptive interviews, scoring, and job matching. AI development tools such as Claude Code and Codex were used to accelerate prototyping and feature development. The platform is deployed on Vercel.

## Results & Impact

Unmapped Voices demonstrates how informal language skills can be transformed into structured, verifiable skill profiles using short assessment tasks. This creates a framework for recognising previously uncredentialed abilities and turning them into trusted signals.
The impact of the system can be understood across three key stakeholder groups:
Young people: The platform provides a pathway for individuals with informal or unrecognised language skills to have their abilities validated and made visible. This increases access to future opportunities by turning invisible skills into portable, structured profiles.
AI companies: The system enables improved access to locally grounded language and cultural data by sourcing verified human input from underrepresented communities. This has the potential to improve the quality, relevance, and inclusivity of AI models, particularly for low-resource languages.
Governments and development organisations: The platform offers a scalable infrastructure for identifying and mapping informal skills within populations. This can support more accurate workforce understanding, better-targeted training programmes, and improved alignment between skills supply and labour market needs.
Overall, the solution provides a bridge between informal human capability and formal economic and technological systems, helping reduce the gap between real-world skills and recognised opportunity.

data.title: AI Scientist: From Hypothesis to Executable Experiment
data.summary: AI Scientist Demo is an end-to-end research intelligence platform that transforms plain-language hypotheses into structured, executable experiment plans. It combines a React frontend, FastAPI backend, and modular AI agents to generate validated protocols, materials, budgets, and timelines while enforcing safety and scientific rigor. With integrated literature grounding, a trust layer for traceability, and simulation-driven success estimation, the platform enables faster, more reliable, and reproducible scientific planning.
data.detail:
# AI Scientist: From Hypothesis to Executable Experiment

## Problem & Challenge

Scientific research is often slowed not by lack of ideas, but by the operational complexity of turning hypotheses into executable experiments. Designing protocols, validating assumptions, sourcing materials, estimating costs, and ensuring safety compliance require significant manual effort, time, and expertise. This leads to delays, inconsistencies, and reduced reproducibility in early-stage research.

## Target Audience

Research scientists and lab professionals R&D teams in biotech, pharma, and academia Early-stage researchers and students Innovation labs and research institutions Organizations seeking faster experiment planning and validation

## Solution & Core Features

AI Scientist Demo provides an end-to-end platform that converts plain-language hypotheses into structured, actionable experiment plans.

Core Features:

Hypothesis parsing and validation (intervention, endpoints, controls)
Safety gate for risk detection and compliance enforcement
Multi-source literature retrieval and novelty analysis
Modular AI agents for:
Protocol generation
Materials and reagent planning
Budget estimation
Timeline sequencing
Trust layer with provenance tracking and uncertainty flags
Monte Carlo simulation for success probability and sample size estimation
Doc Studio for editing, structuring, and exporting research documents
Feedback loop for continuous improvement

## Unique Selling Proposition

Transforms abstract scientific ideas into operational, lab-ready plans Combines agentic AI + evidence grounding + simulation in one pipeline Ensures traceability, transparency, and scientific rigor Integrates safety validation before execution, not after Continuously improves through expert feedback learning loop

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend: React (interactive UI for researchers)
Backend: FastAPI (high-performance orchestration layer)
AI System: Modular agent-based architecture
Data Layer: Multi-source literature retrieval and processing
Simulation: Monte Carlo methods for probabilistic decision support
Architecture Design: Scalable, reproducible, and API-driven

## Results & Impact

Reduces experiment planning time from weeks to hours
Improves clarity and reduces ambiguity in research design
Enhances safety compliance and risk awareness
Increases reproducibility and scientific reliability
Enables faster, data-driven decision-making in R&D environments

data.title: Agora
data.summary: Agora is a multi-component system that turns "agents pay agents" from a slogan into a working economy
data.detail:
# Agora

## Problem & Challenge

AI agents are about to start transacting on their own behalf — verifying facts, buying datasets, hiring specialists, and ordering goods.

But the payment infrastructure they rely on was built for humans clicking buttons.

Card networks
~30¢ minimum per transaction (100× too high for micropayments)
Fraud systems require CAPTCHAs and 3DS challenges
Account creation required
Settlement takes days
Stablecoins
Same gatekeeper model on different rails
Centralized control (fees, freezes)
Transactions tied to public wallets

Result:
Agents that can reason about thousands of services per day are economically constrained to credit-card-era speeds.

## Target Audience

Three distinct groups, each with one sentence of payoff:  Specialist providers with curated knowledge or compute — research firms with cleaned scrapes of regulatory filings, legal tech with indexed case law, infra teams with pre-computed corpus embeddings, designers with verified prompt templates, ops people with battle-tested integration scaffolds. Their economics are "one expensive build, infinite cheap reads" — Agora gives them a per-call sales channel that doesn't exist anywhere else. AI-native product teams building agentic assistants (travel, research, code, compliance, shopping) whose users will increasingly hand the agent a budget and a goal rather than reviewing each call. Agora is the discovery + billing infrastructure their agent talks to. Enterprises running internal AI agents that need to spend on external services — typically blocked today by procurement, vendor contracts, and per-API approvals. A private Agora instance gives them an audited, budget-capped, kill-switchable spending surface.  Secondary: a wave of solo agentic-economy builders (the Origram / Clank / unhuman.coffee cohort) who currently each operate single-vendor demos and have no neutral marketplace to list on.

## Solution & Core Features

Agora is a multi-component system that turns "agents pay agents" from a slogan into a working economy:

Public registry — sellers register with an Ed25519 pubkey (no email, no password, no account). Each lists services with type, price, latency, and a free preview. FTS5 search; type/price filters; signed-write protection.
L402 paywall on every paid endpoint — the agent posts the request, gets HTTP 402 Payment Required with a Lightning invoice, pays it, replays with the payment-proof header, and gets the work back. The payment is the credential — no API key, no account, no session.
Orchestrator — POST /api/v1/orchestrator/recommend ranks candidate services with an explainable score: 60% intent match (semantic over name/description/tags), 20% honor-normalized reputation, 20% price-fit. Returns scored results with the breakdown so the agent can show its reasoning.
Reputation + peer review — buyers rate sellers (gated by a real, recent transaction); sellers can request paid peer reviews against a structured rubric, with blind random reviewer assignment and slashing for bad-faith review work. 90-day honor decay rewards consistent activity.
Subscriptions — prepaid balance per subscriber, per-event debit, automatic top-up and refund-on-cancel, signed alert delivery.
PayMyAgent (MCP server) — a stdio server that gives Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code (anything speaking the Model Context Protocol) access to Agora's tools. Includes a per-call cap, a per-session sat budget, a kill-switch, and a persisted spend counter so a human can hand a budget to the agent without anxiety.
Public web index — a read-only, search-engine-crawlable browser surface (/sellers, /services, /search, /recommend, /activity) that lets humans inspect the marketplace and lets directories like 402index.io discover Agora's catalog.
Live transaction feed — every settled payment surfaces on /activity with seller, service, amount, fee, and payment-hash prefix. Updates per refresh; suitable for an SSE upgrade.
Working sample sellers — vision-oracle-3 (OSM-geocoded listing verification, 240 sat / call), dataset-seller (NOAA Pacific Northwest weather archive 2015–25, 5,000 sat / purchase, 2% platform fee), and market-monitor (GitHub Security Advisory subscriptions, 50 sat / event).

## Unique Selling Proposition

Every other agent-payment project today is one of two things: a single shop (Origram, Clank, unhuman.coffee, unhuman.design — each one vendor) or a read-only directory (402index.io, agentic.market, x402 Bazaar — browse but don't transact).  Agora is the bid layer in between — the first multi-seller marketplace on Bitcoin Lightning where a buyer-agent picks among offers, pays, gets the result, and the receipt is verifiable end-to-end.  Concrete differentiators against the field:  Sub-cent settlement, ~1-second round-trips, zero routing fees in practice. A 240-sat call costs about $0.0016 and clears in under a second. Below the floor of every legacy rail. Identity-free by design. Pubkey is the account — no email, no password, no KYC layer. Stablecoin marketplaces all default to KYC-gated wallets with freeze authority. Explainable orchestration. Every recommendation comes with a 60/20/20 score breakdown, not a black-box ranking. This matters when a regulator or an enterprise audit team asks "why this seller, not that one?" Built-in human-in-the-loop primitive. The marketplace can list a human-on-demand service alongside agent services with the same pricing and reputation surface. Most directories can't structurally model this. MCP-native buyer SDK. Agora ships with a Model Context Protocol server out of the box, meaning any modern AI assistant can use Agora's tools without integration work on the buyer side. Open infrastructure. Lightning belongs to no one. No platform can freeze a seller, throttle their throughput, or rewrite the fee schedule unilaterally.

## Implementation & Technology

A six-workspace TypeScript / Node.js monorepo (packages/agora-core, provider/, mcp/, registry/, dashboard/, web/, plus two sample-seller agents).

Registry: Next.js 16 + SQLite (via better-sqlite3) with FTS5 full-text search, numbered migrations, Ed25519 signed-request middleware, deterministic-hash pseudo-embeddings (semantic search), 90-day honor decay job, lazy revalidation, admin endpoints behind HTTP-Basic.
Provider: Next.js 16 service implementing L402 with HMAC-signed macaroons (HMAC-SHA-256 over a base64url claims payload), preimage verification (SHA-256(preimage) == payment_hash), single-use enforcement via atomic SQLite transition, structured logging (pino), per-IP token-bucket rate limiting, standardized error envelopes ({ error, message, request_id, docs }), and a dual-mode wallet adapter that switches between mock (no setup) and real Lightning over Nostr Wallet Connect via @getalby/sdk.
Sample seller agents: plain Node HTTP servers for the agentic data products (NOAA dataset, GHSA monitor) — same L402 + macaroon contract as the Next.js provider, no framework lock-in.
MCP server: 24 canonical tools (agora_) plus 7 deprecated lumen_ aliases for backwards compatibility, stdio transport via @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, a localhost-only HTTP control plane on a random port for the desktop dashboard, a three-layer spending guardrail (per-call cap, per-session budget, persisted kill-switch), Ed25519 buyer identity auto-generated and persisted on first run.
Public web index: Next.js 16 with React Server Components — SSR-rendered catalog pages, SQLite-backed search delegated to the registry, no client-side state, sitemap + robots, accessibility-first markup.
Cryptography: @noble/ed25519 for keypairs and signing, @noble/hashes for HMAC and SHA-2/3, all native Web Crypto where possible, no native bindings beyond better-sqlite3.
Lightning rail: Nostr Wallet Connect (NIP-47) for both buyer and seller wallets — works with any NWC-speaking wallet (Alby Hub, Lexe, Mutiny, etc.), no node operation required.
Test infrastructure: nine end-to-end phase-gate test scripts spawning the full stack and validating real behavior (signed-write rejection, replay-protection, budget guardrails, blind reviewer assignment, escrow split arithmetic, dataset signed-URL gating, public-index page rendering with live data). 115 of 115 documented checks pass.
Deployment readiness: stateless services where possible, single-file SQLite where not, runs anywhere Node 22 runs. A Cloudflare-Tunnel ephemeral URL exposes the whole stack publicly in 30 seconds.

## Results & Impact

A working, live demo, not slideware:

Six successful mainnet Lightning payments end-to-end from MCP through provider, settled in about 1.05 seconds at the 50th percentile, zero routing fees in practice, total spend $0.88 USD across the run. Each settlement appears as a discrete transaction in Alby Hub's transaction log under the matching app.
115 / 115 documented test-gate checks green across nine phases (workspace setup, multi-seller registry, subscriptions, control plane, orchestrator math, peer review + slashing, dataset commerce + 2% platform fee, public web index, legacy single-provider regression).
Real OSM-geocoded ground truth returned for every paid listing-verify call — Tour Eiffel · 48.8583, 2.2945, Hotel Adlon · 52.5163, 13.3801, Brandenburger Tor · 52.5163, 13.3777, Sydney Opera House · -33.8568, 151.2153 — with HMAC-signed proofs, real osm_id references, and confidence scores up to 0.989.
Explainable orchestration in production: a freshly-rated seller's score moved from 0.317 (im=0.27, hn=0.00, pf=0.76) to 0.519 (im=0.27, hn=1.00, pf=0.76) on a single rating, demonstrating that the 60/20/20 ranking is honest and reactive.
Working subscription delivery: 50-sat-per-event GitHub advisory subscriptions, balance-debited correctly, top-up working, balance-exhausted alerts firing correctly, refund on cancel.
Cross-protocol-friendly discovery: the /api/v1/discovery and /api/v1/transactions/recent endpoints are crawlable by directories like 402index.io and agentic.market in their native formats.

structured.usp: Every other agent-payment project today is one of two things: a single shop (Origram, Clank, unhuman.coffee, unhuman.design — each one vendor) or a read-only directory (402index.io, agentic.market, x402 Bazaar — browse but don't transact).  Agora is the bid layer in between — the first multi-seller marketplace on Bitcoin Lightning where a buyer-agent picks among offers, pays, gets the result, and the receipt is verifiable end-to-end.  Concrete differentiators against the field:  Sub-cent settlement, ~1-second round-trips, zero routing fees in practice. A 240-sat call costs about $0.0016 and clears in under a second. Below the floor of every legacy rail. Identity-free by design. Pubkey is the account — no email, no password, no KYC layer. Stablecoin marketplaces all default to KYC-gated wallets with freeze authority. Explainable orchestration. Every recommendation comes with a 60/20/20 score breakdown, not a black-box ranking. Agora ships with a Model Context Protocol server out of the box, meaning any modern AI assistant can use Agora's tools without integration work on the buyer side. Open infrastructure. Lightning belongs to no one. No platform can freeze a seller, throttle their throughput, or rewrite the fee schedule unilaterally.

structured.problem:
AI agents are about to start transacting on their own behalf — verifying facts, buying datasets, hiring specialists, and ordering goods.

But the payment infrastructure they rely on was built for humans clicking buttons.

Card networks
~30¢ minimum per transaction (100× too high for micropayments)
Fraud systems require CAPTCHAs and 3DS challenges
Account creation required
Settlement takes days
Stablecoins
Same gatekeeper model on different rails
Centralized control (fees, freezes)
Transactions tied to public wallets

Result:
Agents that can reason about thousands of services per day are economically constrained to credit-card-era speeds.
structured.solution:
Agora is a multi-component system that turns "agents pay agents" from a slogan into a working economy:

Public registry — sellers register with an Ed25519 pubkey (no email, no password, no account). Each lists services with type, price, latency, and a free preview. FTS5 search; type/price filters; signed-write protection.
L402 paywall on every paid endpoint — the agent posts the request, gets HTTP 402 Payment Required with a Lightning invoice, pays it, replays with the payment-proof header, and gets the work back. The payment is the credential — no API key, no account, no session.
Orchestrator — POST /api/v1/orchestrator/recommend ranks candidate services with an explainable score: 60% intent match (semantic over name/description/tags), 20% honor-normalized reputation, 20% price-fit. Returns scored results with the breakdown so the agent can show its reasoning.
Reputation + peer review — buyers rate sellers (gated by a real, recent transaction); sellers can request paid peer reviews against a structured rubric, with blind random reviewer assignment and slashing for bad-faith review work. 90-day honor decay rewards consistent activity.
Subscriptions — prepaid balance per subscriber, per-event debit, automatic top-up and refund-on-cancel, signed alert delivery.
PayMyAgent (MCP server) — a stdio server that gives Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code (anything speaking the Model Context Protocol) access to Agora's tools. Includes a per-call cap, a per-session sat budget, a kill-switch, and a persisted spend counter so a human can hand a budget to the agent without anxiety.
Public web index — a read-only, search-engine-crawlable browser surface (/sellers, /services, /search, /recommend, /activity) that lets humans inspect the marketplace and lets directories like 402index.io discover Agora's catalog.
Live transaction feed — every settled payment surfaces on /activity with seller, service, amount, fee, and payment-hash prefix. Updates per refresh; suitable for an SSE upgrade.
Working sample sellers — vision-oracle-3 (OSM-geocoded listing verification, 240 sat / call), dataset-seller (NOAA Pacific Northwest weather archive 2015–25, 5,000 sat / purchase, 2% platform fee), and market-monitor (GitHub Security Advisory subscriptions, 50 sat / event).

jury_scope.notes: Temporary Event 5 live leaderboard display test

structured.targetAudience: Three distinct groups, each with one sentence of payoff:  Specialist providers with curated knowledge or compute — research firms with cleaned scrapes of regulatory filings, legal tech with indexed case law, infra teams with pre-computed corpus embeddings, designers with verified prompt templates, ops people with battle-tested integration scaffolds. Their economics are "one expensive build, infinite cheap reads" — Agora gives them a per-call sales channel that doesn't exist anywhere else. AI-native product teams building agentic assistants (travel, research, code, compliance, shopping) whose users will increasingly hand the agent a budget and a goal rather than reviewing each call. Agora is the discovery + billing infrastructure their agent talks to. Enterprises running internal AI agents that need to spend on external services — typically blocked today by procurement, vendor contracts, and per-API approvals. A private Agora instance gives them an audited, budget-capped, kill-switchable spending surface.  Secondary: a wave of solo agentic-economy builders (the Origram / Clank / unhuman.coffee cohort) who currently each operate single-vendor demos and have no neutral marketplace to list on.

data.title: City Wallet Project
data.summary:
City Wallet is an AI-powered city wallet that connects local merchants with nearby consumers through hyper-personalized, real-time offers. 

When a user walks past a quiet café on a cold Tuesday lunch break, City Wallet detects the moment — combining live weather, time context, user behavior, and merchant demand — and uses GPT-4o to generate a unique offer on the spot: Cold outside? ☕ 15% off, 80m away. The offer didn't exist five minutes ago. It won't exist in twenty minutes. It exists now, because now is when it matters. 

For consumers: Relevant offers that feel personal, not spam. A cashback wallet. Full GDPR privacy — GPS never leaves the device. 

For merchants: Zero marketing effort. Set simple rules once. The AI handles copy, timing, and targeting automatically. Built with Python FastAPI backend, OpenAI GPT-4o, Google Places, OpenWeatherMap, and two Next.js 14 frontends — one for consumers, one for the merchant dashboard.

Three modules. Two interfaces. One living wallet.
data.detail:
# City Wallet Project

## Problem & Challenge

The Problem

Every day, two things happen simultaneously in every city — and no system connects them.

On one side: local merchants have quiet hours. Tables are empty. Pastries go unsold. A café at 12:15 has had only 3 transactions when it normally sees 12. The owner has no way to reach the people walking past outside.

On the other side: people are walking through the city — slightly hungry, 12 minutes to spare, phone in hand — with no idea that a great café offering a discount is 80 meters away.

The gap is a timing and relevance problem. Not a discount problem. Not a technology problem. A matching problem: the right offer, for the right person, at the right second.

## Target Audience

Consumers/Customers, Merchants, DSV Group

## Solution & Core Features

Solution & Core Functions

The Solution

City Wallet is an AI-powered city wallet that detects the perfect local offer for a user in real time, generates it dynamically, and makes it redeemable through a seamless checkout. Offers don't exist until the moment they are needed. They are created specifically for this user, this location, this minute.

Three Core Modules

Module 1 — Context Sensing Layer

The system aggregates 5 real-time signals: live weather from OpenWeatherMap, time slot from the system clock, user intent classified on-device in JavaScript, nearby merchant data from Google Places, and transaction density from a simulated Payone feed. These signals are combined into a composite trigger score. When it exceeds 0.7, the system proceeds to offer generation.

Module 2 — Generative Offer Engine

Context and merchant rules are sent to GPT-4o in a structured prompt. The model returns a complete offer from scratch — not from a template. Every field is decided by the AI: headline, subtext, discount level, CTA text, color scheme, icon, and urgency timer. This is true Generative UI. The frontend renders whatever the model returns.

Module 3 — Checkout and Redemption

When a user accepts an offer, a unique QR token is generated. The user walks to the merchant and shows the QR code. The merchant scans it, the backend validates the token, applies the discount, and credits cashback to the user's wallet. Both dashboards update in real time.

Two Interfaces

The consumer app shows a live context bar with weather and intent signals, an AI-generated offer card with dynamic colors and copy, a QR checkout screen with countdown timer, and a wallet with cashback balance and transaction history.

The merchant dashboard shows a real-time offer funnel from pushed to redeemed, revenue breakdown and ROI metrics, a simple AI rule configuration panel where merchants set max discount, target goal, and brand tone, a live event feed, and a QR scanner for redemption.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most platforms push pre-written offers at people. City Wallet reads the moment and generates the offer that fits it — once, for now, and then it's gone. Merchant sets rules. AI creates the offer. Consumer redeems it in 30 seconds.

## Implementation & Technology

Implementation and Technology

Architecture

City Wallet is built as a full-stack web application with three layers: a Python backend, two Next.js frontends, and an AI generation pipeline connecting them.

Backend

The backend runs on Python FastAPI with SQLite as the database, accessed via aiosqlite for async I/O. It exposes 16 REST endpoints across four modules: context sensing, offer generation, redemption and wallet, and merchant management. The database has 7 tables covering merchants, rules, offers, redemptions, wallet balances, transactions, and simulated payment data. City configuration is JSON-driven, meaning switching from Munich to Stuttgart requires changing a config file, not the code.

AI Pipeline

The context engine aggregates five real-time signals and passes them to GPT-4o along with the merchant's rule constraints. The model returns a complete offer object in JSON — headline, subtext, discount percentage, visual theme, colors, icon, CTA text, and validity window. Nothing is templated. The frontend receives this JSON and renders the card dynamically. This is what we call Generative UI: the AI decides not just the copy, but the entire visual presentation.

Frontend

The consumer app is a Next.js 14 TypeScript application running on port 3000. It shows the context bar, renders the AI offer card, handles QR checkout with a countdown timer, and displays the cashback wallet.

The merchant dashboard is a separate Next.js 14 application on port 3001. It connects to the same backend and shows real-time funnel analytics, revenue metrics, a rule configuration panel, a live event feed, and a QR scanner for redemption.

Privacy Architecture

All GPS behavior analysis runs in JavaScript on the user's device. The app tracks movement speed, stop frequency, and direction variance to classify intent locally. Only the result — an abstract signal like browsing food — is ever sent to the backend. Raw coordinates never leave the phone. This makes City Wallet GDPR-compliant by architecture, not just by policy.

External APIs

OpenAI GPT-4o handles offer generation at roughly one cent per call. Google Places provides real merchant data including names, addresses, ratings, photos, and coordinates for 50 merchants in Munich. OpenWeatherMap provides live weather data including temperature, conditions, and wind on the free tier.

What is Real vs Simulated

Weather data is real, pulled live from OpenWeatherMap. Merchant data is real, seeded from Google Places. AI offer generation is real, using GPT-4o. Payone transaction data is simulated using a realistic hourly pattern model. User GPS in the demo is fixed to a location in Munich city center.

End to end, from context fetch to offer display, the system runs in under 500 milliseconds.

## Results & Impact

Results and Impact

What We Built

City Wallet is a fully functional end-to-end prototype, not a mockup. The backend serves real data through 16 live API endpoints. The AI generates unique offers using GPT-4o on every call. The merchant dashboard displays real analytics pulled from an actual SQLite database. The consumer app renders fully dynamic offer cards where every color, headline, and CTA is determined by the model at runtime.

Demonstrated Metrics

In the demo scenario, a single offer cycle produces the following results. An offer is generated and pushed to a nearby user. The user accepts it within the validity window. The QR code is scanned and redeemed at the merchant. The merchant dashboard shows a net incremental revenue of approximately 19 euros from a discount cost of 3.38 euros — an ROI of 566 percent. The cost per acquired customer is under one euro.

These numbers reflect the core value proposition: the merchant spends a small discount to generate a transaction that would not have happened otherwise. The customer who redeemed the offer was 80 meters away and had no prior intention of entering the café.

Broader Impact

For merchants, City Wallet converts dead hours into revenue without requiring any marketing expertise, campaign management, or creative work. A small café owner sets three rules once and the system handles everything else indefinitely.

For consumers, the experience is frictionless and feels genuinely personal. The offer matches the exact moment — the weather, the time, the pace of walking — in a way that generic coupons never can. Privacy is preserved by design.

For DSV-Gruppe and the Sparkassen ecosystem, City Wallet layers AI-driven personalization directly on top of existing Payone terminal infrastructure and customer banking relationships, creating a new engagement layer with no new payment rails required.

Technical Outcome

The full stack is operational. The backend handles async requests with sub-500ms response times. TypeScript type checking passes with zero errors across both frontend applications. The system is city-configurable through JSON — deploying to Stuttgart or any other city requires no code changes.

City Wallet proves that hyper-personalized, AI-generated offers at the local merchant level are technically feasible, economically viable, and ready to scale.
techStack: Backend Python 3.11, FastAPI, SQLite with aiosqlite, Uvicorn, Pydantic v2
AI and External APIs OpenAI GPT-4o for offer generation, Google Maps and Places API for real merchant data and geocoding, OpenWeatherMap API for live weather data
Frontend Next.js 14 with React 18 and TypeScript for both the consumer app and merchant dashboard, plain CSS for styling with no framework dependency
