data.title: CareGrid India
data.summary: CareGrid India is an AI-ready healthcare trust intelligence platform that helps users discover, assess, and verify healthcare facilities across India using trust scores, recommendation readiness, evidence-based facility data, GIS mapping, Databricks Vector Search, and Tavily web verification.
data.detail:
# CareGrid India

## Problem & Challenge

India has a large and fragmented healthcare ecosystem where facility information is often incomplete, inconsistent, outdated, or difficult to verify. Patients, NGOs, health planners, and emergency responders may struggle to identify which hospitals or clinics are reliable, which facilities provide specific services, and which records require human verification before recommendation. This creates a major trust gap, especially for high-acuity needs such as ICU care, emergency services, dialysis, oncology, and maternity care.

## Target Audience

CareGrid is designed for multiple users who need reliable healthcare facility discovery and verification:  Patients and families looking for trusted healthcare facilities based on location, service type, and readiness.  NGOs and public health organizations identifying underserved regions, trust deserts, and facilities needing verification.  Healthcare administrators and planners analyzing state-level healthcare trust gaps and readiness distribution.  Emergency coordinators who need fast, evidence-supported facility recommendations with human verification prompts.  Data and policy teams working on healthcare infrastructure mapping, verification, and quality improvement.

## Solution & Core Features

CareGrid provides a full-stack healthcare intelligence dashboard with the following core features:

AI-ready agent interface where users can ask natural-language queries such as “Find trusted ICU hospitals in Bihar” or “Find dialysis centers in Uttar Pradesh.”

Trust scoring and recommendation readiness for 10,000 healthcare facility records.

Databricks Vector Search integration for semantic facility retrieval based on medical evidence and capability descriptions.

Tavily web verification to externally verify facility identity, location, and online presence.

GIS map interface using India boundary data and Mapbox, allowing facility recommendations to be visualized geographically.

Search and filtering by state, facility type, trust category, readiness, and score.

Impact analysis dashboards showing trust gaps, priority states, risk index, facility type gaps, and verification burden.

Evidence-based recommendation cards including matched capabilities, evidence snippets, validation findings, warning flags, score breakdown, and safety notes.

## Unique Selling Proposition

CareGrid is not just a healthcare directory. It is a trust intelligence system.  Its USP is the combination of:  Structured healthcare facility scoring,  Semantic AI-powered retrieval,  Human-verifiable recommendation logic,  External web verification,  GIS-based healthcare access visualization,  and transparent evidence-based reasoning.  Unlike simple search engines or listing platforms, CareGrid explains why a facility is recommended, whether it is ready for recommendation, what evidence supports the recommendation, and what should still be verified by a human before action is taken.  This makes CareGrid especially valuable for high-risk healthcare discovery where wrong or outdated information can have serious consequences.

## Implementation & Technology

CareGrid is implemented as a full-stack web application.

The backend is built with FastAPI and serves healthcare facility data, statistics, impact analysis, search, and agent recommendation APIs.

The frontend is built with React, TypeScript, Vite, and a modern responsive UI. It includes dashboards, facility search, impact analysis, GIS mapping, and an AI-ready agent interface.

Databricks is used for vector database and semantic search through a Vector Search index built from a 10,000-row healthcare facility dataset.

Tavily is integrated for web verification of top recommended facilities.

Mapbox is used for the dark GIS basemap, with India shapefile data converted into GeoJSON for boundary visualization.

The agent is designed to be backend-controlled, meaning the frontend never directly calls Databricks, Tavily, or external AI services. This keeps the architecture secure, scalable, and ready for future AI model integration.

## Results & Impact

CareGrid successfully demonstrates an end-to-end healthcare trust intelligence workflow.

The platform processes 10,000 healthcare facility records across India and provides trust-based recommendations, facility search, readiness classification, impact dashboards, and GIS visualization.

The analysis identified major healthcare trust gaps, including a large proportion of facilities that should not be recommended without human review. It also highlights priority states and facility types where verification and evidence enrichment are most urgently needed.

The system improves healthcare discovery by making recommendations more transparent, evidence-backed, and verification-aware. It can support better decision-making for patients, NGOs, emergency responders, and health planners while reducing reliance on unverified healthcare listings.

CareGrid’s long-term impact is to help build a safer, more trustworthy healthcare access layer for India by combining AI, verified data, geospatial intelligence, and human-in-the-loop safety.
Answer 'cause you please answer.

data.title: AccelP3-AI-scientist-clinical-trial-planner
data.summary: An AI-powered multi-agent pipeline that transforms natural-language clinical hypotheses into structured, evidence-grounded Phase 3 trial plans with automated literature QC, schema-constrained planning, and critic-driven revision loops.
data.detail:
# AccelP3-AI-scientist-clinical-trial-planner 

## Problem & Challenge

Designing a Phase 3 clinical trial is a slow, high-stakes process. The required information including prior clinical evidence, disease-area conventions, regulatory guidance, historical trial precedents, and operational constraints is scattered across disparate sources. Generic LLMs can generate plausible-sounding protocol text, but they hallucinate sample sizes, drift from the specified intervention, and produce no formal mechanism to catch endpoint-estimand inconsistencies or weak regulatory precedent. The result is trial designs that look complete but are scientifically and operationally fragile.

## Target Audience

Clinical scientists and medical directors in pharma and biotech preparing to enter Phase 3. CROs and trial design consultants who need rapid, structured protocol drafts. Academic researchers translating Phase 2 findings into late-stage trial designs. Regulatory affairs teams needing evidence-grounded design rationale.

## Solution & Core Features

AccelP3 is a multi-agent AI pipeline that converts a natural-language clinical hypothesis into a structured, evidence-informed Phase 3 trial plan. 

1. Structured Intake Extraction parses user input into a normalized Hypothesis intake object covering disease, intervention, endpoint, and Phase 2 report using schema-constrained LLM output. 

2. Abstract-Aware Literature QC queries PubMed via E-utilities, retrieves full abstracts, and applies a heuristic reranker to surface the most relevant precedent studies. 

3. Schema-Constrained Trial Planning generates a complete TrialPlan covering 15+ fields including estimand, eligibility, randomization, sample size rationale, regulatory strategy, visit schedule, and budget buckets. 

4. Critic-Based Review uses an independent reviewer agent to evaluate the plan with severity-graded findings (critical, important, minor) and structured revision instructions. 

5. The Bounded Planner-Critic Revision Loop iteratively refines the plan until the critic issues a PASS verdict or the revision cap is reached, which can be used for next report based on customer’s historical behavior. 

6. A Vue-based Live Demo Frontend provides async job polling and real-time thinking progress messages.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most AI clinical trial tools are either free-form LLM prompts with no structure or traceability, or citation retrieval systems with no generation capability. AccelP3 is different in three ways.   1. Schema-first architecture enforces Pydantic-validated outputs, forcing separation of endpoint definition, statistical assumptions, and operational planning. This prevents the hallucination and drift that plague single-prompt approaches.   2.Abstract-aware evidence grounding means literature is not just retrieved as titles or links. Abstracts are fetched and reranked so the planner actually incorporates prior evidence rather than relying on latent model knowledge.   3. An explicit critic loop uses a dedicated reviewer agent separate from the planner to identify endpoint-estimand inconsistencies, unsupported effect-size assumptions, and weak regulatory precedent before the plan is finalized. No other open demo system includes this formal review-and-revise cycle.

## Implementation & Technology

The LLM backend uses the OpenAI Responses API with strict JSON-schema mode for all generation steps. All structured outputs including HypothesisIntake, TrialPlan, CriticOutput, and LiteratureQCResult are validated with Pydantic models. Literature retrieval is powered by PubMed E-utilities (esearch, esummary, efetch) with a custom heuristic reranker. The TrialPlanningOrchestrator coordinates the full pipeline with bounded revision loop logic. JSON-structured run logs under logs/ capture every pipeline stage and revision round. The frontend is a static Vue.js application backed by a Python ThreadingHTTPServer with async job endpoints (POST /api/plan and GET /api/jobs/<id>).

## Results & Impact

AccelP3 produces trial plans that are more complete than free-form LLM output, with 15+ schema-enforced fields covering the full design space. Every major design decision is linked to a literature basis field, making the output fully traceable. The critic loop surfaces issues that single-pass generation would miss, including endpoint-estimand mismatches and unsupported statistical assumptions. A full structured Phase 3 plan with literature QC and critic review is generated in minutes rather than weeks of manual drafting. The system is currently focused on antiviral and influenza trials but the modular architecture allows literature retrieval, planning logic, critique rules, and the frontend to be iterated independently for any therapeutic area.

No other open demo system includes this formal review-and-revise cycle.

data.title: Alpha24 WB
data.summary:
Project Summary: Unmapped is an evidence-based hiring platform that replaces resumes with verifiable proof of work.

Millions of people have real skills but cannot prove them without formal credentials, while employers rely on CVs that don’t reflect actual ability. Unmapped solves this by converting real project work into structured, trusted skill signals.

Candidates submit GitHub repositories, live apps, and explanations. A hybrid Proof Engine combines deterministic checks (repository structure, runtime validation) with AI analysis to extract features, detect missing evidence, and infer skills. These are normalized, scored, and aggregated into a Skill Reliability metric, and only turned into badges when strict thresholds are met.

On the employer side, job descriptions are parsed into structured requirements. A hybrid matching engine ranks candidates using rule-based coverage, semantic similarity via embeddings, proof strength, and integrity signals.

The result is an explainable system that moves from evidence → skill → hiring decision, enabling companies to hire based on proven ability instead of credentials.
data.detail:
# Alpha24 WB

## Problem & Challenge

Millions of people have real skills but cannot prove them without formal credentials. Employers rely on CVs and self-declared skills, which are unreliable and exclude qualified talent. This creates a gap between actual ability and hiring decisions.

## Target Audience

Self-taught developers, freelancers, and informal talent globally, especially in emerging markets. On the other side: startups and companies looking for verified, job-ready talent.

## Solution & Core Features

Proof Engine: analyzes GitHub, live apps, and explanations
Skill inference with confidence and negative evidence detection
Skill Reliability scoring and evidence-backed badges
AI job parsing from natural language
Hybrid matching (rule-based + semantic embeddings)
Explainable hiring recommendations

## Unique Selling Proposition

Instead of resumes, Unmapped verifies real work. It combines deterministic validation, AI reasoning, and proof aggregation to create trustworthy, explainable hiring signals.

## Implementation & Technology

React/Vite frontend, Node/Express backend
LLM-based parsing, skill inference, and test generation
Embeddings + cosine similarity for semantic matching
GitHub and live demo analysis
Schema validation and fallback modes
Hybrid scoring (rules + AI + proof strength)

## Results & Impact

Transforms unstructured evidence into structured hiring decisions.
Expands access to jobs for invisible talent and improves hiring accuracy by focusing on proven ability instead of credentials.

data.title: ShotenX AI — Lightning-Native AgentMarketplace
data.summary: ShotenX AI is a Lightning-powered marketplace where users and autonomous AI agents access services through instant Bitcoin micropayments. No API keys, subscriptions, or signups required. Request, pay, and receive results in seconds. It enables a global pay-per-use economy for AI tools, APIs, and machine-to-machine commerce worldwide seamlessly.
data.detail:
# ShotenX AI — Lightning-Native AgentMarketplace

## Problem & Challenge

Current AI APIs rely on subscriptions, API keys, and manual billing systems, making them difficult for developers and impossible for autonomous AI agents to use seamlessly. Small pay-per-use transactions are inefficient, limiting access to AI services globally.

## Target Audience

Developers, startups, freelancers, API creators, SaaS companies, and future autonomous AI agents that need instant, affordable, and global access to AI tools.

## Solution & Core Features

ShotenX AI is a Lightning-native AI marketplace where users and agents discover services, pay instantly in Bitcoin satoshis, and receive results immediately. Features include AI marketplace, auto-agent selection, wallet integration, pay-per-use billing, and custom agent creation.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional API platforms, ShotenX removes subscriptions and API keys. It enables instant micropayments and machine-to-machine commerce, making it ideal for the future AI agent economy.

## Implementation & Technology

Built using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase, Express.js, ASI1 LLM APIs, Agentverser for marketplace, Bitcoin Lightning Network, L402 payment protocol, and Alby wallet integration for seamless real-time transactions.

## Results & Impact

ShotenX AI successfully demonstrates instant pay-per-use AI access using Bitcoin Lightning and L402 payments. We built a working marketplace with agent discovery, automated payments, wallet integration, and custom AI agents. It reduces friction, lowers costs, and enables the future machine-to-machine AI economy.

data.title: Atlas
data.summary: Atlas turns 12 minutes of phone chat into a verified ISCO-08 skills credential for unmapped LMIC youth, then matches them with employers posting projects on WhatsApp.
data.detail:
# Atlas

## Problem & Challenge

600M young people across 75 LIC + LMIC countries have no formal skills credential and no path into the labour market. Existing tools (LinkedIn, ESCO, Northern HR stacks) ignore informal-sector reality. Employers in Ghana, Bangladesh, Vietnam hire through WhatsApp groups, not résumés.

## Target Audience

1. Unmapped youth aged 15–24 in informal sectors. 2. SMEs and recruiters needing verified hands-on skill near them. 3. NGOs and government programme officers designing youth-employment cohorts.

## Solution & Core Features

A chat-native conversational game (12 min, on a phone they already own) measuring STEP-equivalent skills, AI fluency (Tier 0–4), and assigning a first ISCO-08 occupation code. The credential lands in a dual interface:

- **Recruiter Workspace** — verified candidates by ward + Post-a-Project flow that opens a WhatsApp room with a shortlist.
- **Cohort Insights dashboard** — 10 informal-skill clusters, Wittgenstein 2030, WBL 2024, FLFP, automation risk, all hardcoded from public sources.

## Unique Selling Proposition

The only credential that plugs into the channel users already live on (WhatsApp) and surfaces an **Atlas-original AI Tier 0–4** — a measurement that does not exist in any public dataset. Skills taxonomy is **ISCO-08 + O*NET + STEP** (LMIC-appropriate, not Eurocentric ESCO). Honest about limits: hackathon-grade rubrics, calibrated against Ghana 2013 STEP, disclosed up-front.

## Implementation & Technology

- **Frontend + API**: Next.js 16 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 on Vercel.
- **LLM**: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 with prompt caching for the channel-agnostic orchestrator (`runChapter`).
- **Persistence**: Vercel KV (Upstash Redis) for Atlas Cards and project rooms.
- **WhatsApp**: Twilio Sandbox + browser simulator share the same engine.
- **Real-data layer**: ILOSTAT, WDI, Frey-Osborne LMIC reappraisal, WBES, WBL 2024, Findex 2024, Wittgenstein, HCI — every datum cited at source.

## Results & Impact

Shipped on Vercel with three live surfaces and country-swap (Ghana ↔ Bangladesh) demonstrated on stage. 10 informal-skill clusters mapped to ISCO-08 with cohort share, female share, wage band, automation risk and skill-gap demand for both countries. The **Post-a-Project → matched candidates → WhatsApp room** flow closes the loop from credential to real work in the channel LMIC employers already use — turning a 12-minute conversation into a labour-market signal that did not exist before.

data.title: SafeMD.ai
data.summary: India is a nation where a postal code often determines a lifespan, SafeMD.ai uses Databricks-powered intelligence to ensure that a patient’s destination is a verified reality, not a dangerous mirage. SafeMD.ai leverages the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to turn 10,000+ messy facility reports into a life-saving "Reasoning Layer." While traditional registries act as static filing cabinets of outdated claims, our system, fueled by Agent Bricks and Mosaic AI, creates a living nervous system that verifies "Ground Truth" operational realities in real-time. By empowering frontline staff to report resource gaps and "near-misses" through a frictionless, text-based interface, we eliminate the dangerous "Functional Mirage" where hospitals list services they cannot actually provide. This agentic framework instantly flags contradictions, such as an ICU listing that lacks a reported anesthesiologist, to bridge the "Truth Gap" for 70% of the population in rural areas. By identifying "Specialized Deserts" for high-acuity needs like Oncology and Trauma, we reduce Discovery-to-Care time from hours to seconds. Beyond the patient experience, the platform autonomously applies the DMAIC framework to generate Lean-compliant A3 reports, providing NGOs and governments with the verified equity needed to ensure that national capacity building is driven by living needs, not fragmented paperwork.
data.detail:
# SafeMD.ai

## Problem & Challenge

SafeMD.ai is designed to unmask the "Functional Mirage" of healthcare capacity that currently leaves 70% of India’s rural population in a life-threatening "guessing game." In a nation where a postal code too often determines a lifespan, we replace static, theoretical hospital certifications with a "Ground Truth" map that reflects the actual, live capability of 10,000+ facilities. On paper, a hospital may claim to be a "General Surgery Hub," but if our system detects the absence of an anesthesiologist or a failure in the oxygen supply, it instantly flags that designation as a dangerous illusion—saving families from traveling hours for a promise that cannot be kept. By utilizing Databricks-powered agentic intelligence to bridge this "Truth Gap," we eliminate fatal navigational uncertainty and reduce the "Discovery-to-Care" time from a fragmented puzzle into a reliable, life-saving framework. Beyond the immediate patient journey, we solve the "Administrative Blindness" of NGOs and policymakers by rescuing "priceless data" from the burnout of manual paperwork and high-friction reporting systems. By transforming messy, frontline observations into a proactive Reasoning Layer, SafeMD.ai ensures that national resource allocation is no longer a delayed administrative reaction, but a direct, equitable response to the living pulse of India’s actual medical needs.

## Target Audience

SafeMD.ai is built to serve a critical ecosystem of stakeholders, primarily targeting Hospital and Health System Leadership and Governmental Health Authorities who are currently forced to manage in the dark. We empower CEOs and Quality Improvement directors with a real-time command center that transforms silent "near-miss" data into a loud, clear signal for action, automating the A3 and root-cause analysis process to solve safety crises before they reach the patient. Simultaneously, we provide the "Reasoning Layer" for policy architects in complex landscapes like India, replacing outdated, manual census data with a living "Smart Map" of verified truth to ensure that every government intervention is a precise, equitable strike against resource deserts. This transparency directly benefits individuals in rural and low-SES communities, ensuring that a postal code no longer determines a lifespan by turning healthcare from a fragmented gamble into a reliable right. By providing frontline healthcare staff with a frictionless, AI-driven voice to report operational realities—such as a missing anesthesiologist—exactly when they happen, we bridge the "Recall Gap," ensuring that the expertise of those on the ground drives immediate systemic reform and national health policy.

## Solution & Core Features

We solve the healthcare discovery and coordination crisis by creating a frictionless "intelligence highway" that rescues vital safety data from the "Recall Gap" and the burnout of manual paperwork, empowering staff to report near-misses and resource gaps exactly when they happen to ensure the truth is captured before it is lost to history. By leveraging Databricks-powered Agentic Capability Mapping, we unmask the "Functional Mirage" of care, protecting patients from traveling hours to a facility that—while certified as a surgery hub on paper—lacks the real-time staff or equipment necessary to save their lives. To sustain this system, we abolish the administrative burden of Quality Improvement by autonomously applying the DMAIC framework to frontline data, identifying dangerous systemic patterns without requiring a single minute of manual analysis from overworked staff. This transformation empowers leadership with instant clarity, generating Lean-compliant A3 reports that turn messy, unstructured notes into prioritized action points for proactive stewardship. Ultimately, SafeMD.ai democratizes access through verified equity, synchronizing real-time "resource desert" data with governmental databases to provide the essential Reasoning Layer that ensures national policy and capacity building are an equitable response to living needs rather than outdated, fragmented statistics.

## Unique Selling Proposition

What sets SafeMD.ai apart from existing solutions is its shift from passive record-keeping to an agentic, real-time feedback loop that mirrors how information actually flows on the front lines. Unlike traditional systems that rely on complex, manual forms or periodic surveys, methods that suffer from high friction and data loss, SafeMD.ai captures "Ground Truth" updates through a frictionless, text-based and scan-ready interface accessible on any hospital workstation or mobile device. The system achieves real-time updates by allowing staff and patients to immediately report incidents or resource gaps, such as the sudden absence of a specialist or a "near-miss" in the OR, as a simple free-text entry that our AI instantly processes, categorizes, and maps. This turns every staff member into a live sensor, feeding a dynamic "Smart Map" that shows the actual functional status of a facility at any given moment, rather than just its theoretical capabilities. While current software acts as a digital filing cabinet for old data, SafeMD.ai acts as a living nervous system that cross-references these live updates with governmental databases to identify "resource deserts" and generate instant, Lean-compliant A3 reports for immediate administrative action.

## Implementation & Technology

We technically implemented the solution using a Multi-Agent Orchestration pipeline on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, architected as an Agentic RAG framework. At the core of this system is a central controller that coordinates three specialized agents: a Triage Agent that parses messy, unstructured clinical text into structured JSON metadata; a Facility Scoring Agent that utilizes Mosaic AI Vector Search to perform Clinical Dependency Validation—ensuring a hospital’s claimed services are supported by real infrastructure; and a DMAIC Reporting Agent that applies Lean Six Sigma logic to generate actionable A3 Quality Improvement reports. Our tech stack leverages Unity Catalog for robust data governance and Delta Lake "Silver" tables to power our serverless vector indices, with embeddings generated via the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model for high-precision clinical reasoning. By wrapping the entire pipeline as an MLflow Python Model and deploying it through Databricks Model Serving, we have created a scalable, secure REST API that handles real-time inference, allowing for seamless integration into the frontlines of global healthcare.

## Results & Impact

By centering practical, life-saving features at the forefront, SafeMD.ai transforms the frantic "where do we go?" into a clear, filtered path: patients simply input their condition to instantly access a live map of verified facilities, each assigned a high-confidence Trust Score based on real-time capability and location. This "living nervous system" bridges the "Truth Gap" in Indian healthcare by transforming thousands of unstructured records into a dynamic Reasoning Layer that replaces static, often inaccurate hospital certifications with a "Ground Truth" map. By deploying a frictionless reporting interface and automated DMAIC frameworks, we verify actual clinical capabilities—such as the confirmed presence of specialists and life-saving equipment—at the moment of need. Our system achieves systemic social impact by identifying "resource deserts" and providing NGO planners with an executive dashboard for big-picture analysis, effectively turning live frontline updates into a strategic map for eliminating healthcare gaps. Ultimately, SafeMD.ai reduces the Discovery-to-Care time and provides the predictive, Lean-compliant A3 insights necessary to ensure that a patient’s postal code no longer dictates their survival.

data.title: Parwana — Finding the Light in India's Healthcare Deserts
data.summary:
We built the Reasoning Layer for Indian healthcare. An 8-agent 
AI system on Databricks that navigates 10,000+ messy, unstructured 
facility reports to reduce Discovery-to-Care time. It audits 
capabilities at scale, identifies specialized medical deserts, 
and navigates the Truth Gap, flagging contradictions where facility 
claims do not match reported equipment. Every recommendation is 
backed by row-level citations from the original facility notes.
data.detail:
# Parwana — Finding the Light in India's Healthcare Deserts

## Problem & Challenge

In India, a postal code often determines a lifespan. While the 
nation boasts world-class medical hubs, 70% of the population 
lives in rural areas where healthcare access is a fragmented puzzle. 
The issue is not just a lack of hospitals, it is a discovery and 
coordination crisis. Patients travel hours only to find a facility 
lacks the specific oxygen supply, neonatal bed, or specialist they 
urgently need.

The deeper problem is the Truth Gap: facility records are 
non-standardized, unstructured, and often contradictory. A hospital 
may list Advanced Surgery capabilities while having no Anesthesiologist 
on record. There is no ground truth, no verification layer, and no 
system that can audit 10,000 facilities at scale. Families are left 
guessing where to find help, and that gap costs lives.

## Target Audience

Primary: NGO planners, public health officials, and district healthcare  administrators across India who need reliable facility intelligence to  allocate resources and guide patients.  Secondary: Emergency responders and ambulance coordinators who need  real-time facility capability verification during critical situations.  Tertiary: Policy makers and the World Bank teams working on equitable  healthcare access in low-to-middle income countries who need data-driven  evidence of medical deserts to justify intervention budgets.

## Solution & Core Features

We built an Agentic Healthcare Intelligence System that addresses 
all three MVP requirements and all three stretch goals from the 
challenge brief:

MVP FEATURES:

1. Massive Unstructured Extraction
   Agent 4 and Agent 6 process free-form text from 10,000 facility 
   records stored in Unity Catalog (hackathon.healthcare.facility_docs). 
   This includes equipment logs, 24/7 availability claims, staff 
   specialties, and unstructured facility notes via Apache Spark on 
   Databricks Free Edition.

2. Multi-Attribute Reasoning
   Our 8-agent LangGraph pipeline answers complex natural language 
   queries combining location, capability, urgency, and availability. 
   Example: "Find the nearest facility in rural Bihar that can perform 
   an emergency appendectomy and typically leverages part-time doctors."
   Agent 1 parses the intent, Agent 2 resolves geography, Agent 3 
   filters by capability flags, Agent 4 retrieves semantically relevant 
   candidates, and Agent 5 ranks by proximity.

3. The Trust Scorer
   Agent 7 (Citation Verifier) flags suspicious and incomplete data. 
   It catches the exact contradiction the brief describes: a facility 
   claiming Advanced Surgery but listing no Anesthesiologist. Every 
   Trust Score is broken into equipment consistency, staff verification, 
   availability plausibility, and data completeness.

STRETCH GOALS ACHIEVED:

1. Agentic Traceability
   Agent 6 extracts the exact sentence from the raw facility note 
   that justifies every recommendation. The frontend displays the 
   full reasoning chain via MLflow 3 trace integration, every agent 
   step, input, output, confidence score, and duration is visible.

2. Self-Correction Loops
   Agent 7 is a dedicated Validator Agent that cross-references 
   Agent 6's extracted citations against the source text. It ensures 
   the primary agent is not hallucinating by verifying each citation 
   before it reaches the final answer.

3. Dynamic Crisis Mapping
   The Next.js frontend overlays all agent findings onto an 
   interactive India map. Medical deserts are highlighted by PIN 
   code using a D3 heatmap. NGO planners can filter by state, 
   district, capability, and trust score to identify the 
   highest-risk zones instantly.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Our system was built to win on all 4 judging criteria:  Discovery & Verification (35%): Our Validator Agent double-checks  its own citations, there is no other team with a dedicated  self-correction loop that verifies every extracted claim against  the source text before surfacing it to the user.  IDP Innovation (30%): We go beyond keyword search. Meta Llama 3.3  70B reasons through non-standardized, messy Indian facility  descriptions in natural language, handling vague queries like  "I need medical help in Mumbai" and returning ranked, cited results.  Social Impact (25%): Our medical desert heatmap by PIN code gives  NGO planners and district health officers a tool they can act on  immediately, not just data visualizations but verified, cited  intelligence about where capabilities are missing and where the  nearest alternatives are.  UX & Transparency (10%): Every recommendation shows its full  reasoning chain. A human can audit every decision the AI made  before trusting it, critical for life-or-death healthcare  decisions in a country of 1.4 billion people.

## Implementation & Technology

Backend — Databricks Free Edition:
- Data: 10,000+ facility records processed via Apache Spark and stored 
  in Unity Catalog (hackathon.healthcare.facility_docs)
- Agent Framework: LangGraph StateGraph with 8 nodes, each powered by 
  Meta Llama 3.3 70B via Databricks Foundation Model API
- Observability: MLflow 3 for full agent trace logging and cost tracking
- Retrieval: Keyword search with Spark SQL fallback; Vector Search ready
- Orchestration: Databricks Jobs API for scheduled and on-demand runs

Frontend — Next.js 14:
- Framework: Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript
- Map: react-leaflet with marker clustering, color-coded by trust score
- Charts: Recharts + D3.js for analytics and medical desert heatmap
- State: Zustand for global filter and facility state management
- API Layer: Next.js API Routes as passthrough proxy to Databricks
- UI: Tailwind CSS + Framer Motion + shadcn/ui

Integration:
- Frontend calls Databricks Jobs API to trigger the 8-agent pipeline
- Results fetched via Databricks SQL Warehouse
- MLflow traces fetched for chain-of-thought display
- All credentials server-side only, never exposed to browser

## Results & Impact

Technical Achievements:
- All 3 MVP requirements delivered in full
- All 3 stretch goals delivered in full  
- 8-agent LangGraph pipeline processes queries in 30-60 seconds
- Handles vague, complex, and multi-attribute natural language queries
- Agent 7 Validator catches contradictions autonomously with no 
  human intervention
- Full MLflow 3 trace logging for every agent run
- 10,000+ facility records processed via Apache Spark on 
  Databricks Free Edition with Unity Catalog governance

Social Impact:
- Reduces Discovery-to-Care time by replacing manual facility 
  verification with real-time AI intelligence
- Identifies medical deserts by PIN code enabling targeted 
  resource allocation
- Turns a static list of 10,000 buildings into a living 
  intelligence network — exactly the vision stated in the 
  challenge brief
- Creates a replicable blueprint for equitable healthcare 
  intelligence applicable to any country with similar unstructured 
  facility data challenges

Confidence Scoring (Research Question answered):
- We addressed the brief's open research question on confidence 
  scoring by building a 4-dimensional Trust Score breakdown per 
  facility, giving planners not just a number but a breakdown of 
  exactly which dimensions are reliable and which are uncertain

IDP Innovation (30%): We go beyond keyword search. Meta Llama 3.3  70B reasons through non-standardized, messy Indian facility  descriptions in natural language, handling vague queries like  "I need medical help in Mumbai" and returning ranked, cited results.  Social Impact (25%): Our medical desert heatmap by PIN code gives  NGO planners and district health officers a tool they can act on  immediately, not just data visualizations but verified, cited  intelligence about where capabilities are missing and where the  nearest alternatives are. data.title: City Wallet — Real‑Time Contextual Offers for Local Commerce
data.summary: City Wallet is an AI‑powered city wallet that generates hyper‑personalized local offers in real time using context such as weather, time, proximity, and merchant demand. Unlike static coupon apps, offers are created only when the moment is right and redeemed seamlessly through a simulated checkout.
data.detail:
# City Wallet — Real‑Time Contextual Offers for Local Commerce

## Problem & Challenge

Local shops lack the ability to respond to real‑time customer context. Existing coupon and loyalty apps use static, long‑validity discounts that fail to influence immediate decisions. Even when a customer and a relevant offer are in the same place and moment, there is no system connecting weather, time, location, and merchant demand into a timely, actionable offer.

## Target Audience

Urban consumers making spontaneous decisions during daily routines, and small local merchants (cafés, bakeries, retail stores) who lack access to dynamic personalization and real‑time marketing tools.

## Solution & Core Features

City Wallet is an AI‑powered city wallet that detects real‑time context (weather, time, proximity, and merchant demand) and generates hyper‑personalized local offers on the fly. Offers are created dynamically at the moment they are needed, displayed with clear UX, and redeemed through a seamless simulated checkout. Merchants participate by setting simple rules, while the system handles offer creation automatically.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional coupon apps, City Wallet does not store static offers. Offers only exist when the context is right. It combines real‑time city signals with privacy‑first, on‑device intent inference to deliver the right local offer at the right moment, giving small merchants algorithmic power previously available only to large platforms.

## Implementation & Technology

The solution is implemented as an end‑to‑end MVP with a context sensing layer, a generative offer engine, and a redemption flow. Context signals are evaluated through configurable rules, offers are generated dynamically at runtime, and redemption is simulated via tokens/QR. Personalization logic is designed to run locally, with only abstract intent shared, supporting GDPR‑compliant privacy by design.

## Results & Impact

The project demonstrates a complete working flow from context detection to offer redemption. It shows how local merchants can increase footfall during quiet periods while providing users with relevant, timely offers. City Wallet proves that inner‑city retail can compete with global platforms by using local context, not static discounts.

data.title: CityWallet — AI-Powered Generative Local Offers
data.summary: Real-time AI wallet that generates hyper-relevant local offers based on weather, time, location and demand. Not coupons — live deals.
data.detail:
# CityWallet — AI-Powered Generative Local Offers

## Problem & Challenge

Local merchants lose revenue during quiet hours while nearby customers pay full price elsewhere. Existing coupon apps show static, irrelevant offers that users ignore. There is no system that connects real-time merchant demand with real-time customer context to generate the right offer at the right moment.

## Target Audience

Two audiences: (1) City residents and shoppers who want relevant, timely local deals without searching for them. (2) Local merchants — cafés, bakeries, restaurants — who want to fill quiet periods without marketing expertise. Powered by Sparkasse/DSV-Gruppe banking infrastructure for German cities, scalable globally.

## Solution & Core Features

CityWallet is an AI-powered mobile wallet that generates dynamic local offers in real time. Core features: (1) AI Signal Engine — reads weather, time of day, user location, nearby events and merchant demand patterns every minute. (2) Generative Offer Engine — creates fresh, personalised offer text and pricing automatically — never static coupons. (3) Transparent AI Reasoning — shows users exactly WHY an offer was generated for them. (4) One-tap Checkout — secure payment via Sparkasse CityWallet, PSD2 compliant, with QR code redemption. (5) Merchant Rule Dashboard — shop owners set simple trigger rules, AI writes the offer automatically. (6) Ask AI — users can request custom offers based on their preferences and budget.

## Unique Selling Proposition

CityWallet is the only local offer platform where offers are generated fresh in real time — not pre-written. Every deal is created for a specific user, at a specific moment, based on live signals. Merchants need zero marketing skills — they set a rule once, AI does everything else. The AI reasoning layer builds trust by showing users why they received each offer.

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend: Single-page HTML/CSS/JavaScript app — mobile-first, fully responsive. AI Layer: Claude AI (Anthropic) for dynamic offer generation based on contextual signals. Signals: Real-time time, weather context, geolocation proximity, demand simulation. Payment: Simulated Sparkasse CityWallet checkout with PSD2 security compliance. Deployment: Vercel (zero-config, instant CDN). Repository: GitHub. The entire MVP is a deployable single HTML file — lightweight, fast, demo-ready.

## Results & Impact

Working end-to-end MVP with 5 fully interactive screens: Home wallet, AI offer feed, generative offer builder, secure checkout, and merchant dashboard. Demonstrates complete user journey from signal detection to QR code redemption in under 4 taps. Merchant portal shows rule-based setup, live performance stats and AI preview. Scalable beyond Germany to any city with local banking infrastructure.

structured.problem: Local merchants lose revenue during quiet hours while nearby customers pay full price elsewhere. Existing coupon apps show static, irrelevant offers that users ignore. There is no system that connects real-time merchant demand with real-time customer context to generate the right offer at the right moment.
structured.solution: CityWallet is an AI-powered mobile wallet that generates dynamic local offers in real time. Core features: (1) AI Signal Engine — reads weather, time of day, user location, nearby events and merchant demand patterns every minute. (2) Generative Offer Engine — creates fresh, personalised offer text and pricing automatically — never static coupons. (3) Transparent AI Reasoning — shows users exactly WHY an offer was generated for them. (4) One-tap Checkout — secure payment via Sparkasse CityWallet, PSD2 compliant, with QR code redemption. (5) Merchant Rule Dashboard — shop owners set simple trigger rules, AI writes the offer automatically. (6) Ask AI — users can request custom offers based on their preferences and budget.

jury_scope.notes: Temporary Event 5 live leaderboard display test

structured.implementation: Frontend: Single-page HTML/CSS/JavaScript app — mobile-first, fully responsive. AI Layer: Claude AI (Anthropic) for dynamic offer generation based on contextual signals. Signals: Real-time time, weather context, geolocation proximity, demand simulation. Payment: Simulated Sparkasse CityWallet checkout with PSD2 security compliance. Deployment: Vercel (zero-config, instant CDN). Repository: GitHub. The entire MVP is a deployable single HTML file — lightweight, fast, demo-ready.
structured.targetAudience: Two audiences: (1) City residents and shoppers who want relevant, timely local deals without searching for them. (2) Local merchants — cafés, bakeries, restaurants — who want to fill quiet periods without marketing expertise. Powered by Sparkasse/DSV-Gruppe banking infrastructure for German cities, scalable globally.

data.title: Operon AI
data.summary: An AI-powered scientific planning pipeline that transforms a research hypothesis into a complete, evidence-grounded, QA-validated experiment plan in under 60 seconds.
data.detail:
# Operon AI

## Problem & Challenge

Scientists lose days — sometimes weeks — turning a hypothesis into a lab-ready experiment plan. The process is manual: literature searches are ad hoc, failure modes are missed, controls are undefined, and plans are frequently rejected by reviewers before a single experiment runs. There is no structured, AI-assisted layer between a scientist's idea and a rigorous experiment design.

## Target Audience

Research scientists, lab PIs, and graduate students in life sciences, synthetic biology, and any regulated research domain who need to move from hypothesis to structured experiment plan faster and with higher scientific rigor.

## Solution & Core Features

Operon AI is a five-agent pipeline built on Anthropic Claude claude-sonnet-4-5. A scientist enters their hypothesis via a 7-field structured wizard. Agent 1 audits the hypothesis across 7 scientific dimensions and blocks weak hypotheses before the pipeline runs. Agents 2–3 retrieve live evidence via two parallel Tavily searches, novelty-classified by Semantic Scholar. Agent 4 generates a full experiment plan: protocol, controls, failure modes, statistical analysis, and timeline. Agent 5 — the QA Critic — enforces a confidence score gate (≥70, zero blockers) before the plan is released. An Expert Feedback Memory system stores scientist corrections and injects them as few-shot examples in future runs, so the system improves with every use.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike generic AI writing tools or chatbots, Operon AI enforces scientific rigor at every step through hard pipeline gates — a weak hypothesis is blocked before anything generates, and a plan cannot be released unless it passes a QA confidence threshold server-side. Every evidence claim is traceable to a real paper DOI retrieved live via Tavily and Semantic Scholar — no hallucinated citations. The system also learns from expert corrections over time, injecting validated feedback as few-shot examples into future runs.

## Implementation & Technology

Built on Next.js 16 App Router with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. Five Claude claude-sonnet-4-5 agents orchestrated via Server-Sent Events for real-time streaming. Tavily Search API fires two parallel evidence searches per run. Semantic Scholar API handles novelty classification deterministically — no LLM, for reproducibility. Zod schema validation enforces typed I/O on every agent boundary. Prisma ORM with SQLite in development and libSQL Turso in production. Expert Feedback Memory stores ScientistReview and ScientistCorrection records, retrieved by cosine similarity at inference time.

## Results & Impact

Operon AI reduces the time from hypothesis to lab-ready experiment plan from days to under 60 seconds. It surfaces failure modes, missing controls, and weak assumptions before the experiment runs — not after it fails. The QA gate eliminates plans that would be rejected in peer review. For research institutions, this translates directly into fewer wasted experiment cycles, faster iteration, and higher-quality submissions. The Expert Feedback Memory means every domain expert who uses the system makes it measurably better for the next researcher.

The system also learns from expert corrections over time, injecting validated feedback as few-shot examples into future runs.

data.title: UNMAPPED Pro
data.summary: UNMAPPED is a Skill Passport infrastructure that converts informal work experiences (like gig work or family businesses) into structured, verified, and portable digital profiles. Using AI, it maps these skills to international standards (like ESCO) to help users unlock global economic opportunities and access fair wage projections.
data.detail:
# UNMAPPED Pro

## Problem & Challenge

Hundreds of millions of young people in low- and middle-income countries have real, demonstrable skills (Amara — 22, Accra, runs a phone-repair business since 17) but no
  formal labor-market signal. Education credentials don't translate, informal experience is invisible, and AI is rewriting the skills landscape faster than institutions can
  track.

## Target Audience

• Primary: youth in LMICs with informal, uncredentialed skills (low-bandwidth, shared device).   • Secondary: training providers, NGOs, employers, and policymakers who need a portable, comparable skills signal.

## Solution & Core Features

UNMAPPED — an open, localizable infrastructure layer (not just an app) covering all three challenge modules:
  • Skills Signal Engine — intake → portable Skills Profile mapped to ISCO-08 / ESCO / O*NET, with a transparent credibility score (Hack-Nation 5-component formula).
  • Opportunity Matching & Econometric Dashboard — grounded, conservative role matches; dual youth + policymaker view.
  • AI Readiness Lens — per-cluster automation risk, durable vs. at-risk parts, adjacent skills, LMIC-discounted.
  • Limitations screen — explicit about what the tool does not know.
  • One-click Amara persona — full demo profile prefilled.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Real economic data, not synthetic proxies. Surfaces 2 econometric categories visibly with source codes on every card: ILOSTAT wages + WDI/Data360 indicators     (SL.UEM.NEET.ZS, SL.ISV.IFRM.ZS, NY.GDP.PCAP.KD.ZG) + Frey-Osborne LMIC-adapted automation exposure.   • Honest, not aspirational. Trust-gated matching, education filters, "roles we deliberately did NOT show you" panel.   • Explainable. Every inference logs step / input / rule / output / taxonomyRef — no black box.   • Localizable by design. Country, language, taxonomy, automation calibration are runtime inputs (countryConfig), not hardcoded.   • Built for constraint. Works on a shared phone over 2G — vanilla MVP is ~30 KB, no CDN, no fonts, no build step.

## Implementation & Technology

• Frontend: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS + Recharts + Framer Motion (team build); a parallel vanilla HTML/CSS/JS MVP for ultra-low-bandwidth contexts.
  • Architecture layers: skillsProfileSchema (portable JSON v0.2.0), countryConfig, taxonomy (ISCO/ESCO/O*NET), mappingLogic, credibilityScore, matchingLogic, labormarket,
    aiReadiness.
  • Real datasets bundled: iloWages.ts/.js (~140 countries, ILOSTAT mean hourly earnings) and wdiSignals.js (World Bank WDI / Data360 / ITU indicators with explicit codes).
    Replacement path = single file → live World Bank API.
  • Persistence: localStorage (deliberate — no login wall on a shared device).
  • No external AI calls in the critical path so it works offline / on slow connections.

## Results & Impact

Working end-to-end demo: prefill Amara → Skills Profile (ISCO 7421 / ESCO / ONET) → Credibility 75/100 with 5-component breakdown → 3 grounded opportunities (electronics
    mechanic, micro-business, junior dev training pathway*) with real ILOSTAT wages + 29.3% youth NEET (WDI) + 88.1% informal share (Data360) → AI Readiness lens flagging
    durable vs. at-risk skills.
  • Why it matters: turns invisible informal skills into a portable, verifiable signal that any government, NGO, or employer can plug into — a building block for shared
    economic mobility for the 600M+ young people the formal economy currently fails to see.

data.title: Project Panacea
data.summary: An AI-powered healthcare intelligence system that extracts structured data from unstructured hospital records, verifies facility claims using rule-based reasoning, and recommends trusted healthcare providers using semantic search and explainable ranking.
data.detail:
# Project Panacea

## Problem & Challenge

Healthcare facility data in India is often unstructured, incomplete, and unreliable. Patients and organizations rely on self-reported information from hospitals, which may contain inconsistencies or exaggerated claims (e.g., ICU availability without proper equipment). 

This creates a “trust gap” where users cannot confidently identify facilities that truly meet their medical needs. Additionally, finding suitable hospitals requires reasoning over multiple attributes such as specialties, infrastructure, distance, and reliability—something traditional search systems cannot handle effectively.

## Target Audience

Our solution primarily benefits:  - Patients and caregivers searching for reliable healthcare facilities - NGOs and policymakers identifying underserved regions - Healthcare analysts evaluating facility capabilities and gaps  The main target group is patients and families who need trustworthy and explainable healthcare recommendations.

## Solution & Core Features

We built a system that combines structured extraction, verification, and semantic retrieval to provide reliable healthcare recommendations.

Core features:

1. Structured Data Extraction  
   Converts unstructured hospital descriptions into structured fields (beds, doctors, ICU, specialties, etc.) using LLM-based parsing.

2. Claim Verification Engine  
   Detects contradictions between claimed services and actual capabilities using rule-based reasoning (e.g., ICU claim without ventilators).

3. Trust Scoring  
   Assigns each facility a reliability score (VERIFIED / LIKELY / SUSPECT) with clear explanations.

4. Semantic Search (RAG-based)  
   Uses embeddings to retrieve relevant hospitals based on user queries like “cardiac surgery near Delhi”.

5. Multi-Attribute Reasoning  
   Filters facilities based on required capabilities (e.g., surgery requires surgeon + OT + ICU).

6. Explainable Results  
   Each recommendation includes:
   - Why it was selected  
   - Trust score  
   - Supporting evidence from source text  

7. Desert Detection (Stretch Feature)  
   Identifies underserved regions using a simple population-to-facility ratio.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional RAG systems that only retrieve information, our solution focuses on verification and reasoning.  Key differentiators:  - Trust-aware recommendations instead of raw search results   - Explicit contradiction detection between claims and data   - Explainable outputs with supporting evidence   - Multi-factor ranking combining relevance, trust, and distance    This makes the system more reliable, transparent, and decision-oriented.

## Implementation & Technology

The system is implemented as a modular pipeline:

- Data Processing: Python (Pandas)
- Extraction: LLM-based structured parsing
- Embeddings: Sentence Transformers for semantic search
- Backend: FastAPI for API endpoints
- Frontend: Next.js for user interface
- Visualization: Plotly for underserved region analysis

Pipeline flow:

Unstructured Text → LLM Extraction → Structured Data  
→ Trust Scoring (rule-based)  
→ Embedding Search  
→ Query Reasoning (ontology-based filtering)  
→ Multi-factor Ranking (similarity + trust + distance)  
→ Explainable Output via API

The system is designed to be lightweight, modular, and easily extensible.

## Results & Impact

The system successfully demonstrates:

- Accurate extraction of structured healthcare attributes from messy text  
- Detection of inconsistencies in facility claims  
- Reliable ranking of hospitals based on both relevance and trust  
- Clear, explainable recommendations for end users  

Impact:

- Reduces misinformation in healthcare discovery  
- Improves decision-making for patients  
- Helps identify gaps in healthcare infrastructure  

Overall, the solution bridges the trust gap in healthcare data using AI-driven reasoning.

data.title: SERVING A NATION - AI HEALTHCARE AGENT
data.summary: An agentic AI system that extracts, validates, and queries 10,000 Indian medical facility records. Built a Trust Scorer that flags contradictions (e.g. facilities claiming ICU but listing no oxygen supply), a Medical Desert Score by district to identify underserved regions, and a natural language query agent powered by Llama 3.3 70B on Databricks. Includes a live Gradio interface and interactive Folium map of India's highest-risk medical deserts.
data.detail:
# SERVING A NATION - AI HEALTHCARE AGENT

## Problem & Challenge

In India, a postal code often determines a lifespan. While 70% of the population lives in rural areas, healthcare access remains a fragmented discovery crisis. Patients travel hours only to find a facility lacks the specific equipment or specialist they urgently need. The problem is not just a lack of hospitals — it is that no intelligent system exists to verify what facilities actually have versus what they claim to have.

## Target Audience

NGO healthcare planners, government health ministries, and emergency responders who need accurate, actionable intelligence about medical facility capabilities across India's 10,000+ facilities.

## Solution & Core Features

MedRadar India is an agentic AI system built on Databricks that extracts, validates, and queries 10,000 Indian medical facility records. Core features: (1) Intelligent extraction of capabilities from unstructured facility notes into structured fields. (2) A Trust Scorer that flags contradictions such as facilities claiming ICU but listing no oxygen supply, or claiming 24/7 service with fewer than 2 doctors. (3) A Medical Desert Score by district identifying India's most underserved regions. (4) A natural language query agent powered by Llama 3.3 70B that answers complex multi-attribute queries like "Find a 24/7 facility in rural Bihar for emergency surgery.(5)  An interactive Folium map overlaying trust scores and medical deserts across India.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike simple hospital directories, MedRadar India does not trust what facilities claim — it verifies it. Our Trust Scorer applies medical logic rules to flag suspicious data, penalising facilities that claim advanced capabilities without the staff or equipment to support them. This makes our recommendations genuinely reliable, not just a list of names.

## Implementation & Technology

Built entirely on Databricks Free Edition. Data pipeline: pandas-based extraction from 10,000 CSV records into structured capability fields. Validation layer: rule-based Trust Scorer with 5 contradiction detection rules logged via MLflow. Query agent: Llama 3.3 70B via Databricks serving endpoints, combining keyword-based candidate filtering with LLM multi-attribute reasoning. Frontend: Gradio live interface with natural language search. Visualisation: Folium heatmap of medical deserts with facility-level popups showing trust scores and contradictions.

## Results & Impact

Processed all 10,000 facilities with zero errors. Identified 35 suspicious facilities making unverifiable claims, 33 genuinely high-trust facilities, and mapped India's highest-risk medical deserts by district. The system can answer complex emergency queries in seconds, providing ranked results with trust scores, contradiction warnings, and evidence from raw facility notes. For NGO planners, this turns a static spreadsheet into a living intelligence network that knows where help is and where it is critically missing.

data.title: CareMap: India's Healthcare Intelligence Network
data.summary: In India, a postal code often determines a lifespan, facility claims are messy and families can't find trustworthy emergency care. CareMap AI validates 10,000 facilities through six AI agents that extract capabilities from unstructured text, detect medical contradictions, and assign interpretable trust scores. Families access verified emergency care for free. NGOs map medical deserts. Institutions pay for supplier matching, connecting facilities flagged for missing equipment with verified vendors, creating a B2B marketplace that funds the social impact layer. Built on Databricks with Delta tables, Mosaic Vector Search, and MLflow tracing.
data.detail:
# CareMap: India's Healthcare Intelligence Network

## Problem & Challenge

In India, 70% of the population lives in rural areas where healthcare discovery is a crisis. Facility claims are messy, incomplete, and contradictory. A hospital might claim ICU capability but have no oxygen supply. Families searching for emergency care can't verify which facilities are trustworthy. NGOs can't identify where medical deserts exist. There's no systematic way to validate facility claims across the country, leaving millions at risk of choosing the wrong facility in life-or-death situations.

## Target Audience

Three primary users: (1) Families in rural India searching for verified emergency care during medical crises, (2) NGOs and health planners identifying medical deserts and resource gaps to deploy interventions strategically, and (3) Hospitals seeking verified suppliers for critical medical equipment.

## Solution & Core Features

CareMap AI validates facility claims through six AI agents built on Databricks. The Extraction Agent structures unstructured facility text into capabilities. The Validation Agent detects medical contradictions (e.g., claims surgery but no anesthesiologist). The Trust Scoring Agent assigns 0-100 scores with evidence. The Query Agent uses Mosaic Vector Search for semantic retrieval and ranks by trust + distance. Families get a search interface with reasoning traces. NGOs get a dashboard mapping medical deserts by district. Hospitals access supplier matching for flagged equipment gaps.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike keyword search or simple facility listings, CareMap is the only system that validates facility claims using medical logic and shows you the evidence. When a facility has an orange trust score, you see exactly why: "Claims 24/7 care but no night staff recorded." Every decision is traceable through MLflow. The dual-interface serves both social impact (free for families/NGOs) and sustainable revenue (B2B supplier marketplace).

## Implementation & Technology

Built entirely on Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Delta tables store 10,000 facility records. Six Python agents (Extraction, Validation, Trust Scoring, Symptom Triage, Query, Desert Detection) process data with Pydantic validation. Mosaic AI Vector Search enables semantic facility retrieval. MLflow 3 traces every agent decision for full observability. Unity Catalog governs data access. React + Tailwind frontend with FastAPI backend gateway. Databricks Genie powers natural language queries for the NGO dashboard. Complete end-to-end pipeline from raw Excel to production web app.

## Results & Impact

Validated 10,000 facilities with interpretable trust scores and evidence-backed contradiction flags. Built dual interface serving families (emergency search with reasoning traces), NGOs (medical desert mapping with actionable insights), and hospitals (supplier matching marketplace). Created a sustainable business model: free social impact layer funded by B2B revenue. Demonstrated that agentic AI can turn messy healthcare data into life-saving intelligence at national scale. Full Databricks stack integration proves production readiness.

The dual-interface serves both social impact (free for families/NGOs) and sustainable revenue (B2B supplier marketplace).

Created a sustainable business model: free social impact layer funded by B2B revenue. Demonstrated that agentic AI can turn messy healthcare data into life-saving intelligence at national scale. Full Databricks stack integration proves production readiness.
structured.problem: In India, 70% of the population lives in rural areas where healthcare discovery is a crisis. Facility claims are messy, incomplete, and contradictory. A hospital might claim ICU capability but have no oxygen supply. Families searching for emergency care can't verify which facilities are trustworthy. NGOs can't identify where medical deserts exist. There's no systematic way to validate facility claims across the country, leaving millions at risk of choosing the wrong facility in life-or-death situations.
structured.solution: CareMap AI validates facility claims through six AI agents built on Databricks. The Extraction Agent structures unstructured facility text into capabilities. The Validation Agent detects medical contradictions (e.g., claims surgery but no anesthesiologist). The Trust Scoring Agent assigns 0-100 scores with evidence. The Query Agent uses Mosaic Vector Search for semantic retrieval and ranks by trust + distance. Families get a search interface with reasoning traces. NGOs get a dashboard mapping medical deserts by district. Hospitals access supplier matching for flagged equipment gaps.

jury_scope.notes: Temporary Event 5 live leaderboard display test

structured.implementation: Built entirely on Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. Delta tables store 10,000 facility records. Six Python agents (Extraction, Validation, Trust Scoring, Symptom Triage, Query, Desert Detection) process data with Pydantic validation. Mosaic AI Vector Search enables semantic facility retrieval. MLflow 3 traces every agent decision for full observability. Unity Catalog governs data access. React + Tailwind frontend with FastAPI backend gateway. Databricks Genie powers natural language queries for the NGO dashboard. Complete end-to-end pipeline from raw Excel to production web app.
structured.targetAudience: Three primary users: (1) Families in rural India searching for verified emergency care during medical crises, (2) NGOs and health planners identifying medical deserts and resource gaps to deploy interventions strategically, and (3) Hospitals seeking verified suppliers for critical medical equipment.

data.title: unmap
data.summary: An open, localizable infrastructure protocol that bridges the gap between unrecognized youth skills and economic opportunity in low-bandwidth, informal labor markets using AI-driven narrative intake and contextual automation risk mapping.
data.detail:
# unmap

## Problem & Challenge

Global labor markets rely on credential-heavy platforms that exclude youth in informal economies. Existing systems often ignore local infrastructure constraints (low bandwidth, shared devices) and fail to provide honest, evidence-based skills verification. Additionally, generic AI "displacement" metrics don't account for local realities, leaving youth without actionable upskilling pathways.

## Target Audience

Youth in constrained economic environments (e.g., urban informal economies in Ghana, rural agriculture in Bangladesh), NGOs, and governments looking for resilient, data-sovereign labor infrastructure.

## Solution & Core Features

Mirror Test Engine: An adaptive, swipe-based interface for verifying experience against O*NET/ESCO task descriptions.

Narrative Skill Extraction: AI-driven intake that assigns transparent confidence tiers (Unverified, Recognized, Demonstrated).

Contextual AI Readiness Lens: Recalibrates automation risk using local ITU and Global Findex data rather than global averages.

Protocol Architecture: Configuration-driven design that allows hot-swapping labor data and languages without code changes.

## Unique Selling Proposition

UNMAPPED is an offline-first protocol, not just an app. Unlike competitors, it integrates World Bank econometric signals to match users with "locally reachable" opportunities and uses an honest confidence-tier system that prioritizes data sovereignty and evidence over generative certainty.

## Implementation & Technology

Languages/Frameworks: Python, Next.js.

Core Logic: AI-driven narrative extraction and adaptive verification engines.

Architecture: Fully configuration-driven for instant localization; designed for low-bandwidth and shared-device environments.

Data Integration: O*NET, ESCO, ITU Digital Development, and Global Findex datasets.

## Results & Impact

UNMAPPED delivers a portable, evidence-based skills profile that empowers youth with data sovereignty. It transforms "unrecognized" skills into verifiable economic assets, providing a resilient path to employment that is grounded in the user's specific regional and structural reality.
techStack: python next js ai labor-economics offline-first

data.title: UzairTalentmap.ai
data.summary: TalentMap.ai is an AI-powered skill verification platform that evaluates individuals based on real-world abilities instead of formal degrees. It analyzes user projects, portfolios, and GitHub data to generate a credibility score and connect skilled individuals with relevant job opportunities, enabling fair and efficient skill-based hiring.
data.detail:
# UzairTalentmap.ai

## Problem & Challenge

Millions of self-taught and skilled individuals remain invisible to employers because they lack formal degrees or certifications. Traditional hiring systems prioritize academic credentials over real-world skills, creating a gap where capable individuals are overlooked. This results in unemployment, underutilized talent, and inefficient hiring processes.

## Target Audience

Self-taught developers, freelancers, and skilled workers Students without strong academic backgrounds but with practical skills Startups and companies looking for skill-based hiring Recruiters seeking verified and reliable talent

## Solution & Core Features

TalentMap.ai is a skill-first verification platform that evaluates individuals based on real ability rather than degrees.

Core Features:
User Skill Profiling: Users register and submit their skills and projects
AI-Based Skill Analysis: System analyzes submitted data and evaluates capability
Proof of Work Verification: GitHub, project demos, and portfolio validation
Skill Scoring System: Generates a credibility score for each user
Smart Talent Matching: Connects users with relevant job opportunities

## Unique Selling Proposition

Focuses on skills over degrees Uses AI-driven verification instead of self-claimed profiles Provides real proof-based credibility scoring Bridges the gap between informal talent and formal job markets

## Implementation & Technology

The system is implemented as a modern web-based application using:

Frontend: Next.js (React + TypeScript)
Backend: Node.js API
Database: MySQL
AI Integration: Resume parsing and skill analysis models
APIs: GitHub API for project verification
Deployment: Web-based scalable architecture

## Results & Impact

Enables fair opportunities for non-degree holders
Improves hiring efficiency for companies
Promotes a skill-based economy
Encourages practical learning and project-based growth
Helps uncover hidden talent globally

data.title: Unmapped  Work search opportunity
data.summary:
Our project is an AI-powered hiring platform that connects job seekers and employers through smart candidate matching.
Job seekers upload their CV, ChatGPT history, and personal information, and the system analyzes their skills, experience, and job suitability using AI, storing the results in ChromaDB.
Employers describe the type of employee they need, and the platform uses semantic search and AI to find and rank the best matching candidates. Employers can then view full candidate profiles and contact them directly.
data.detail:
# Unmapped  Work search opportunity

## Problem & Challenge

Our project solves the problem of inefficient and inaccurate hiring.

Traditional hiring often depends only on CVs and keyword matching, which can miss strong candidates and takes a lot of manual work. Employers spend too much time filtering applications, while job seekers may be ignored even if they are a good fit.

Our platform uses AI to analyze real skills, experience, and communication patterns from CVs and ChatGPT history, then matches candidates with employers based on actual compatibility instead of simple keywords. This makes hiring faster, smarter, and more accurate.

## Target Audience

Our target audience includes job seekers looking for better career opportunities and employers who want to hire the right people faster.  It is especially useful for students, junior developers, freelancers, and professionals who want their real skills to be recognized beyond a traditional CV. On the employer side, it helps startups, small businesses, and recruiting teams reduce hiring time and find better-matched candidates through AI-powered analysis.

## Solution & Core Features

Our solution is an AI-powered hiring platform that improves recruitment by matching employers with the most suitable candidates based on skills, experience, and overall compatibility.

Core features include CV and ChatGPT history upload, AI analysis of candidate profiles, semantic search using ChromaDB, candidate ranking by compatibility score, full profile viewing for employers, and direct contact through email. This replaces manual CV filtering with faster and smarter AI-driven hiring.

## Unique Selling Proposition

What makes our project different is that it goes beyond traditional CV-based hiring and simple keyword filtering.  Instead of relying only on resumes, our platform also analyzes ChatGPT history and communication patterns to better understand a candidate’s real skills, problem-solving ability, and professional thinking. Using AI with semantic search and ChromaDB, it finds deeper compatibility between employers and candidates, not just matching words on a CV. This makes hiring more accurate, personalized, and efficient compared to standard job platforms.

## Implementation & Technology

We implemented it as a full-stack web app.

Frontend: React + Vite, with custom CSS animations and forms for job seekers and employers.

Backend: Python + FastAPI, which receives applications, uploaded CVs, and ChatGPT history files through API endpoints.

AI/Matching: We use an AI agent to analyze candidate data and generate candidate profiles.

Database/Search: Candidate profiles are stored in ChromaDB, a vector database. Employers submit a job description, and the backend uses semantic search to find and rank the most relevant candidates.

Connection: The frontend sends requests to the backend using fetch() API.

## Results & Impact

Our project improves the hiring process by making candidate selection faster, smarter, and more accurate.

Instead of manually reviewing large numbers of CVs, employers receive ranked candidates based on real compatibility and AI analysis. This reduces hiring time and increases the chance of finding the right person for the role.

For job seekers, it creates fairer opportunities by evaluating actual skills, experience, and problem-solving ability—not just keywords on a resume. This helps strong candidates stand out even if their CV is not perfect.
