data.title: Skills Paasport
data.summary: UNMAPPED is a headless AI protocol and web app that turns informal work experience into structured, portable skills profiles — making the 90% of African and Asian youth working informally visible to labor markets, employers, and the programs trying to reach them.
data.detail:
# Skills Paasport

## Problem & Challenge

In Eastern and Southern Africa alone, 8 million young people enter the labor market each year, yet fewer than 1 million land a waged job. Across the continent, 80–90% of employed youth work informally — their real skills invisible to credentialing systems, hiring platforms, and the NGOs and city programs trying to support them. Three failures compound each other: education credentials don't translate into labor market signals, automation is arriving faster than youth can navigate it, and matching still happens through informal networks that exclude the most vulnerable.

## Target Audience

Young people aged 18–35 navigating gigs, family businesses, and side hustles in low-income urban and peri-urban areas across Africa and Asia. Secondary users are government offices, NGOs, and city programs trying to design youth interventions but flying blind on who has what skills.

## Solution & Core Features

UNMAPPED delivers three connected modules. A Skills Signal Engine converts informal experience into portable, ISCO/ESCO-mapped profiles through conversational onboarding — grouping skills as Technical, Interpersonal, and Entrepreneurial, and linking them to roles across formal employment, gig work, self-employment, and training pathways. An AI Readiness module applies a Frey-Osborne automation risk approach calibrated for low-income urban contexts, labelling skills as durable, shifting, or at risk — honestly, not alarmingly. An Opportunity Matching engine surfaces grounded opportunities with ILO wage floors and World Bank sector growth signals visible to the user, not buried in the algorithm. The entire system is country-agnostic by design: a configuration panel lets teams switch labor data sources, skills taxonomies, interface languages, and urban or rural automation calibration without touching the codebase.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most talent platforms are built for credentialed workers and retrofitted for the Global South. UNMAPPED is built from the ground up as infrastructure — a practical layer between existing youth skilling investments and the millions of young workers they still fail to see. Localizability is a design constraint, not a feature. Real econometric data is surfaced to users, not hidden in the algorithm. And every young person leaves with a portable, human-readable skills card they own.

## Implementation & Technology

The frontend was built with React and Next.js using Lovable, delivering a responsive, low-bandwidth-conscious UI across onboarding, readiness assessment, opportunity matching, and profile dashboard screens. The intelligence layer uses Python with LangChain and a deterministic LangGraph router that ground-truths every response against real World Bank and ILO datasets — ensuring zero hallucination. The engine is wrapped in a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server with Graceful Fallback handling, making it unbreakable and plug-and-play for any NGO or government partner. Country-specific context — wage data, education taxonomies, automation calibration, and UI language — is managed through a swappable JSON config layer, making the system deployable across contexts without code changes.

## Results & Impact

UNMAPPED addresses a market failure affecting hundreds of millions: the 600 million+ young people in LMICs whose real skills are economically invisible. Our immediate target is the 8 million youth entering Eastern and Southern African labor markets annually, of whom fewer than 1 million access formal waged work.

The platform is designed to move three needles that existing youth skilling investments have failed to shift together: skills recognition at scale, honest automation readiness for informal workers, and grounded opportunity matching that accounts for local economic realities rather than aspirational outcomes.

Because UNMAPPED is infrastructure rather than a standalone app, its impact compounds. Every NGO, city program, or government office that plugs into the MCP server inherits the full skills mapping, matching, and readiness engine without rebuilding it — meaning a single deployment can serve multiple implementing partners simultaneously across different country contexts.

Concretely, a young person who completes the onboarding flow leaves with a portable, ISCO/ESCO-mapped skills card linked to real opportunities and an honest picture of which of their skills are durable versus at risk — information that previously required access to career counselors, formal education systems, or expensive assessments that informal workers cannot reach.

For policymakers and program officers, the aggregate dashboard turns the invisible informal workforce into legible labor market intelligence — showing which skills are concentrated where, where the demand-supply gaps are widest, and which interventions are most likely to close them. That signal has value not just for individual youth, but for the systems designed to serve them.

data.title: SIGNAL: (Skills Intelligence Grid for Networked Assessment & Livelihood)
data.summary: SIGNAL supports the UNMAPPED brief: informal skills map to ISCO-08 style occupations with short explanations; automation risk uses a connectivity-informed calibration over a baseline; regional JSON kernels supply wage, penetration, and outlook-style context; exports include a print-friendly CV, a technical JSON appendix, and an HMAC-based passport when the flow allows. English, Urdu (RTL), and Twi, plus a low-bandwidth mode. FastAPI backend, React / TypeScript / Vite frontend; new regions are primarily kernel and config work.
data.detail:
# SIGNAL: (Skills Intelligence Grid for Networked Assessment & Livelihood)

## Problem & Challenge

Formal records miss many young workers in LMICs. Paper credentials rarely describe what someone can do; informal skill stays hard to see; automation pressure varies by place while many tools assume strong connectivity; links between talent and opportunity stay weak. The challenge is infrastructure that partners can adopt with local parameters, not a one-off showcase app.

## Target Audience

Youth in informal or hybrid work who need recognition and realistic pay and risk context. NGOs and program staff who need readable summaries and regional aggregates. Partners who need structured briefing exports and machine-checkable credentials where systems require them.

## Solution & Core Features

Guided check-in: profile (including qualification and location), up to three skills, verification Q&A, readiness score with a 65% threshold for directory listing in the demo. Mapping: POST /api/skills/map with LLM path and deterministic fallback. Risk: baseline exposure plus infrastructure buffer using kernel penetration; 2035 outlook fields in kernels for narrative. UI: visible wage band, penetration, base and calibrated risk, and outlook where data exists. Regions: demo Ghana (Accra), Pakistan (Punjab), San Francisco Bay via kernels and config/regions. Outputs: CV HTML export, technical appendix JSON, POST /api/passport/generate when the flow qualifies. Employer: CIA pack (JSON + text) after register or sign in (demo accounts in the browser). Programs: GET /api/analytics/regional on the demo store. Also in API: pathways, opportunities search, market signals, directory search (see README table).

## Unique Selling Proposition

Market and region behaviour live in kernels and manifests, not only in scattered code. Risk is adjusted for connectivity instead of treating every context like a high-bandwidth market. Youth-facing copy stays simple; reviewers still get buffer logic, mapping rationale, and credential proof fields. CIA export and passport address partners and systems without claiming a national identity registry in the demo.

## Implementation & Technology

Backend: Python, FastAPI, routes for skills map, risk, verification, passport, profiles, analytics, skill-density, opportunities, market signals, regions. Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite, shared i18n files. Data: data/kernels/*.json, config/regions/. Optional LLM for classification (see deployment env); fallback mapping without it. Tests under backend/tests/. Deploy: typical split of static frontend and hosted API; frontend uses VITE_API_BASE_URL.

## Results & Impact

A working prototype: full guided path from intake to results, CV and appendix, and passport when verification and scoring allow. Three regions and three languages in one codebase. Judges can run the live app, inspect the repo, and trace documented endpoints. Honest scope: demo in-memory profile store; production needs a governed database, stronger partner identity, and kernels fed from operational labour statistics where you have license to use them.

data.title: HumanAPI Lightning
data.summary: HumanAPI Lightning is a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans for tasks they cannot complete alone and pay them instantly through Bitcoin Lightning escrow. Agents create and fund bounties, while humans can receive personalized offers based on their skills, qualifications, location, and availability. Humans apply or accept direct requests, submit work, and get fast global payouts in sats when results are approved.
data.detail:
# HumanAPI Lightning

## Problem & Challenge

AI agents are becoming active economic participants, but today they still cannot easily pay for help or services. Traditional payment systems are too slow, expensive, account-heavy, and not designed for autonomous machine-to-human transactions. At the same time, many tasks still need real human judgment, local presence, creativity, or verification. Our project solves this gap by letting AI agents hire humans directly and pay them instantly through the Lightning Network.

## Target Audience

Our main target users are AI agents, agent developers, businesses using AI workflows, and humans who want to earn money by completing tasks. Agents benefit because they can outsource real-world or human-only work, while humans benefit by getting access to a new source of paid tasks from the agent economy. This is especially useful for freelancers, researchers, photographers, developers, translators, lawyers, designers, and other skilled professionals.

## Solution & Core Features

HumanAPI Lightning is a Lightning-powered marketplace where agents can create bounties, fund them with sats, and release payment when a human completes the task. Core features include human registration with skills and Lightning address, a bounty board, profession-based hiring, direct requests, applications, escrow funding, work submission, approval/rejection, reviews, and automatic payout through Lightning. Agents can use the platform through a web UI, REST API, or MCP server.

## Unique Selling Proposition

What makes our project different is that Lightning is not just added as a payment button; it is the core infrastructure that makes the marketplace possible. Agents can fund escrow instantly, humans can receive global micropayments, and the system supports trust through applications, approval, reviews, and escrow. The MCP integration also makes the product agent-native: AI agents can discover humans, create bounties, fund payments, and approve work directly from their own tool environment. The platform is also easy to monetize through a configurable marketplace fee, for example a 5% fee on completed transactions.

## Implementation & Technology

We implemented the project with Next.js, React, TypeScript, SQLite, JWT/API-key authentication, LNbits for Lightning invoices and payments, Lightning Address/LNURL payouts, and a TypeScript MCP server. The backend exposes API routes for users, humans, bounties, escrow, payments, reviews, direct requests, and platform stats. The MCP server gives AI agents tools such as search_humans, create_bounty, fund_bounty, review_submission, and get_wallet_info, with optional Nostr Wallet Connect support for automatic agent-side payments.

## Results & Impact

We built a functional prototype that demonstrates the full agent-to-human payment flow: an agent creates a task, funds it through Lightning escrow, a human applies and submits work, and the agent approves the result to release payment. This creates a practical bridge between autonomous AI agents and real human labor. The impact is a new way for humans to earn in the agent economy while giving agents access to trusted real-world capabilities that software alone cannot provide.

data.title: Hypothesis→Plan: Real-Data AI Co-Scientist for Experiment Planning
data.summary: Hypothesis→Plan turns a scientific hypothesis into a source-backed experimental plan using live Semantic Scholar literature retrieval, protocol matching, verified material sourcing, lab readiness scoring, risk analysis, and a scientist review loop.
data.detail:
# Hypothesis→Plan: Real-Data AI Co-Scientist for Experiment Planning

## Problem & Challenge

Scientists often spend significant time turning an initial research hypothesis into an executable experimental plan. Generic AI tools can draft protocols, but they often hallucinate sources, fail to show evidence quality, and do not clearly separate live data from fallback assumptions.

Our project addresses this gap by building an AI co-scientist workflow that starts from a hypothesis and produces a structured experimental plan with literature QC, evidence mapping, protocol support, materials, budget, timeline, validation plan, risks, and lab readiness scoring.

## Target Audience

The main users are biomedical researchers, lab teams, early-stage biotech founders, students, and scientific reviewers who need to evaluate whether an experimental hypothesis is feasible before spending time and lab resources.  The demo focuses on cell biology and cryopreservation, but the workflow is designed to generalize to other experimental domains.

## Solution & Core Features

Hypothesis→Plan accepts a scientific hypothesis and generates a complete experiment planning dashboard.

Core features include:
- Live Literature QC using Semantic Scholar
- Evidence map with ranked related papers and source badges
- Protocol matching with transparent fallback when live protocol APIs fail
- Verified supplier/material registry
- Lab Readiness Score
- Timeline, budget, validation plan, and risk analysis
- Scientist Review feedback loop
- Judge Presentation View
- Debug/provenance panel showing which sources were live and which used fallback

The system explicitly labels Live Semantic Scholar data, protocols.io fallback, and verified supplier registry data so judges can see what is real-source-backed.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike a generic AI experiment planner, Hypothesis→Plan does not simply generate text. It separates evidence retrieval from plan generation and shows provenance throughout the UI.  The MVP demonstrates a real-data pipeline: - Semantic Scholar returned 10 live papers for the demo hypothesis - Materials were matched through a verified supplier registry - protocols.io integration is attempted, and when the API returns HTTP 400, the app transparently falls back to curated verified protocols - Lab Readiness Score is reduced when protocol or material verification is incomplete  This makes the tool more trustworthy, auditable, and useful for scientific review.

## Implementation & Technology

The frontend is built with a modern React/TanStack-style architecture, Tailwind UI components, and a dashboard-oriented user experience.

The backend uses server-side API routes for real-data retrieval and orchestration:
- /api/search-literature
- /api/search-protocols
- /api/resolve-materials
- /api/generate-plan

Semantic Scholar is used as the primary live literature source. protocols.io is integrated as the primary protocol source, with transparent curated fallback when the API fails. Materials are resolved through a verified supplier registry.

The app also includes source diagnostics, fallback warnings, source badges, staged loading states, and a real-data pipeline panel.

## Results & Impact

We built a working MVP that turns a hypothesis into a structured experiment plan in under 60 seconds.

For the demo hypothesis about replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant for HeLa cell freezing, the system:
- Retrieved 10 live papers from Semantic Scholar
- Generated Literature QC and evidence mapping
- Matched 8 required materials through a verified supplier registry
- Produced a protocol, validation plan, timeline, risks, and budget
- Displayed a Lab Readiness Score of 87/100 with review-needed flags
- Transparently showed that protocols.io returned HTTP 400 and curated fallback protocols were used

The impact is a more transparent AI co-scientist workflow that helps researchers move from hypothesis to reviewed experimental plan faster, while showing where human review is still required.

data.title: Green Crisis Grid AI — Autonomous Disaster Response & Energy Optimization System
data.summary: An AI-powered crisis command system that combines real-time disaster prediction, NDMA intelligence retrieval (RAG), hospital prioritization, and autonomous energy allocation to generate government-level emergency response plans.
data.detail:
# Green Crisis Grid AI — Autonomous Disaster Response & Energy Optimization System

## Problem & Challenge

Pakistan and similar regions face a major gap in real-time disaster coordination. During floods, heatwaves, and earthquakes, critical decisions are delayed due to fragmented data sources, lack of AI-driven prediction, and slow resource allocation. Emergency response systems are reactive instead of proactive.

## Target Audience

National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), Emergency response teams, Government crisis units, Hospitals and healthcare systems, Urban planning authorities

## Solution & Core Features

Green Crisis Grid AI solves this by creating an autonomous crisis command center that:

Detects disaster severity using live weather APIs
Uses RAG (Pinecone + NDMA data scraping) for real-time intelligence
Ranks hospitals based on geographic proximity
Allocates emergency energy resources dynamically
Generates structured government-level reports using LLaMA 3 (Together AI)
Visualizes crisis zones using interactive maps (PyDeck)

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional dashboards, this system is:  Fully autonomous (no manual decision-making required), Combines AI + real-time government data (NDMA scraping), Uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for grounded responses, Includes energy redistribution simulation (rare in disaster systems), Produces structured executive-level crisis reports automatically

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend: Streamlit (interactive dashboard + UI tabs)
Backend Logic: Python
AI Model: Meta LLaMA 3.3 70B via Together AI API
Embeddings: SentenceTransformers (BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5)
Vector DB: Pinecone (NDMA knowledge base)
APIs: Open-Meteo (weather forecasting), USGS Earthquake API
Visualization: PyDeck (3D crisis map)
Data Pipeline: BeautifulSoup scraping (NDMA dataset ingestion)

## Results & Impact

Generates real-time crisis response plans in <5 seconds
Improves disaster decision-making speed significantly
Helps prioritize hospitals and critical infrastructure
Enables AI-driven energy redistribution during emergencies
Demonstrates scalable national-level disaster intelligence system

Embeddings: SentenceTransformers (BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5)
Vector DB: Pinecone (NDMA knowledge base)
APIs: Open-Meteo (weather forecasting), USGS Earthquake API
Visualization: PyDeck (3D crisis map)
Data Pipeline: BeautifulSoup scraping (NDMA dataset ingestion)
structured.targetAudience: National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), Emergency response teams, Government crisis units, Hospitals and healthcare systems, Urban planning authorities

data.title: Evidentia
data.summary: Evidentia is an agentic AI system that navigates 10,000+ messy Indian healthcare facility records to help patients find verified, life-saving care and shows policymakers exactly where the gaps are.
data.detail:
# Evidentia

## Problem & Challenge

70% of India's population lives in rural areas where healthcare is fragmented and hard to find. Patients travel hours to reach facilities that may not exist, have dead websites, or lack the specific service they need. The crisis isn't just a lack of hospitals it's a discovery and coordination failure affecting an estimated 52 million people in high-urgency regions.

## Target Audience

Rural patients in India who need to locate verified healthcare quickly, urban users seeking specialist care, NGOs coordinating healthcare access, and government policymakers identifying where intervention is most urgent.

## Solution & Core Features

Evidentia is an agentic healthcare intelligence system with three core features. First, a natural language search users type or speak their healthcare need and receive ranked, verified provider results. Second, an automated data validation pipeline that processes 10,000+ facility records, probes links for legitimacy, and assigns each facility a confidence score. Third, the Desert Map  a population-weighted urgency visualisation across 3,686 PIN codes that shows policymakers exactly where healthcare gaps are most critical.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike static healthcare directories, Evidentia reasons about data quality at query time. It doesn't just find hospitals it validates them, scores their trustworthiness, and weights urgency by population density. No existing solution combines agentic validation, semantic search, and population-weighted gap analysis in one system.

## Implementation & Technology

Built with a Python backend running an agentic validation pipeline across 10,000+ facility records. Facility embeddings are indexed for semantic search, enabling natural language queries to match against unstructured data. Website validation is powered by Tavily Extract, which inspects each facility's website for red flags including placeholder images, fake reviews, and location mismatches. The Desert Map uses hexagonal binning with population-weighted urgency scoring across Indian PIN codes. The frontend is built in React with voice input supported by an xAI voice agent.

## Results & Impact

Evidentia has analysed 3,686 PIN codes across India, identifying 1,093 high-urgency regions representing approximately 52 million people without reliable access to essential services including oncology, emergency care, trauma, and dialysis. The system reduces discovery-to-care time by surfacing only verified, confidence-scored providers ensuring no family travels hours to a facility that doesn't exist.

data.title: AI_Scientist_
data.summary:
An AI-powered planning engine that transforms scientific hypotheses into lab-actionable blueprints, featuring real-time novelty detection and live-priced supply chain manifests.

It integrates a dynamic parameter simulator, allowing researchers to optimize for cost, time, and reproducibility while using a closed-loop memory system that learns from every manual edit.
data.detail:
# AI_Scientist_

## Problem & Challenge

Scientific planning and operations slow down science and act as bottlenecks. Researchers spend weeks on literature reviews, vendor price comparisons, and protocol drafting before the experiment starts. This delays breakthroughs and costs lives in time-sensitive development, like with the COVID-19 vaccine.

## Target Audience

Academic Researchers, Lab Managers

## Solution & Core Features

The AI Scientist is a planning engine that generates full-stack experiment blueprints from a single natural language prompt.

Multi-Dimensional Parameter Tuning: A dynamic control panel allowing researchers to toggle variables like Vendor Selection, Automation Level, and Sample Size to vary with budget needs, reproducibility, and timeline.

Novelty Signal: Real-time cross-referencing with Semantic Scholar to determine if work is original.

Live-Priced Materials: Integrated market search (Tavily) that finds real catalog numbers and current prices.

7-Tab Dashboard: A modular UI covering Summary, Protocol, Materials, Budget, Timeline, Literature, and Expert Memory.

## Unique Selling Proposition

The parameters simulator. By adjusting the "Automation" or "Sample Size" parameters, researchers can see the immediate trade-offs in their budget and reproducibility score. Plus the AI Scientist includes "Expert Memory" to every manual edit made to the plan is summarized and stored in a closed-loop system.

## Implementation & Technology

Orchestration: Node.js/TypeScript backend managing Azure GPT-5.1-Codex with a 272k token context window.

Data Layer: Multi-agent retrieval using Tavily (Web/Market) and Semantic Scholar (Academic).

Optimization: Custom token-pruning logic to handle massive research datasets (400k+ tokens) without exceeding model limits.

Frontend: Built with React/Vite/Tailwind, featuring a native CSS-Print engine for vector-clear PDF exports.

## Results & Impact

The AI Scientist reduces the Hypothesis-to-Plan cycle from 14 days to 2 minutes. The result is accompanied by sources and a lab-actionable plan that covers everything from the first reagent purchase to the final data analysis step. Using the parameter variance tab, a researcher has much more autonomy than in a static report. For example, it can determine within seconds whether they have the funding for a high-automation study or need to scale back sample sizes to fit a grant limit. This eliminates the "Trial-and-Error" approach to lab procurement and logistics.

data.title: AutoNomics
data.summary: AutoNomics is an L402-native marketplace where autonomous agents discover paid APIs, pay per request over Lightning, and unlock results with payment proof. The demo shows a buyer agent paying a provider agent for URL verification, with the full flow visible: discovery, invoice, payment, proof, response, and provider revenue.
data.detail:
# AutoNomics

## Problem & Challenge

Problem: AI agents can make decisions and call APIs, but they still lack a native way to discover, pay for, and trust external services on demand. Today, most paid APIs require accounts, API keys, subscriptions, and human-operated billing.

Challenge: Build the commerce layer for autonomous agents: a system where agents can buy services like data, verification, summarization, or compute per request, pay instantly with Lightning/L402, and prove payment before unlocking results.

## Solution & Core Features

arget Audience: Developers and teams building autonomous agents that need to buy external capabilities on demand, plus API/service providers who want to monetize agent-facing services per request. This includes AI agent builders, data providers, verification services, compute providers, and marketplaces for machine-to-machine commerce.

## Implementation & Technology

USP: AutoNomics turns paid APIs into agent-native services: agents can discover a provider, receive an L402 invoice, pay over Lightning, and unlock results with cryptographic payment proof in a single automated flow. Unlike traditional API marketplaces, it removes accounts, subscriptions, and manual billing, enabling true pay-per-request commerce between autonomous agents.

## Results & Impact

AutoNomics demonstrates a working agent-to-agent commerce flow: service discovery, HTTP 402 payment challenge, Lightning-style settlement, payment proof, unlocked API response, and provider revenue tracking. It shows how agents can pay for data, verification, summarization, or compute per request, creating a foundation for open marketplaces where useful agent services can earn instantly without accounts, API keys, or manual billing.

2 billion people work in the informal economy (street vendors, day laborers, domestic workers, etc.) with real, transferable skills — but zero formal proof of them. data.title: Aarogya Atlas — agentic healthcare intelligence for 1.4B people
data.summary: Trust-scored, cost-aware, multilingual healthcare facility recommender for India. A 12-tool agent (Claude Opus 4.7, parallel fan-out, on-device PHI extraction via Qwen 2.5 32B) ranks the Virtue Foundation's 10,000-facility dataset by Trust Score + total ₹ cost + travel time, surfaces source contradictions, and validates each recommendation. Live in Databricks (Mosaic AI VS, Genie, UC mask UDF, MLflow per-tool spans). EN · हिंदी · தமிழ். 0 errors across 12 audited queries.
data.detail:
# Aarogya Atlas — agentic healthcare intelligence for 1.4B people

## Problem & Challenge

In rural India, a postal code can decide a lifespan. Families load into a bus at 5 AM and travel three hours only to learn the dialysis machine broke yesterday or the brochure's Ayushman Bharat (national insurance) coverage was outdated. The Virtue Foundation's 10,000-facility India dataset is the most comprehensive open registry — but it's messy, contradictory, and unsearchable for a non-expert. Existing tools (Google Maps, ChatGPT) return distance and generic suggestions. Neither catches a clinic that lists "Advanced Surgery" with no anesthesiologist on staff. Neither computes the real cost — bus fare + lost MGNREGA wage + treatment. Neither speaks Hindi or Tamil. Neither tells you the words to say to the receptionist. Indian healthcare's Discovery-to-Care time is the friction we attack.

## Target Audience

ASHA (Accredited Social Health Activist) workers, NGO planners (Virtue Foundation, World Bank field offices), clinic coordinators, and family members helping a relative navigate India's healthcare system. Secondary: state-mission policy makers using the equity-counterfactual planner.

## Solution & Core Features

A streaming agent (Claude Opus 4.7 with adaptive thinking, manual tool-use loop, parallel asyncio.gather fan-out — 22% wall-clock reduction) coordinates 12 tools to answer healthcare-discovery queries:

• geocode (Nominatim) → facility_search (Postgres + Haversine) → check_hours / status_feed
• Trust Scorer with 7 contradiction rules + 4 metadata signals + 80% bootstrap CI + cited evidence
• Validator agent (PASS/WARN/FAIL with source-text snippet)
• Cost ranker: total ₹ = treatment + KSRTC bus + auto-rickshaw + MGNREGA wage-loss
• On-device PHI extraction (Qwen 2.5 32B + bge-m3 multilingual via Ollama) — patient text never leaves the device
• On-device multimodal triage (medgemma 27B vision) — upload a wound photo, prescription, X-ray, or oxygen-cylinder gauge; ~4s structured triage with severity + recommended specialty
• Mosaic AI Vector Search (Databricks Delta Sync Index)
• find_medical_deserts — district-level coverage gaps with severity scoring
• Clinical-pathway routing: obstetric_emergency → CEmONC; snakebite → polyvalent + species coverage; neonatal → SNCU/NICU; STEMI → cathlab+thrombolytics

Output: 3-tier answer card (⭐ Best · 📍 Closest payer-eligible · 💡 Backup) with the exact words to ask the receptionist, in the user's language.

Five public routes: /, /compare (vs ChatGPT/Maps 14/0/0), /equity (disparate-impact + counterfactual planner), /architecture (interactive SVG), /eval (live audit).

## Unique Selling Proposition

Five things no other Challenge-3 entry shipped: (1) Multimodal on-device triage (medgemma 27B vision) — wound photo / X-ray / snake-bite / prescription gives a structured triage in ~4s with severity + recommended specialty + rationale; PHI never touches the cloud. (2) Trust Scorer with 80% bootstrap CI + Validator self-check that catches the spec's named contradiction (advanced surgery, no anesthesia). (3) Drive-time isochrones rendered as 3 saffron rings (15/30/60 min) around the top recommendation; multi-modal travel always shows auto / public bus / 108 ambulance (free). (4) Equity counterfactual planner: "add N CEmONC beds in {district} → averted maternal deaths/yr" via gravity model + Six-Delays attribution. (5) DAS-style adversarial robustness eval reporting 100% static / 100% dynamic / 0% gap after the no-location fallback fix. Plus a bespoke design system (Instrument Serif + Tiro Devanagari Hindi + saffron palette + Bloomberg-style mono tickers + ⌘K command palette) that the AI-template-aesthetic majority of the field cannot match. Mosaic AI Vector Search verified end-to-end while many Databricks-track teams in #challenge-03-databricks remain blocked on it.

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend: Next.js 16 + React 19 + MapLibre GL (clusters + heatmap + 3D buildings + ranked DOM pins + OSRM routes + pulsing critical-district halos) + framer-motion + react-countup. 5 routes, mobile-responsive, OG card.

Backend: FastAPI + official Anthropic SDK + Ollama wrapper. Manual streaming agent loop in apps/api/aarogya_api/agent.py — no LangGraph, no LangChain. Thinking-signature blocks preserved across turns. Server-Sent Events to the frontend. Parallel asyncio.gather over tool_use blocks.

Data: Postgres 17 + pgvector for local Lakebase analog. Schema is FHIR R4 (Location + HealthcareService + intake_note + status_event).

Databricks (workspace dbc-12ce3b55, all live):
• Unity Catalog: workspace.aarogya.facilities (10k Delta), workspace.aarogya_raw.intake_notes (PHI-mask UDF on patient_phone column), workspace.aarogya_curated.facility_capability_summary
• Genie Space (NL→SQL verified)
• MLflow 3 Tracing at /Shared/aarogya-atlas — 23 traces, per-tool spans
• Mosaic AI Vector Search: endpoint aarogya_vs + Delta Sync Index workspace.aarogya.facilities_idx with managed databricks-bge-large-en embeddings — verified returning ranked results

Local LLM: Qwen 2.5 32B (capability extraction) + bge-m3 (multilingual embeddings, EN/HI/TA) via Ollama on M-series Mac.

Deployment: Cloudflare Tunnel (frontend + backend) — judges click and use immediately, no browser-warning interstitial.

Eval: scripts/evaluate.py (12 fixed queries) + scripts/evaluate_robustness.py (DAS-style adversarial perturbation, static-vs-dynamic gap).

## Results & Impact

Latest auditable evaluation (12 queries, mixed languages, 0 human review):
• Mean wall-clock 31.7s (P95 49.5s) — 22% faster after parallel tool fan-out vs the sequential baseline
• 0 errors across 12 queries spanning EN/HI/TA, NGO-planner, patient, trust-scoring, desert-detection, edge-case profiles
• 11 of 12 tools invoked across the run; Validator verdicts 2 PASS · 4 WARN · 0 FAIL
• 33% of answers include a callable next-step (phone number / 104 helpline)

Aarogya vs ChatGPT vs Google Maps on the same query, scored on 14 healthcare-specific capabilities the spec asks for: Aarogya 14/14 · ChatGPT 0/14 · Google Maps 0/14.

Equity audit publishes our own disparate-impact ratios across 25 Indian states: ICU 7.7× · Dialysis 7.0× · Neonatal 5.4×. Counterfactual planner shows that adding 10 CEmONC beds in Patna would avert ~70 maternal deaths/year.

For the user — an ECG query in Bengaluru returns three tier-ranked facilities in 6 tool calls, with ₹484 total cost broken down (treatment + transport + wage-loss), Trust Score 65/100 [CI 50-95], a Validator WARN flag, and the Hindi sentence to ask the receptionist. The map shows numbered ranked pins, the OSRM driving route, and the medical-desert overlay with red severity halos.
techStack: Next.js React TypeScript MapLibre GL FastAPI Python Anthropic Claude Claude Opus 4.7 Ollama Qwen 2.5 32B medgemma bge-m3 Postgres pgvector Databricks Unity Catalog Mosaic AI Vector Search MLflow Genie FHIR R4 Cloudflare Tunnel Server-Sent Events
tags: agentic-AI healthcare India multilingual Hindi Tamil on-device-LLM multimodal vision medical-imaging RAG vector-search trust-scoring validator equity-audit parallel-tool-use adaptive-thinking PHI-safe ASHA NGO hackathon solo-build

data.title: searchRE
data.summary: searchRE turns a research question into a lab-ready experiment plan in minutes. Powered by AI, it searches the literature, checks for novelty, and generates a complete protocol — with budget, timeline, and bill of materials — all in an interactive workspace your team can edit and ship from. Science moves faster when the planning takes care of itself.
data.detail:
# searchRE

## Problem & Challenge

Scientific research moves slowly — not because scientists lack ideas, but because turning a question into an actionable experiment plan is grueling, manual work. A researcher can spend weeks combing through literature, checking for novelty, sourcing protocols, building timelines, and estimating budgets before a single experiment begins. This overhead is the hidden tax on scientific progress. Labs waste time, grants are delayed, and promising ideas die in the planning phase. The bottleneck isn't intelligence — it's infrastructure.

## Target Audience

searchRE is built for academic researchers, PhD students, and lab teams who regularly design experiments — particularly in life sciences, biomedical research, and applied sciences. Secondary audiences include research institutions and universities looking to accelerate output, and biotech startups that need to move fast from hypothesis to execution. Anyone who has stared at a blank document wondering how to turn a research question into a protocol is the target user.

## Solution & Core Features

searchRE compresses weeks of planning into minutes. A researcher enters a scientific question — searchRE handles the rest:

Literature QC — automatically searches and evaluates relevant existing work to ground the plan in prior art
Novelty detection — signals whether the question is sufficiently differentiated from what's already been published
Protocol retrieval — pulls reference protocols from external sources and incorporates them into the plan
AI-generated experiment plan — produces a complete, structured plan including overview, methodology, and rationale
Interactive workspace — an editable, Notion-style document with dedicated tabs for Protocol, Bill of Materials, Budget, Timeline, and Team
AI copilot chat — a real-time assistant embedded in the workspace that answers questions and proposes section-level edits on the fly

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most AI tools give you text. searchRE gives you a lab-ready plan. Unlike general-purpose LLM assistants, searchRE is purpose-built for the scientific workflow — it doesn't just write prose, it generates a structured, multi-tab document that a real lab can pick up and execute. The combination of literature grounding, novelty checking, protocol sourcing, and structured output in a single tool has no direct equivalent. searchRE doesn't replace the scientist — it eliminates the grunt work so the scientist can focus on the science.

## Implementation & Technology

searchRE is a full-stack web application built for speed, reliability, and collaborative use:

Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript, Vite 5, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui — a clean, responsive SPA with progressive rendering via SSE streaming
Backend: Node.js + Express 5 — a lightweight orchestration layer handling API calls, retry logic, and two-phase streaming (overview renders in ~15s; full structured data in ~55s)
AI engine: Anthropic's Claude API — claude-sonnet-4-6 for deep plan generation (up to 16,384 tokens), claude-haiku for the fast copilot chat
Architecture: Feature-module codebase (input-api, plan-engine, workspace-ui) enabling parallel team development with minimal conflicts
Resilience: 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff on AI responses, ensuring stability even under flaky model outputs

## Results & Impact

searchRE turns a process that used to take days or weeks into one that takes minutes. For the individual researcher, this means less time buried in planning overhead and more time doing actual science. At scale, it means faster grant cycles, faster publications, and a lower barrier to entry for early-career researchers who lack the institutional knowledge to design rigorous experiments from scratch. The long-term vision is clear: if AI can close the gap between scientific curiosity and experimental execution, the pace of discovery accelerates for everyone.

data.title: ClashOfAgents
data.summary: ClashOfAgents is an agent-first task competition platform where AI agents compete on funded tasks and receive Bitcoin Lightning payouts based on performance.
data.detail:
# ClashOfAgents

## Problem & Challenge

Current autonomous AI workflows can generate useful work, but there is no simple, trusted way for agents to:
- Compete on real tasks,
- Be judged in a transparent flow,
- And get paid instantly in native internet money.

Most existing freelancing/task tools are human-first and slow for agent-driven execution. Payments are often manual, delayed, or disconnected from autonomous task orchestration.  
Our project solves this by creating an agent-first competition and payout loop with Bitcoin Lightning.

## Target Audience

Primary target groups: - *AI agents / agent operators* who want to discover tasks, submit work, and earn sats.- *Task posters* (builders, teams, hackathon operators) who want fast execution on digital tasks.   Secondary beneficiaries: - Ecosystem contributors who need a reproducible runtime to test automation loops. - Communities that want transparent, gamified agent leaderboards.

## Solution & Core Features

We built *ClashOfAgents*, a competitive task arena with payment-integrated automation.

Core functionalities:
- Create tasks with reward amounts in sats.
- Run task lifecycle states (draft -> funded -> competing -> voting -> completed -> paid).
- Register agents and allow submissions/voting workflows.
- Select winners and distribute payouts.
- Track leaderboard metrics and agent performance.
- Demo orchestration endpoints to run full end-to-end flows quickly.
- UI dashboard + pages for tasks, leaderboard, agent profile, task details, and demo mode.
- Optional runtime (openclaw-runtime) for manual/automation agent control via chat-like interface.

## Unique Selling Proposition

What makes this project different: - *Agent-first competition model* instead of generic marketplace CRUD. - *Lightning-native payout flow* designed for machine-speed value transfer. - *End-to-end demo-ready architecture* (task creation, funding, execution, judging, payout, leaderboard). - *Dual experience*:   - Main product UI for arena management,   - Runtime UI for direct agent automation control. - Strong balance between hackathon practicality (fast demo flow) and extensible architecture.

## Implementation & Technology

- *Frontend + API layer:* Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript.
- *Styling/UI:* Tailwind-based styling with custom premium design system.
- *Backend pattern:* Next.js route handlers (/api/v1/*) + shared service layer.
- *Data/persistence:* Supabase-backed storage and schema.
- *Payments:* Mock + Lightning-oriented provider abstractions (MDK-compatible paths).
- *Automation runtime:* Separate openclaw-runtime Next.js app for agent operations and diagnostics.
- *Architecture:* Contract-based endpoint design with modular data store and payment provider boundaries.

## Results & Impact

Current project impact:
- Functional MVP that demonstrates a full agent economy loop from task posting to payout.
- Clear UX for monitoring tasks, agents, and leaderboard performance.
- Faster experimentation for agent orchestration through the runtime control page.
- A practical foundation for scaling autonomous paid task markets with transparent competition mechanics.

data.title: Operon - From Hypothesis to Executable Workflow
data.summary: Operon - From Hypothesis to personalized executable workflow, step-by-step grounded in authoritative protocols.
data.detail:
# Operon - From Hypothesis to Executable Workflow

## Problem & Challenge

Scientists do not struggle to generate ideas, but to translate them into executable, operationally realistic experiment plans. This process requires identifying the closest existing SOPs, adapting procedures, selecting methods, estimating materials and cost, and sequencing execution steps. Today, this is slow, error-prone, and dependent on individual experience, leading to wasted time, inconsistent results, and outdated lab protocols.

## Target Audience

Our primary users are scientists and researchers in academic and industrial labs who need to design and execute experiments. This includes graduate students, research scientists, and lab managers who rely on SOPs, protocols, and prior runs to operationalize new hypotheses.

## Solution & Core Features

Operon is an AI lab operations engine that converts a scientific hypothesis into a structured, executable workflow. It retrieves internal SOPs, runbooks, and prior experiments using vector embeddings, and external protocols and literature using Tavily. It compiles workflows with labeled steps (exact reuse, adapted, decision-required), generates materials, budget, timeline, validation, and risks, and surfaces uncertainty as explicit decision nodes. Scientists select branches, add reasoning, and the system dynamically recompiles the downstream plan. It also tracks execution and stores feedback to improve future workflows.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike AI tools that generate static experiment plans, Operon maps hypotheses onto real lab SOPs and turns protocols into interactive, stateful workflows. It explicitly separates deterministic execution from scientific uncertainty, forces decisions to be structured and traceable, and continuously improves SOPs based on real usage. The system does not hallucinate protocols — it compiles from real sources and evolves over time.

## Implementation & Technology

Operon uses a SvelteKit frontend deployed on Vercel and a FastAPI backend. Internal lab documents are embedded locally using sentence-transformers and stored in a Chroma vector store. External protocols and literature are retrieved using Tavily and then embedded for reuse. A multi-stage LLM pipeline handles intent extraction, protocol compilation, uncertainty classification, decision generation, and plan synthesis. Workflows are stored as structured objects with steps, decision nodes, plan sections, and execution traces.

## Results & Impact

We built a working system that retrieves real protocol sources, compiles structured workflows, supports interactive decision-making, dynamically updates experiment plans, and tracks execution and feedback. This enables scientists to move from hypothesis to executable plan significantly faster while reducing operational errors and improving the quality of SOPs over time.

The system does not hallucinate protocols — it compiles from real sources and evolves over time.

data.title: Agentic Healthcare Maps Projects
data.summary: AI agent that turns messy hospital records (PDFs, Excel, handwritten notes) into a living intelligence network. Patients describe symptoms in Hindi or English. AI recommends nearest hospital with available beds, specialist doctors, and room numbers. Hospitals update availability via secure dashboard. Zero cost using Google Gemini + Tavily API.
data.detail:
# Agentic Healthcare Maps Projects

## Problem & Challenge

In India, a postal code can determine a lifespan. Families waste critical golden hour time hopping between hospitals because there is no centralized healthcare directory. Hospital records exist as messy PDFs, Excel files, and handwritten registers. No one knows which hospital has the right specialist, available ICU beds, or which doctor is on duty and in which room. The challenge was to build an AI agent that turns 10,000 messy hospital records into a living intelligence network so no family ever travels hours only to find help isn't there.

## Target Audience

Primary target is patients and families in India seeking emergency care, especially rural patients who speak Hindi. Secondary target is hospital administrators who need a professional way to update bed availability and doctor room assignments without using WhatsApp. Tertiary target is public health officials and NGOs who need transparency into healthcare capacity for epidemic response.

## Solution & Core Features

Agentic Healthcare Maps provides symptom triage where users describe symptoms in Hindi or English. Google Gemini extracts medical specialty and urgency with confidence scores. Tavily API returns real-time citations from PubMed, WHO, and Mayo Clinic. Deterministic fallback rules handle cases when APIs are unavailable. The system currently has working authentication with JWT, user registration and login, role-based access for admin and hospital staff, and a fully functional triage endpoint that returns specialty, urgency, rationale, and citations. Database models for hospitals, users, specialties, and availability logs are complete with Alembic migrations. The frontend has React with Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, React Hook Form with Zod validation, and a working triage form connected to the backend. Pending implementation includes hospital search ranking with weighted scoring formula, hospital admin dashboard endpoints for bed updates, WebSocket real-time updates, Leaflet map integration, and hospital recommendation cards.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Zero cost using Google Gemini free tier and Tavily hackathon credits - no expensive GPT-4 or Claude. Anti-hallucination design where fallback rules and citation-only responses prevent invented information. Multilingual support for Hindi keywords like सीने and सांस. Deterministic fallback ensures demo works even without API keys. Professional web dashboard approach with no WhatsApp or SMS shortcuts. Complete audit trail with availability logs. Production-ready infrastructure with Docker, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Alembic migrations.

## Implementation & Technology

Backend uses FastAPI with Python 3.11, PostgreSQL database, SQLAlchemy ORM, Alembic migrations, JWT authentication with bcrypt, and Redis for caching. AI uses Google Gemini 1.5 Flash free tier via direct REST calls with few-shot prompting enforcing JSON output, Tavily API for medical search returning citation titles and URLs, and deterministic regex fallback for chest pain, stroke, trauma, fever, and Hindi keywords. Frontend uses React 18 with Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, React Hook Form with Zod validation, and Axios for API calls. Infrastructure includes Docker Compose with PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7 containers, pytest with pytest-asyncio for testing, and Ruff and Black for linting. Current working endpoints include health check, auth register, auth login, auth me, and patient triage.

## Results & Impact

Currently demoable features include user registration with bcrypt password hashing, JWT authentication returning valid access tokens, role-based user info retrieval, and symptom triage that returns specialty (cardiology, neurology, general medicine), urgency (normal, urgent, emergency), confidence score, short rationale, and medical citations from Tavily or empty citations if unavailable. The triage endpoint works without any API keys using fallback rules and handles Hindi keywords. Infrastructure is production-ready with Docker, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Alembic migrations. Testing includes triage fallback tests and API integration tests. The system is extensible for hospital search ranking, WebSocket real-time updates, and Leaflet map visualization in Phase 1 and Phase 2 implementation.

Anti-hallucination design where fallback rules and citation-only responses prevent invented information. Multilingual support for Hindi keywords like सीने and सांस. Deterministic fallback ensures demo works even without API keys. Professional web dashboard approach with no WhatsApp or SMS shortcuts. Complete audit trail with availability logs. Production-ready infrastructure with Docker, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Alembic migrations.

data.title: Sawt-net
data.summary: Sawt-Net is a voice-first platform that transforms informal workers’ spoken experiences into structured, professional CVs using AI and real labor data. It maps real-world skills to ISCO-08 standards, evaluates automation risk using trusted economic datasets, and connects users to job opportunities instantly. Built as a low-bandwidth, country-agnostic infrastructure, it also provides policymakers with real-time insights into informal labor markets and AI exposure.
data.detail:
# Sawt-net

## Problem & Challenge

There are over 600 million young people in LMICs — like Amara, the self-taught phone repair technician from the World Bank brief — who have real, valuable skills but remain completely invisible to the formal economy. Existing job platforms assume a typed résumé, formal credentials, and English proficiency. They ignore the informal-skill blind spot  and the automation-exposure blind spot. As AI accelerates, these youth are at risk of losing their livelihoods without ever knowing why. We are not facing a talent shortage; we are facing a matching and signaling crisis.

## Target Audience

●Primary (The Unmapped Youth): Young people (16–30) in the informal economy (repair, retail, agriculture, trades) with low literacy, operating on low-bandwidth 3G connections. ●Secondary (Policymakers & NGOs): Labor ministries operating with massive blind spots regarding the informal sector. We replace delayed, multi-year surveys with a live labor market dashboard, giving them real-time visibility into grassroots skill supply and its vulnerability to AI displacement.

## Solution & Core Features

Sawt-Net is not an app; it is a localized, country-agnostic infrastructure layer built to close the distance between informal skills and economic opportunity. We built the exact three modules required:
●Module 1: The Skills Signal Engine (Voice-to-CV): A strictly Voice-First PWA (Web Speech API + Edge TTS). A user simply speaks in their native dialect (Darija, Hindi, etc.). Our edge functions extract their spoken stories and map them directly to a standardized, portable ISCO-08/ESCO profile. It then instantly generates a professionally formatted, downloadable PDF CV based purely on that voice conversation.
●Module 2: AI Readiness & Displacement Risk Lens: Every extracted skill is instantly scored against the Frey & Osborne (2017) dataset to show automation probability, paired with Wittgenstein Centre (2025-2035) education projections to highlight regional workforce trajectories.
●Module 3: Opportunity Matching & Policymaker Dashboard: For youth, we surface ILOSTAT median wages for their ISCO codes and generate an AI-powered "Smart Apply" cover letter. For governments, our /policy route provides an anonymized, aggregate dashboard of skill supply vs. automation risk.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Sawt-Net replaces LLM hallucinations with real econometric evidence. We are the only platform that combines Zero-Click Voice-to-CV Generation (designed for illiterate/low-literacy users), Offline-Resilient UX (graceful audio-visual fallbacks for bad connections), and Zero-Code Scalability. Adding a new country is a single row in country_configs—proving this is a true, plug-and-play global infrastructure, not just a localized app.

## Implementation & Technology

●Frontend: React 19, TanStack Start (SSR), TanStack Router, TanStack Query, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion, OGL (WebGL starfield), and Recharts.
●Voice Engine: Native Web Speech API (for free, offline-resilient recognition) + custom TTS edge function.
●Backend: Lovable Cloud (Supabase) — Postgres with RLS, Auth (email + Google), Storage, and Deno Edge Functions (analyze-skills, chat-followup, cover-letter, tts).
●AI Engine: Lovable AI Gateway (Google Gemini / GPT-4o) exclusively for skill extraction and cover letter generation (no data hallucination).
●Data Layer (Edge Hosted): Embedded JSON datasets (frey-osborne.json, ilo-wages.json, wittgenstein-2035.json) + ESCO ↔ ISCO-08 crosswalk to guarantee 0ms latency and factual accuracy.
●Deployment: Cloudflare Workers (Edge SSR) via @cloudflare/vite-plugin + wrangler for instant global loads.
●Security & Roles: Strict Row-Level Security (RLS) on every table, separate user_roles table with has_role() security-definer function, and fully role-gated /admin/configs and /policy routes.

## Results & Impact

●For Youth: Turns a 60-second voice note into a globally recognized, downloadable PDF CV, an ISCO-coded portable profile, and an AI-generated cover letter, empowering them to transition to the formal economy instantly.
●For Policymakers: Replaces outdated, multi-year labor surveys with a live pulse of the informal economy's exposure to AI.
●Direct SDG Alignment: Drives immediate action on SDG 8 (Decent Work), SDG 4 (Quality Education), and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities).

structured.targetAudience: ●Primary (The Unmapped Youth): Young people (16–30) in the informal economy (repair, retail, agriculture, trades) with low literacy, operating on low-bandwidth 3G connections. ●Secondary (Policymakers & NGOs): Labor ministries operating with massive blind spots regarding the informal sector. We replace delayed, multi-year surveys with a live labor market dashboard, giving them real-time visibility into grassroots skill supply and its vulnerability to AI displacement.

data.title: LabPlan AI
data.summary: Multi-agent AI system that converts scientific hypotheses into structured, runnable experiment plans — including protocol design, materials sourcing, cost estimation, and execution timelines.
data.detail:
# LabPlan AI

## Problem & Challenge

Turning a scientific idea into a real experiment takes a lot of time and experience.
Researchers need to design protocols, choose materials, estimate costs, and plan timelines — and small mistakes can ruin the whole experiment.

The main problem is going from a high-level hypothesis to a complete, realistic experiment plan.

## Target Audience

Scientists, Researches, Students.

## Solution & Core Features

We built a multi-step AI system that converts a scientific hypothesis into a runnable experiment plan. It first performs a literature quality check to determine whether similar work already exists and surfaces relevant references. Then, it generates a structured experimental design, including a step-by-step protocol, appropriate controls, replicates, and quality control checks. This is followed by an operations layer that turns the design into something executable by adding required materials, estimated costs, and a realistic timeline. A validation layer ensures the plan is internally consistent and feasible. Finally, the system includes a feedback memory component that stores scientist corrections and reuses them in future generations to improve output quality.

## Unique Selling Proposition

This system goes beyond idea generation by producing complete, runnable experiment plans and improving over time through scientist feedback.

## Implementation & Technology

The system uses a React frontend deployed on Vercel and a FastAPI backend hosted on Railway, with Supabase as the database for storing structured feedback. It is built as a multi-stage AI pipeline that separates literature checking, scientific design, and operational planning. The model outputs are constrained to structured JSON, which allows reliable parsing and post-processing. Additional Python logic is used to validate outputs and calculate budgets deterministically. A retrieval-based memory system ranks and injects relevant past feedback into new generations, enabling the system to improve over time.

## Results & Impact

We built a working end-to-end system that can take a scientific hypothesis and generate a complete, structured experiment plan. The output includes protocol steps, materials, cost estimates, and execution timelines, making it directly useful for real-world lab planning. The system also introduces a feedback loop where scientist corrections are stored and reused, allowing it to improve over time. Overall, it reduces the time and effort required to move from idea to experiment and helps make experiment design more consistent and accessible.

data.title: MediFind
data.detail:
# MediFind

## Problem & Challenge

In rural India, 70% of people don't know which hospital can actually treat their emergency. Patients travel hours only to find facilities lack ICU, ventilators, or specialists. Google Maps shows locations but not real capabilities. People die not from lack of hospitals, but from lack of truthful information.

## Target Audience

Rural Indian families needing emergency care, NGO planners identifying medical deserts, and policymakers allocating healthcare resources across 10,000+ facilities.

## Solution & Core Features

4-agent AI system that reads messy hospital notes, flags contradictions, calculates trust scores (0-100%), and recommends verified facilities. Plus: voice in 10 Indian languages, camera injury detection with animated first aid, medical desert mapping, and equipment verification via Gemini Vision.

## Unique Selling Proposition

No other platform reads unstructured hospital notes to catch lies like "claims ICU but no intensivist." Transparent truth gaps, self-correcting agents, and offline-first design make MediFind uniquely trustworthy for life-or-death decisions.

## Implementation & Technology

React + TypeScript frontend, Groq Llama 3.3 70B for clinical reasoning, Gemini Vision for equipment/injury analysis, RAG with Transformers.js embeddings, Leaflet maps, Web Speech API for 10 languages.

## Results & Impact

Processed 10,000 real Indian hospitals, identified only 98 as truly emergency-capable, and mapped medical deserts across 28 states. Delivers verified hospital recommendations in under 5 seconds with transparent trust scores.

data.title: MediSync AI
data.summary: An AI-powered healthcare dashboard that unifies fragmented medical records into a single, intelligent patient timeline for faster clinical decision-making.
data.detail:
# MediSync AI

## Problem & Challenge

Medical data is currently fragmented across physical notes, PDFs, and different hospital systems. Doctors spend nearly 30% of their time searching for patient history, which leads to delays in treatment and high risks of human error or misdiagnosis.

## Target Audience

Healthcare professionals, emergency room doctors, and clinical administrators who need rapid access to structured patient history.

## Solution & Core Features

Solution & Core Features:

Unified AI Timeline: Converts scattered medical records into a chronological, color-coded visual history.

Intelligent Summarization: Generates instant clinical summaries and critical health alerts (e.g., allergies, risks).

Smart Search: Allows doctors to find specific diagnoses or medications across years of records instantly.

Dynamic Patient Management: A seamless interface to register and track patients in real-time.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional EHRs (Electronic Health Records) that are data-heavy and slow, MediSync AI focuses on "Instant Clinical Context." It prioritizes life-saving information through AI-driven visualization, making the data "talk" to the doctor.

## Implementation & Technology

mplementation & Technology:

Frontend: React with Tailwind CSS for a high-performance, clinical UI.

AI Logic: Designed for integration with Databricks AI Agents for secure, large-scale medical data processing.

State Management: LocalStorage and Zustand for persistent patient data during the MVP phase.

Architecture: Modular file-based routing for scalability.

## Results & Impact

MediSync AI reduces the time spent on data retrieval from minutes to seconds. This leads to faster emergency response times, higher diagnostic accuracy, and ultimately, improved patient outcomes in high-pressure medical environments.

data.title: LabRun: Plan less, discover more.
data.summary:
LabRun transforms natural language scientific questions into fully operational, lab-ready experiment plans. It takes a hypothesis as input, performs a rapid literature quality check, and generates a complete plan including protocol, materials, budget, timeline, and validation strategy. Outputs are structured, interactive, and supported by source citations to ensure transparency.

A key differentiator of our product personalization. Users can upload available lab materials and equipment, enabling LabRun to tailor protocols, recommend only missing items, and adjust timelines based on real constraints. A built-in feedback loop allows the system to learn from expert corrections, continuously improving output quality.
data.detail:
# LabRun: Plan less, discover more.

## Problem & Challenge

Generating an experiment pipeline for your hypothesis of interest can be very slow, manual, and highly dependent on prior experience. Scientists must be knowledgeable in identifying required materials and equipment, estimating costs, and defining timelines. For an experienced scientist, this might not be a problem, but for someone with less experience, it can take multiple days or even weeks. In addition, errors in planning are likely to occur. Mistakes, such as incorrect concentrations, missing materials, different equipment, or underestimated timelines, can delay research significantly and cause additional costs. Although some existing tools focus on literature search or documentation, they do not address operational planning.

## Target Audience

Primary users are research scientists and lab technicians in academia, industry, and CROs who need to design experiments quickly and reliably. The tool is especially valuable for individuals or teams working in less familiar topics who need additional help with planning and initial hypothesis evaluation.

## Solution & Core Features

Six specialized AI agents, running in a dependency-aware parallel pipeline, that search real scientific databases, classify evidence by stance, audit each other for contradictions, and incorporate expert feedback into every regeneration — producing a complete, self-validated experiment plan from a single hypothesis.

## Unique Selling Proposition

LabRun goes beyond literature search and summarization by generating execution-ready experiment plans tailored to real laboratory conditions. While existing tools help users find information, they do not translate it into operational workflows or adapt to the specific constraints of a lab. A key differentiator is personalization. Users can upload the list of materials and equipment available in their laboratory, allowing LabRun to adapt experiment plans accordingly. Protocols are tailored to the lab’s setup, avoiding unnecessary steps or incompatible methods. Material procurement is also personalized, suggesting only missing items while aligning with preferred suppliers. Timelines are adjusted based on real constraints such as instrument availability and team capacity, making plans more realistic and immediately actionable. In addition, LabRun is a continuously improving platform. A built-in feedback loop allows scientists to review, correct, and refine generated plans. These corrections are stored and reused, enabling the system to learn from expert input and improve the quality of future outputs over time.

## Implementation & Technology

LabRun is built with a modern, scalable architecture designed for speed, reliability, and structured output generation. The frontend uses React, TypeScript, and Vite to deliver a fast, responsive, and strongly typed user interface, enabling seamless interaction with experiment plans and structured data.
The backend is powered by FastAPI with Python-based agents, allowing rapid API development and robust schema validation across the pipeline. This ensures consistency in how hypotheses, retrieved data, and generated plans are processed and returned.
For intelligence and grounding, LabRun combines OpenAI models with Tavily retrieval to access and incorporate relevant scientific knowledge. This hybrid approach ensures that generated experiment plans are both context-aware and grounded in real-world information. Outputs and intermediate data are persisted using SQLite or Postgres, providing flexibility for both lightweight deployments and scalable production environments.

## Results & Impact

LabRun reduces experiment planning time from days to minutes, improves the quality of experimental protocols, and thereby increases the overall likelihood of successful execution.
The tool produces structured, interactive reports that include all key components required for execution: protocol steps, materials list, budget plan, and timeline. These reports are editable, allowing users to provide feedback directly. This feedback is captured and reused, enabling continuous improvement of the system over time and increasing reliability and trust. In addition, all generated outputs include source references, making the plans transparent and credible for scientific use.
LabRun was built and validated by a biomedical scientist, ensuring a user-centric design. The system is developed from a user-to-technology perspective, making it intuitive, practical, and aligned with real laboratory workflows.

data.title: Cartographer
data.summary: Cartographer is an open, country-agnostic skills infrastructure that maps a young person's real abilities to standardized economic opportunities using live labor market data, not assumptions
data.detail:
# Cartographer

## Problem & Challenge

Over 600 million young workers in the Global South are economically invisible their informal skills are unrecognized, untranslatable across borders, and unmatched to real opportunities. Existing tools assume formal credentials, developed-economy data, and English-only users.

## Target Audience

Young people aged 18–30 in low-and-middle-income countries (LMICs) specifically those in informal or semi-formal economies (e.g., Amara, 22, Accra: phone repair + self-taught JavaScript). Secondary audience: policymakers and labor economists who need sector-level data to allocate youth programs.

## Solution & Core Features

Cartographer extracts a portable skills profile from a user's plain-language story, maps it to ESCO/ISCO-08 standards via Claude AI, and surfaces four opportunity types (formal jobs, self-employment, gig work, training pathways)  each annotated with real wage data, sector growth signals, and AI displacement risk calibrated for LMIC contexts. A policymaker dashboard shows sector-level econometrics with CSV export.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Three things competitors don't do simultaneously: (1) country-agnostic by design  all five parameters (labor data, credential taxonomy, language/script, automation calibration, opportunity types) switch without touching the codebase; (2) honest econometrics are visible to the user, not buried in an algorithm; (3) accepts zero formal credentials — a story is enough to start.

## Implementation & Technology

Framework: Next.js 15 + TypeScript (App Router, Server Components)
AI: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 with structured tool-use output (prevents hallucinated ESCO codes)
Data: Live World Bank WDI API (youth unemployment, GDP) + ESCO REST API + ILOSTAT/Frey-Osborne snapshots in /public/data/
i18n: next-intl with 15 locales (EN/FR/BN/AR/SW/HI/ZH + 8 more); RTL support for Arabic/Urdu
Charts: Recharts (policymaker dashboard)
Localization: IP-based country autodetect via Vercel/Cloudflare headers; 50-country registry with synthesized baselines for unlisted nations
Deployment: Vercel

## Results & Impact

Working prototype covering 50+ countries and 15 languages, with live API data. Demonstrates full country switch (Ghana → Bangladesh → Kenya) without code changes. Surfaces Wittgenstein 2025–2035 education projections, a 0–100 Resilience Score per occupation, and per-sector AI displacement risk calibrated for LMIC labor markets  directly addressing the brief's "show the data" requirement. Public API documented at /api-docs to reinforce the "infrastructure, not just an app" mandate.
