data.title: Localyse
data.summary:
Localyse is an AI-powered local commerce platform that helps small businesses create smarter, more profitable offers while giving customers personalized deals that actually match their preferences, budget, and mood.

For merchants, Localyse turns business data like inventory, sales, and margins into intelligent offer recommendations. Instead of randomly discounting products, merchants can upload their data and let the system suggest the right product, the right discount, the right timing, and the expected business impact. This helps them increase foot traffic, clear slow-moving inventory, and protect profit margins.

For customers, Localyse creates a personalized “For You” experience. Users set their preferences, and the app filters offers based on what they actually like. It also learns from claim history and previous behavior, so recommendations improve over time. A unique mood-based recommendation feature lets users choose how they feel, like tired, stressed, hungry, cozy, or focused, and the AI suggests the best available offer for that mood.

What makes Localyse powerful is that it connects both sides of local commerce. Businesses get data-driven decisions, and customers get relevant, timely, emotionally aware offers. The result is better deals for users, better conversion for merchants, and a smarter local marketplace.

In short, Localyse is not just another discount app. It is an AI decision engine for local businesses and a personalized deal companion for customers.
data.detail:
# Localyse 

## Problem & Challenge

Small local businesses often struggle to create effective discounts and offers because they lack data-driven decision-making tools. Most promotions are either random or based on intuition, which leads to low conversion rates, reduced profit margins, and poor inventory management. On the customer side, users are overwhelmed by irrelevant discounts that do not match their preferences, budget, or current needs, causing engagement fatigue and missed opportunities for meaningful savings.

## Target Audience

Primary: Small and medium-sized local businesses (retailers, cafes, restaurants, service shops) Secondary: Local customers looking for personalized and relevant deals Tertiary: Merchants and store managers who want to optimize inventory and increase foot traffic

## Solution & Core Features

Localyse is an AI-powered local commerce platform that connects merchants and customers through intelligent, personalized offers.
Core Features:

-AI-generated offer creation based on merchant data (inventory, revenue, cost, margins)
-Smart discount suggestions to maximize profit and clearance of slow-moving stock
-Budget-aware customer recommendations
-Personalized “For You” deal feed
-Mood-based offer system (e.g., hungry, tired, stressed, focused)
-Learning system that improves recommendations based on user behavior and claim history
-Location, weather, and time-based contextual offer optimization (via APIs)

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike traditional discount or coupon platforms, Localyse operates as a dual-sided AI decision engine that intelligently serves both merchants and customers. For merchants, it functions as a financial and inventory intelligence assistant that analyzes business data and recommends what to discount, when to apply it, and why it makes strategic sense. For customers, it acts as a personalized shopping assistant that understands their preferences, budget, and emotional state to surface the most relevant offers. This creates a real-time intelligent marketplace where offers are no longer static or random, but dynamically optimized to balance business profitability with user personalization.

## Implementation & Technology

Localyse is built using a modern full-stack architecture with a React or Next.js frontend and a backend powered by Node.js or Python (FastAPI/Express). The AI layer integrates large language models through services like OpenAI, Claude, or Tavily-based logic to generate intelligent offer recommendations. Merchants upload structured business data such as inventory, revenue, and costs via CSV or Excel files, which is processed and analyzed to generate insights. The system also integrates external APIs including location services for geo-based targeting, weather APIs for contextual offers, and a time-based logic engine to improve relevance. Data is managed using PostgreSQL or MongoDB, while the recommendation engine combines rule-based logic, LLM reasoning, and behavioral learning to continuously improve accuracy.

## Results & Impact

Localyse helps merchants significantly improve profit efficiency by eliminating unnecessary or poorly timed discounts and instead focusing on high-impact offers. It improves inventory turnover by intelligently targeting slow-moving stock and optimizing sales strategies. For customers, it enhances engagement by delivering highly personalized and context-aware offers that match their preferences and needs. This leads to higher conversion rates compared to traditional coupon platforms and creates a more meaningful shopping experience. Overall, Localyse builds a smarter local commerce ecosystem where both businesses and customers benefit through data-driven intelligence and personalization.

data.title: NeoUNMAPPED
data.summary: UNMAPPED is an open, localizable platform that connects young people in low and middle income countries to economic opportunity by making their informal skills visible. It maps real skills to international standards, assesses automation displacement risk calibrated for low and middle income realities, and matches users to opportunities grounded in real econometric data, with a policymaker dashboard for program officers. Any government or NGO can deploy it for their own country by adding a single config file, without making any changes.
data.detail:
# NeoUNMAPPED

## Problem & Challenge

Over 600 million young people in low and middle income countries hold real, demonstrable skills that broken credentialing systems and absent matching infrastructure make economically invisible. There is no connective tissue between what they can do and what the labor market needs.

## Target Audience

The primary audience are the young people in low and middle income countries with informal skills and no formal credentials. The secondary audience are governments, NGOs, training providers, and program officers who need aggregate data on youth skills and labor market readiness.

## Solution & Core Features

UNMAPPED maps informal skills to international standards (ESCO/ISCO-08), assesses automation displacement risk calibrated for low and middle income countries, and matches users to opportunities grounded in real econometric data. UNMAPPED has three modules: Skills Signal Engine, AI Readiness Lens, and Opportunity Matching, with a Program Officer Dashboard page.

## Unique Selling Proposition

UNMAPPED is not a product, but a configurable infrastructure layer. Any government or NGO can deploy it with their own country data by creating two files, without changing any code. It is the only skills-matching platform with automation risk explicitly calibrated for low-infrastructure labor markets, with the formula shown transparently to users.

## Implementation & Technology

UNMAPED is built on Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, and Recharts. Country context is driven by pluggable config files. The project includes optional GPT-4o integration for AI-powered skills mapping with full rule-based fallback. UNMAPPED has no backend or database, it is fully stateless and deployable anywhere. All data sourced from ILO, World Bank, ESCO, and Frey-Osborne indices.

## Results & Impact

UNMAPPED demonstrates the full end-to-end flow (from skills input to opportunity matching) across two live country contexts. The architecture proves that a single codebase can serve multiple countries without modification, making it immediately scalable to any LMIC context with minimal deployment cost.

data.title: City Wallet: The Autonomous Settlement Protocol for Local Merchant Liquidity
data.summary: An autonomous street protocol that turns the sparkasse wallet into a real-time market maker, using agentic negotiation to liquidate local merchant surplus into generative consumer bundles
data.detail:
# City Wallet: The Autonomous Settlement Protocol for Local Merchant Liquidity

## Problem & Challenge

Local commerce is a fragmented map of data silos. While giants like amazon use centralized intelligence to optimize every cent of margin, local shops are left blind. This isolation turns idle capacity into waste, empty seats, and surplus stock that can never be recovered. 
DSV sits on the richest data moat in Germany but it is currently passive. We are building the autonomous street protocol to turn this data into an action layer, solving merchant waste and consumer decision fatigue by making the bank the real-time coordinator of the neighborhood GDP.

## Target Audience

Primary target group: local smb merchants Small businesses on the high street with 'perishing' inventory or idle capacity. They lack the time and tools to run dynamic pricing or marketing but have the most to gain from 'automated liquidation'  Primary beneficiary: high-intent urban consumers Users who prioritize convenience and local quality but suffer from decision fatigue. They don't want a map of coupons, they want a 'generated solution' to an immediate intent   The strategic partner: DSV-gruppe  The entity that already owns the merchant and user accounts but needs an 'action layer' to stay relevant as the operating system for local commerce. They benefit from increased transaction volume and deeper merchant loyalty

## Solution & Core Features

The autonomous street protocol: an agent-driven coordination layer for the sparkasse wallet that turns passive POS and spending data into a real-time neighborhood market maker

Core features:
- Agentic negotiation: merchant agents publish 'pain' (surplus inventory) and user agents broadcast 'intent'. A DSV coordinator agent runs a real-time background negotiation to find a liquidation price
- Generative bundling: our engine creates 'ghost skus'. unique multi-merchant bundles that do not exist in any store system until the agent generates them for a specific user
- DSV clearing house: bank-grade settlement that handles instant multi-merchant payment splitting, underwritten by the sparkasse trust layer

## Unique Selling Proposition

Rather than building a 'coupon apps', we are building an autonomous market maker for the high street  our edge: - From static to generative: we don't show pre-existing deals, we generate unique financial agreements in real-time based on live merchant pain - the trust moat: we are using the bank as a 'liquidity oracle'. An agentic negotiation is only as good as the verified bank account backing it - Coordination as a service: we give small businesses the 'centralized intelligence' of Amazon without them losing their independence. Turning the bank from a vault into an operating system

## Implementation & Technology

City Wallet is built as a TypeScript Turborepo monorepo with three runtime apps (Consumer Wallet, Merchant Portal, API Gateway) and seven domain packages, so the same intelligence pipeline powers every surface.

Stack

Frontend: Next.js 15 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS.
Backend: Fastify API Gateway, Prisma ORM, SQLite (Postgres-ready).
Contracts: Zod schemas as single source of truth; type-safe and runtime-validated across client, server, and agents.
AI layer: Three cooperating agents
Context Assembler: turns raw signals into a privacy-filtered user context
User Negotiator: derives constraints, preferences, and urgency
Offer Negotiator: scores merchants and generates the bundle with a full reasoning trail
Settlement: Simulated DSV rail issues idempotent tokens (CW-SUSHI-14F), splits revenue in ~120 ms, emits analytics events (no_show_recovery, surplus_cleared).

Architectural principles
Domain-driven: business logic lives in isolated packages, not UI.
Privacy-by-design: raw biometrics never leave the device boundary; agents only see derived context.
Auditable: every offer produces a JSON trail (candidates, scores, reasoning, idempotency key) replayable by regulators or merchants.
Two-sided & real-time: merchant signals (no-shows, surplus stock) and consumer context flow into the same pipeline, enabling end-to-end no-show recovery in under 2 minutes.

## Results & Impact

- For merchants: reduces waste by converting idle inventory and empty capacity into revenue with near zero manual effort
- For consumers: gives them immediate, contextual offers instead of generic coupons or endless search
- For DSV: increases transaction volume, merchant loyalty, and relevance by turning the bank into the coordination layer for local commerce
- Broader impact: helps local shops compete with large platforms by giving them shared intelligence and real time coordination without losing independence

An agentic negotiation is only as good as the verified bank account backing it - Coordination as a service: we give small businesses the 'centralized intelligence' of Amazon without them losing their independence. Turning the bank from a vault into an operating system

data.title: TrustCare India — Agentic Healthcare Intelligence for Facility Discovery & Desert Detection
data.summary: Agentic AI system that turns 10,000+ messy Indian facility records into verified, trust-scored healthcare answers — with specialty desert detection and an interactive crisis map for NGO planning.
data.detail:
# TrustCare India — Agentic Healthcare Intelligence for Facility Discovery & Desert Detection

## Problem & Challenge

In India, discovery-to-care is often the bottleneck: families travel long distances only to find that a nearby facility lacks the required specialist, equipment, or emergency capability. Existing facility databases are unverified, full of contradictions, and provide no trust signal. There is also no tool that surfaces healthcare deserts — entire regions with critical specialty gaps — in an actionable way.

## Target Audience

Primary: Patients and families in rural and semi-urban India seeking verified care before making a long journey. Secondary: NGOs, government health planners, and policy teams who need actionable desert maps for resource allocation. Tertiary: Health system auditors who need to verify facility capability claims at scale.

## Solution & Core Features

TrustCare India is a 7-agent LangGraph pipeline that transforms raw facility data into explainable, trustworthy answers. Core features: (1) Capability Audit — audit_agent extracts and verifies clinical claims against row-level evidence. (2) Contradiction-Aware Trust Scoring — trust_scorer assigns a 0–100 score with explicit flags and a trust_evidence_map linking each flag to its source sentence. (3) Specialty Desert Detection — desert_finder identifies high-acuity gaps at PIN, city, and state level. (4) Self-Correction Loop — validator_agent retries and corrects responses before final delivery. (5) Interactive Crisis Map — Leaflet.js map filterable by state, priority, and severity with CSV export for planning workflows.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike simple facility search tools, TrustCare India does not just retrieve — it reasons. Every answer surfaces a trust score, contradiction flags, chain-of-thought reasoning, and row-level source citations. The system explicitly tells you why it trusts or doubts a claim, making it the only healthcare discovery tool in this space that is both agentic and fully explainable. The desert detection layer adds a policy-planning dimension no existing consumer tool offers.

## Implementation & Technology

Built on Python 3.11 with Flask as the API layer and LangGraph as the multi-agent orchestration framework. The dataset (10,000+ facility records) is normalized, embedded using Gemini embedding-001 (with a local HuggingFace fallback), and indexed in FAISS for fast multi-attribute retrieval. The 7-agent pipeline — intent, query, audit, trust, desert, validator, response — is wired as a LangGraph StateGraph in graph.py. The LLM backbone is Gemini 2.5 Flash with OpenAI as an optional fallback. Observability is handled via MLflow step-level tracing. The crisis map frontend uses Leaflet.js. Deployed on Vercel with a serverless adapter.

## Results & Impact

The system successfully indexes and reasons over 10,000+ real Indian facility records. It surfaces trust scores with evidence-backed flags, detects specialty deserts at PIN-code resolution, and exports crisis-ranked regions as CSV for NGO and policy use. The self-correction loop reduces hallucinated capability claims. The interactive map allows health planners to visually identify and prioritize underserved regions in seconds — a workflow that previously required manual data analysis over hours.

data.title: MachinaHub
data.summary: Lightning-settled marketplace for AI agents : Humans post tasks. Agents compete. The best submission wins the pot, paid in sats, seconds after the call. No accounts, no escrow, no platform tax.
data.detail:
# MachinaHub

## Problem & Challenge

Problem: Renting agentic work today means being locked into a handful of closed providers, with no way for arbitrary agents to compete on a task and get paid instantly per outcome.

Challenge: Build a permissionless settlement layer where any agent can join, compete, and get paid in seconds, without accounts, custody, or a platform sitting in the middle.

## Target Audience

Primary: Indie agent builders and AI tinkerers who run their own nodes (Ollama, fine-tuned models, custom AIDO stacks) and want a way to monetize them without begging for API access or building a SaaS.  Secondary: Bounty issuers, devs, researchers, small teams, who need agentic work done (code, content, reverse engineering, classification) and want results in minutes, paid per outcome, no subscription.  Tertiary: Lightning-native builders and the broader "agent economy" crowd (Spiral, Alby, Fewsats orbit) looking for real protocols, not demos, that move sats between machines.

## Solution & Core Features

olution: MachinaHub is an open protocol where humans post tasks with a Lightning bounty, any agent can submit a response, and the winning submission gets paid in sats the moment a verdict is reached.

Core features:

    Dual-mode duels. Race mode for deterministic tasks (first valid answer wins, auto-verified). Selection mode for subjective tasks (issuer picks the best of N submissions).
    Lightning-native settlement. Hold invoices lock bounties upfront, release to the Victor and refund Forfeits in seconds, no custody by the platform.
    Permissionless agent participation. Any node speaking the protocol joins, no accounts, no API keys, no whitelist.
    Live duel feed. Public stream of open tasks, ongoing duels, and verdicts, so agents can subscribe and bid in real time.
    RLHF-ready data trail. Every duel produces a structured record (task, submissions, winner, verdict reason) that becomes a clean preference dataset.
    Kill-and-reroute robustness. If a Combatant drops mid-duel, the protocol re-routes without breaking settlement.

## Unique Selling Proposition

USP: MachinaHub is the only marketplace where AI agents are the workers, paid per outcome in Lightning sats, with no platform sitting between them and their money.  Three angles depending on the audience:  For the agent economy crowd: the first protocol where machines earn directly from machines, settlement in seconds, no custody, no accounts.  For bounty issuers: post a task, get N competing submissions in minutes, pay only the winner, at Lightning speed, with zero subscription.  For the RLHF/data angle: every duel is a clean preference datapoint. The marketplace pays for itself, and the data exhaust is the moat.

## Implementation & Technology

Stack: Rust (Tokio + Axum) for the protocol core and node runtime, SQLite for duel state, SSE for the live feed, plain HTML/JS for the demo client.

Lightning: LDK or CLN via hold invoices — bounties locked on post, settled to Victor and refunded to Forfeits on verdict.

Agent side: any node speaking the protocol's JSON-over-HTTP spec; reference Combatant built on AIDO.

Verdict layer: Race mode runs deterministic asserts (test suite, regex, hash); Selection mode exposes a minimal UI for the Issuer to pick.

## Results & Impact

Results & impact:

For agents, a real revenue stream that doesn't depend on a closed API or a SaaS wrapper — any node, anywhere, can earn the moment it's online.

For issuers, work delivered in minutes instead of days, with N competing submissions instead of one locked vendor, and zero subscription overhead.

For the ecosystem, the first concrete proof that machine-to-machine payments aren't a thought experiment: every duel is a settled Lightning transaction between autonomous agents.

Long-term, the protocol generates a continuous stream of preference data — task, submissions, winner, reasoning — which becomes a public RLHF corpus that no closed provider can match.

Core features:

    Dual-mode duels. Race mode for deterministic tasks (first valid answer wins, auto-verified). Selection mode for subjective tasks (issuer picks the best of N submissions).
    Lightning-native settlement. Hold invoices lock bounties upfront, release to the Victor and refund Forfeits in seconds, no custody by the platform.
    Permissionless agent participation. Any node speaking the protocol joins, no accounts, no API keys, no whitelist.
    Live duel feed. Public stream of open tasks, ongoing duels, and verdicts, so agents can subscribe and bid in real time.
    RLHF-ready data trail. Every duel produces a structured record (task, submissions, winner, verdict reason) that becomes a clean preference dataset.
    Kill-and-reroute robustness. If a Combatant drops mid-duel, the protocol re-routes without breaking settlement.

jury_scope.notes: Temporary Event 5 live leaderboard display test

structured.implementation:
Stack: Rust (Tokio + Axum) for the protocol core and node runtime, SQLite for duel state, SSE for the live feed, plain HTML/JS for the demo client.

Lightning: LDK or CLN via hold invoices — bounties locked on post, settled to Victor and refunded to Forfeits on verdict.

Agent side: any node speaking the protocol's JSON-over-HTTP spec; reference Combatant built on AIDO.

Verdict layer: Race mode runs deterministic asserts (test suite, regex, hash); Selection mode exposes a minimal UI for the Issuer to pick.
structured.targetAudience: Primary: Indie agent builders and AI tinkerers who run their own nodes (Ollama, fine-tuned models, custom AIDO stacks) and want a way to monetize them without begging for API access or building a SaaS.  Secondary: Bounty issuers, devs, researchers, small teams, who need agentic work done (code, content, reverse engineering, classification) and want results in minutes, paid per outcome, no subscription.  Tertiary: Lightning-native builders and the broader "agent economy" crowd (Spiral, Alby, Fewsats orbit) looking for real protocols, not demos, that move sats between machines.

data.title: CliniXAI
data.summary: AI powered medical system
data.detail:
# CliniXAI

## Problem & Challenge

In India, a postal code can determine a lifespan. Despite having advanced medical hubs, nearly 70% of the population lives in rural areas where healthcare access is fragmented and unreliable. The core issue is not only the lack of hospitals, but a severe discovery and coordination crisis. Patients often travel hours to reach a facility, only to find it cannot actually provide the needed care—no ICU, missing oxygen supply, unavailable specialists, or insufficient emergency infrastructure.
This is further complicated by a large truth gap in healthcare data. Hospital records are often unstructured, inconsistent, and sometimes misleading, making it difficult to trust listed capabilities or verify real medical readiness. As a result, decision-making in critical situations becomes slow, uncertain, and high-risk, directly increasing discovery-to-care time.

## Target Audience

Patients in India (18+), especially in urban and rural areas with limited healthcare access, along with healthcare providers, NGOs, and government agencies.

## Solution & Core Features

We propose an Agentic Healthcare Intelligence System powered by the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, designed to transform 10,000+ unstructured medical facility records into a reasoning layer for Indian healthcare.
At its core, the system performs massive unstructured extraction, processing free-form hospital notes, equipment logs, staffing details, and availability claims. Instead of simple keyword search, it applies multi-attribute reasoning to understand real-world capability—such as ICU readiness, emergency surgery support, and specialist availability.
A key component is the Trust Scorer, which identifies inconsistencies and incomplete information in hospital data. For example, it flags cases where facilities claim advanced surgical services without supporting infrastructure like anesthesiologists or critical care units, helping reduce the truth gap in healthcare reporting.
To ensure transparency and reliability, we implement agentic traceability using MLflow 3, allowing every recommendation to be backed by step-level evidence from the source data.
On top of this intelligence layer, we build an AI Assistant Chat Mode, where users can describe symptoms or needs and receive instant, personalized hospital recommendations. The assistant uses real-time reasoning to minimize discovery-to-care time.
Additionally, a basic healthcare guidance mode helps users understand urgency levels and next steps before visiting a facility.

## Unique Selling Proposition

AI-powered hospital matching that combines real-time data, proximity, and medical capability—delivering fast, personalized healthcare recommendations through an intelligent assistant.

## Implementation & Technology

DataBricks, AI,

## Results & Impact

Our system converts a large, messy set of 10,000+ hospital records into a usable reasoning layer for Indian healthcare, making hidden medical capacity visible and actionable.
With massive unstructured extraction and multi-attribute reasoning, it improves decision accuracy in hospital selection and significantly cuts down discovery-to-care time, especially in rural and high-risk regions.
The Trust Scorer filters unreliable or contradictory data, reducing the impact of the truth gap in facility reports. At the same time, MLflow 3 agentic traceability provides clear, step-by-step justification for every recommendation, increasing transparency and trust.
On the user side, the AI Assistant Chat Mode enables fast, natural interaction for finding nearby care, while the basic healthcare guidance mode supports better-informed decisions before reaching a facility.

data.title: Nudge - It's local. It's waiting. It's yours.
data.summary:
Nudge connects local merchants with nearby customers at the exact moment both sides need it.
On the customer side, the app runs quietly in the background. It uses data that already exists on the devic, location, weather, time of day, and incoming notifications — to build an understanding of a person's daily routine. When something breaks that routine, like a delayed bus or an unexpected gap in the day, Nudge detects the anomaly and sends a single, relevant offer from a local merchant nearby. No browsing required. The user taps, sees the route, and gets the coupon. All processing happens on-device, keeping personal data local and the system fully GDPR-compliant.
The first time a user opens Nudge, a short setup flow captures basic preferences. From that point on, the app learns from behavior and refines its suggestions over time. A homescreen widget lets users actively choose when to engage with the tool.
On the merchant side, setup takes minutes. The owner sets two parameters — maximum discount and number of vouchers — and the system handles the rest. Nudge activates automatically when transaction volume drops or when external signals like weather suggest low foot traffic. Customers redeem offers by scanning a QR code in-store. Merchants see a simple dashboard showing how many new customers came in and how much additional revenue was generated.
A key part of the architecture is designed to incorporate transaction-level demand signals — such as those available through payment infrastructure like Payone — as a trigger for offer activation. This integration is a clear next step as the system scales.
We have already secured our first pilot partner, Gym Cook, where we plan to validate the application in a real-world setting in the near term.
Two problems. One system. No wasted attention on either side.
data.detail:
# Nudge - It's local. It's waiting. It's yours.

## Problem & Challenge

Local retail in Germany is disappearing — not because people stopped spending, but because small merchants have no infrastructure to compete against big Corporations who spend billions of Marketing,  to reach nearby customers when it matters.
At the same time, customers default to the same two or three places out of habit — not because they prefer them, but because nothing shows them what else exists within walking distance. The undiscovered café around the corner stays empty. The familiar one stays overcrowded.
The Challange for NUDGE is that its needs to connects both sides at the right moment. When a routine breaks, the app surfaces a relevant local alternative nearby.

## Target Audience

User Side Urban residents between 20 and 40 who organize their day through their phone. Their routines are consistent enough to detect when something breaks  and that is exactly when Nudge acts. Merchant Side Small independent businesses — cafés, retail, food — with no marketing budget and no dedicated staff to run campaigns. They have idle capacity they cannot fill and no infrastructure to reach customers in real time. Payone is already in place.Ist

## Solution & Core Features

- Anomaly detection on user routine: the system identifies deviations from established daily patterns — delayed transport, unexpected free time, weather shifts — and uses these as triggers
- Real-time context aggregation:  incomming device notifucatiosn, weather, GPS location, time of day, a are combined into a composite signal
- Payone transaction density as a demand proxy: merchant activity levels are used to detect idle periods and trigger offer generation automatically
- Generative offer engine: offers are created dynamically based on context — not retrieved from a static database
- Merchant-side rule interface: businesses set parameters once (maximum discount, voucher quantity, activation conditions) — execution is fully automated
- Data protection: Personal data never leaves the device. All processing runs locally via on-device SLM.
-Behavioral feedback loop: acceptance and rejection patterns refine future recommendations over time

## Unique Selling Proposition

Nudge turns a broken routine into a local discovery — the moment it happens, for both sides, automatically.

## Implementation & Technology

Architecture

A scalable, privacy-first system with three layers:

Backend (Firebase): Data aggregation, orchestration, APIs
Frontend (Flutter): Single codebase for iOS & Android
Apps: User and Merchant

Data Sources
Weather APIs
Geolocation
Payment signals (e.g., Payone)

Processing Pipeline

1. Interruption Detection: Identifies context changes (e.g., weather, location)
2. Ranking Engine: Scores nearby merchants based on preferences and context
3. Notifications: On-device SLM (Gemma 3 270M) generates personalized Messages based on context

Privacy
On-device AI: Personal data stays on the device

## Results & Impact

Claude responded: We built a working end-to-end application with real-time context detection, widget adaptability, and an intelligent notification system that scans incoming sig…We built a working end-to-end application with real-time context detection, widget adaptability, and an intelligent notification system that scans incoming signals, identifies behavioral anomalies, and serves both sides of the local commerce problem simultaneously.
For users, Nudge detects deviations in their daily routine and delivers a single, contextually relevant offer at the exact moment they are available — no active search, no pressure. For merchants, the system identifies idle capacity in real time and triggers targeted offers automatically, requiring no marketing effort or technical expertise.
The value is not theoretical. Gym Cook has committed as our first pilot partner and is actively testing Nudge in daily operations, providing live feedback on user behavior and merchant outcomes.
What we have achieved is a system where two disconnected problems — a customer with unexpected free time and a local business with an empty table — resolve each other automatically, in the moment, with a single tap.

data.title: MediAlert: AI-Powered Medical Facility Verification and Healthcare Desert Detection for India
data.summary: MediAlert is an AI-powered healthcare intelligence platform that validates the legitimacy of 10,000 Indian medical facilities, identifies regions with critical gaps in specialist care, and provides and citizens with trusted, actionable insights through an interactive map and WhatsApp interface.
data.detail:
# MediAlert: AI-Powered Medical Facility Verification and Healthcare Desert Detection for India

## Problem & Challenge

While India has many world-class medical hubs, they are only available to a select few. 70% of the population lives in rural areas where healthcare access is fragmented. The core crisis is not just a lack of hospitals but a discovery and coordination failure. Patients travel hours only to find a facility lacks the specialist or resources they urgently need. Compounding this, the data describing these facilities is messy, unstructured, and unverified.  Facilities often claim to have capabilities they don't actually have, and no standardized method exists to validate claims against.

## Target Audience

Citizens in rural India, Government health departments and NGO planners

## Solution & Core Features

MediAlert is a full-stack agentic healthcare intelligence system built on Databricks.

1. Data Pipeline: Raw facility data ingested and cleaned via PySpark on Databricks serverless compute. Delta Lake used for reliable, versioned storage of all tables. Three core tables produced: facilities_sql (structured metadata), facilities_for_embedding (unstructured notes), facilities_audit (contradiction flags).

2. Trust Scoring: Each facility receives a trust score (0–7) computed from social presence, staff data, logo verification, fact count, and follower metrics. Nine binary flag columns catch specific contradictions such as claiming ICU capability with zero beds, or offering surgery without an anaesthesiologist on record.

3. Two-Layer Validator Agent: 
 Layer 1: Deterministically checks facility notes for 8 specialties verifying required                       keywords, staff, equipment, and minimum bed counts
Layer 2: Uses databricks-meta-llama-3-3-70b-instruct to semantically confirm or override Layer 1 verdicts, extracting exact supporting or contradicting sentences from the notes and suggesting corrective actions.
Self-correction loop: When Layer 1 and Layer 2 disagree, the LLM provides an explicit override reason and reasoning chain, logged to MLflow.

4. Medical Desert Detection: State-level and PIN-code-level desert scores computed using facility count, trust scores, flag counts, and specialty gap detection across 8 critical specialties. Results saved to desert_scores.

5. Streamlit App:
Features in the streamlit app include: Find Facilities, Facility Status, Desert Map with filtered heatmap, Near Me distance comparison, and WhatsApp simulator. Validator Chain of Thought tab showing step-by-step reasoning per facility. All data served via Databricks SQL Warehouse.

6. WhatsApp Interface:
Twilio-powered conversational interface allowing citizens to query facilities via WhatsApp commands like find ICU Patna or deserts oncology. Location-aware prompting when no city is specified.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Combines LLM-powered self-correction, citation-backed contradiction detection, and specialty desert mapping on unverified, unstructured Indian healthcare data, all queryable via WhatsApp.

## Implementation & Technology

1. Databricks Serverless Compute with Unity Catalog for governance
2. Apache Spark / PySpark for large-scale data processing
3. Delta Lake for ACID-compliant storage and versioning
4. Databricks SQL Warehouse for low-latency query serving to the Streamlit frontend
5. databricks-meta-llama-3-3-70b-instruct served via Databricks Model Serving as the LLM validator
6. TF-IDF vectorisation (scikit-learn) as a pragmatic replacement for Mosaic AI Vector Search for semantic similarity search across facility notes
7. MLflow 3 for experiment tracking, agent tracing (SpanType.AGENT, SpanType.TOOL, SpanType.CHAIN), and artifact logging
8. Streamlit for the main web interface
9. Folium + streamlit-folium for interactive multi-layer maps with heatmaps, clustered markers, and desert score overlays
10. Plotly for inline bar charts and specialty heatmaps within Databricks notebooks
11. Twilio for WhatsApp messaging interface
12. OpenAI-compatible client pointed at Databricks serving endpoints for LLM calls
13. python-dotenv and Streamlit secrets for credential management

Pipeline notebooks:
01_cleaning.ipynb: data ingestion, cleaning, trust scoring, flag computation
02_embeddings.ipynb: notes blob construction for embedding table
03_agent.ipynb: primary search agent
04_validator.ipynb: two-layer validator, bulk audit, desert scoring
05_medical_deserts.ipynb: map generation and desert analysis
streamlit_app.py: full frontend application
filtered_heatmap_test.py: integrating filtered heatmaps into streamlit app

## Results & Impact

1. 10,000+ facilities processed, cleaned, and validated across India
2. 30,060 audit records generated with per-facility contradiction flags
3. 9 binary flag types catching specific capability mismatches
4. 8 medical specialties tracked for desert coverage at both state and PIN-code level
5. PIN-code level desert scores computed for every unique postal code in the dataset, giving NGO planners a directly actionable ranked list
6. LLM overrides rule-based verdicts in cases of alternate phrasing, reducing false negatives from rigid keyword matching
7. Every validation run is logged with metrics, artifacts, and reasoning chains giving health officials an auditable record of how conclusions were reached
8. Citizens without smartphones or app access can query the system via basic WhatsApp messages, dramatically lowering the barrier to access for rural populations

data.title: Talentify
data.summary: AI platform that turns real (formal + informal) skills into visible opportunities, matching people to jobs, reducing risk, and guiding reskilling across regions.
data.detail:
# Talentify

## Problem & Challenge

Millions of young people faces have real, valuable skills but those skills are invisible to employers and policy systems because they were never formally documented. This creates a structural mismatch: talent goes unrecognised, jobs go unfilled, and policymakers act on incomplete data. they also have a lack of job market understanding and its trends to navigate themselves to find the jobs.

## Target Audience

Employers and policy makers are the main audiences but talents are the users of the platform. also our secondary targets are education instituts and NGOs.

## Solution & Core Features

Talentify lets a young person describe their experience in plain language (plus uploading their CV), then maps it to the ESCO taxonomy using AI and vector search — generating a Skills Passport, automation risk score, real job opportunities, reskilling pathways, and a regional wage comparison. Policy makers get a separate dashboard with skill risk maps, a 30-country bubble map, skill-gap analysis, talent discovery, and AI-generated policy briefs.

## Unique Selling Proposition

we add a layer to discover the real skills with adding a self assessment that the talent describe untold skills in different ways and also let them and policymakers see the risk of AI in theri field to navigate the best possible future for each easier

## Implementation & Technology

FastAPI backend orchestrates a LangGraph multi-agent pipeline (Skills Signal Agent → Opportunity Agent → Dashboard Agent). Skills are embedded into ChromaDB using sentence-transformers; labour market data (wages, employment, HCI) is stored in DuckDB and sourced from ILOSTAT and World Bank WDI. The Next.js frontend renders interactive dashboards with Recharts and a react-leaflet bubble map. The LLM layer is provider-agnostic — supporting Groq, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, and Ollama via a single env-var switch.

## Results & Impact

6. we can profile a youth and return a full Skills Passport, matched opportunities, and reskilling recommendations in under 10 seconds — for any configured country with zero code changes. For policymakers, it turns raw ILOSTAT and World Bank data into an actionable briefing that would otherwise require a data analyst team, making evidence-based labour policy accessible even to under-resourced ministries.

data.title: UNMAPPED - PAINEERS
data.summary: We have developed a web-based platform that allows users to enter their region, skills, and level of education to generate a personalized employment profile. Based on this input, the system identifies and suggests job roles that best match the user’s capabilities and background.
data.detail:
# UNMAPPED - PAINEERS

## Problem & Challenge

Many young people—especially in informal and underserved regions—have valuable skills and experience, but these are not visible or discoverable online. As a result, they struggle to connect with realistic job opportunities, while policymakers and training providers lack reliable, data-driven insights to align education with labor market needs.
The challenge is to build a web-based prototype of an open, localizable infrastructure layer that bridges this gap by translating a person’s real skills into a standardized, portable profile and matching it to realistic economic opportunities.

## Target Audience

The solution is for people with skills seeking for jobs all over the world.

structured.problem:
Many young people—especially in informal and underserved regions—have valuable skills and experience, but these are not visible or discoverable online. As a result, they struggle to connect with realistic job opportunities, while policymakers and training providers lack reliable, data-driven insights to align education with labor market needs.
The challenge is to build a web-based prototype of an open, localizable infrastructure layer that bridges this gap by translating a person’s real skills into a standardized, portable profile and matching it to realistic economic opportunities.

data.title: Dr. Nexus
data.summary:
Dr. Nexus is an AI-powered scientific planning platform that turns natural-language hypotheses into execution-ready experiment plans within minutes.
Its multi-agent system combines literature review, protocol design, materials sourcing, budgeting, timelines, and validation into one transparent workflow.
By learning from researcher feedback through a knowledge graph, Dr. Nexus makes every future experiment plan smarter, faster, and more reliable.
data.detail:
# Dr. Nexus

## Problem & Challenge

Scientific progress is often slow not by lack of ideas, but by the operational complexity of experiments

Researchers and lab teams spend days or weeks manually translating scientific questions into executable plans: checking prior literature, designing protocols, selecting reagents, sourcing materials, estimating budgets, coordinating timelines, and defining validation criteria.

These users need fast, trustworthy, execution-ready experiment plans under time pressure.

## Target Audience

Laboratory researchers Principal investigators (PIs) Scientific founders & biotech startups R&D teams Lab operations managers Specialist labsContract Research Organisations (CROs) Biotech start-ups

## Solution & Core Features

The AI Scientist transforms a natural-language hypothesis into a complete, operationally realistic experiment plan.

Multi agent interconnected planning engine: Literature Scout, Protocol Designer, Materials Agent, Budget Agent, Timeline Agent, Review Agent, Validation Agent
Plan generation follows involvement of different roles checking each other (first planner to literature role, materials, budget awaits other roles, …)
Literature Quality Control: checks whether the experiment has already been done
Executable Experiment Draft: Protocol (based on tavily), materials (from tavily search with confidence annotation), budget, timeline, validation, 
Scientist review loop: give annotations that are integrated into an updated plan
Knowledge graph: stores research plan and stores feedback to prior research plans to anticipate desired plans in the future

## Unique Selling Proposition

Multi agent delegation - mirrors how real labs divide work across specialists Iterative learning - knowledge graph captures researchers annotations and project knowledge. It improves over time Operational validity - checked with real protocols, real material catalogues, real literature (tavily integration) Transparent generation process - every step is reviewable Report to PDF Ask questions about the  research plan, and its technical details through Personal Knowledge Graph (PKG)

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript, + Vite + Recharts + React Router DOM
Backend: Pxthon, FastAPI, superbase + Uvicorn + Pydantic + HTTPX + python-dotenv
KI/Agents: LangGraph (Multi-Agent-Orchestrierung), GPT-4o / Claude (LLM)
Datenbank: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Realtime, pgvector für Embeddings)
Suche: Tavily API (Literatur- & Protokoll-Discovery)

## Results & Impact

creation of executable science - in minutes not weeks
Every scientist correction makes the next plan smarter
We scale scientific expertise

fast experiment planning, fast validation of ideas, 
Comprehensive literature research, protocol research, material research
Validation of plans, consistent plans
Less dependency on individual senior researcher knowledge, better reuse of institutional expertise
Zero trust in AI required - every step reviewable
Automated creation of report
Researchers can focus on research, don’t waste time in administrative tasks
involvement of 2-15 researchers for days to weeks: saved many thousand euros per experiment

data.title: AI Scientist OS
data.summary: An AI-powered scientific experiment planner that transforms any hypothesis into a fully structured, lab-ready experiment plan. It is complete with protocols, reagents, budgets, timelines, and safety warnings — in seconds.
data.detail:
# AI Scientist OS

## Problem & Challenge

Scientists and researchers waste weeks translating a hypothesis into an actionable experiment plan. Writing protocols, sourcing reagents, estimating budgets, and building timelines requires deep expertise and significant time — creating a massive bottleneck between scientific curiosity and actual lab work. Early-stage researchers, students, and underfunded labs especially lack the resources to do this efficiently.

## Target Audience

University researchers, PhD students, Principal Investigators (PIs), science educators

## Solution & Core Features

AI Scientist OS is a web-based AI lab assistant. A user enters any scientific hypothesis in plain English and receives a fully structured experiment plan in seconds, organized into clear sections:

Protocol Steps — numbered, detailed lab procedures
Reagents & Costs — itemized materials list with USD estimates
Week-by-Week Timeline — realistic schedule a real lab could follow
Safety Warnings — critical precautions and handling notes
PI Summary — a concise executive brief ready to hand to a team

The output is displayed in a clean tabbed interface — each section accessible via a dedicated colored panel, making it easy to navigate and present.

## Unique Selling Proposition

It produces structured, actionable, lab-ready documents organized exactly the way a real research team needs them. No prompt engineering required from the user.

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend & Backend: 
Next.js frontend → FastAPI (Python) backend

AI & Agents:
Local LLMs via Ollama (Qwen3, LLaMA3, Mistral)

Data Sources:
PubMed, Semantic Scholar, CrossRef — for scientific literature
PubChem — for chemical/reagent data
SerpAPI — for web fallback search

Knowledge & RAG:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — answers grounded in real papers, not guesswork
pgvector (PostgreSQL) — semantic search across retrieved documents
Pydantic — structured, validated JSON outputs

Infrastructure:
Docker — fully containerized, single-command deployment

## Results & Impact

AI Scientist OS reduces experiment planning time from days or weeks to under 60 seconds. It provides access to structured scientific thinking — giving students, independent researchers, and underfunded labs the same planning capability as well-resourced institutions. In the long run, faster hypothesis-to-plan conversion means more experiments run, more discoveries made, and a lower barrier to entry for the next generation of scientists.

data.title: OpportunityMap
data.summary: UNMAPPED is an open infrastructure layer closing the gap between real skills and economic opportunity for 600 million youth in low and middle income countries. It provides a portable and data-driven professional identity via a minimalist web interface and a radical 2G SMS protocol for the digitally excluded.
data.detail:
# OpportunityMap

## Problem & Challenge

•⁠  ⁠Over 600 million young people in low and middle income countries possess valuable informal skills that remain economically invisible to the formal labor market.
•⁠  ⁠Traditional education credentials fail to translate into actionable labor market signals and leave employers unable to verify talent.
•⁠  ⁠High data costs and limited internet access exclude the most vulnerable youth from modern digital matching platforms.
•⁠  ⁠Rapid automation is shifting job landscapes faster than institutions can track and leaves youth without the data to navigate future risks.

## Target Audience

Invisible youth in emerging markets who lack formal credentials but possess demonstrated informal competencies.

## Solution & Core Features

•⁠  ⁠A dual access architecture featuring an intuitive and 250 KB minimalist web interface alongside a 2G SMS gateway for offline users.
•⁠  ⁠Module 01 (Skills Signal Engine) maps informal experiences to portable and human readable ISCO or ESCO profiles.
•⁠  ⁠Module 02 (Risk and Resilience Lens) provides automation readiness assessments calibrated specifically for local LMIC contexts.
•⁠  ⁠Module 03 (Econometric Dashboard) surfaces real world signals like ILOSTAT wage floors and sector growth for honest matching.
•⁠  ⁠A dual interface system that empowers individual users while providing policymakers with aggregate talent signals.

## Unique Selling Proposition

UNMAPPED is a lightweight, SMS-accessible open protocol that enables globally inclusive profile creation and grounded, data-driven matching, while remaining adaptable across regions without code changes.

## Implementation & Technology

•⁠  ⁠Skill mapping logic is grounded in international taxonomies including ISCO 08 and the ESCO Skills Taxonomy.
•⁠  ⁠Automation risk analysis incorporates Frey Osborne scores and ILO task indices calibrated for regional infrastructure.
•⁠  ⁠Future landscape projections integrate the Wittgenstein Centre 2025 2035 education and population datasets.
•⁠  ⁠The architecture supports a roadmap for local NGOs to act as validation nodes for human verified skill integrity.

## Results & Impact

•⁠  ⁠Provides a recognized professional identity to 600 million youth currently excluded from the formal economy.
•⁠  ⁠Empowers governments with the infrastructure to systematically map and mobilize their country's latent talent pools.
•⁠  ⁠Bridges the trust gap between informal skill claims and employer requirements through standardized and explainable data.
•⁠  ⁠Promotes long term economic mobility by guiding youth toward durable skills in a rapidly automating world.

data.title: UNMAPPED - LETS MAP THEM
data.summary: UNMAPPED turns informal work experience into a portable, internationally recognized skills profile and shows how exposed that work is to automation, calibrated to the user's actual country context using real ILO and World Bank data.
data.detail:
# UNMAPPED - LETS MAP THEM

## Problem & Challenge

Hundreds of millions of young people in low and middle income countries have real, demonstrable skills but no formal credentials to prove it. A motorcycle mechanic in Lahore, a phone repair technician in Accra, a house wiring worker in Nairobi are all invisible to formal labor markets. No credential check system recognizes them. No employer database has a record of them. At the same time, automation is arriving unevenly and these workers have no tool to understand which parts of their livelihood are at risk and what realistic next steps exist for them. The gap is not a lack of skills. It is a lack of infrastructure to make those skills visible, portable, and actionable.

## Target Audience

Primary users are youth aged 18 to 30 in low and middle income countries who work informally, have no university degree, and have never had their skills formally assessed. Secondary users are policy officers, NGO program managers, and government labor departments who need aggregate signals about skill gaps and automation exposure across their populations.

## Solution & Core Features

UNMAPPED is a two-module skills infrastructure layer. First, a guided conversational intake collects plain language work descriptions with optional speech input, asks a small number of AI generated follow up questions, and maps the result to an internationally portable ISCO-08 occupation profile with ESCO aligned skill labels. Second, an AI readiness view shows the raw Frey-Osborne automation probability for that occupation alongside an LMIC calibrated number adjusted for the user's country context, with a transparent explanation of why the two numbers differ. Live labor market signals from ILO ILOSTAT and World Bank WDI provide country specific context. Realistic upskilling paths and a downloadable profile card give users something tangible to keep and show.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most career tools start from resumes, degrees, or personality tests. UNMAPPED starts from plain language informal work. The core technical decision is computing readiness as raw automation probability multiplied by a country specific LMIC calibration factor, so a mechanic in Karachi gets a different risk score than the same occupation in Germany, because the economic infrastructure context is genuinely different. Every output is labeled as live, cached, or illustrative so users and judges can see exactly what the system knows and what it does not. The system is also config driven: adding a new country requires only one JSON entry with no code changes, which means any government or NGO can deploy a localized version without a developer.

## Implementation & Technology

Frontend is React 18 with Vite, deployed on Vercel. Backend is FastAPI on Python 3.11, containerized with Docker and deployed on Hugging Face Spaces. AI inference uses Groq API with llama-3.3-70b-versatile for profile mapping, follow up question generation, and readiness narratives, with rule based fallback when the API is unavailable. Data sources include the Frey-Osborne automation dataset, ISCO-08 taxonomy, and ESCO skills reference embedded locally, plus live ILO ILOSTAT and World Bank WDI calls with transparent cached fallback. Speech input uses the browser Web Speech API. Profile export uses html2canvas.

## Results & Impact

The prototype correctly maps informal work descriptions to ISCO occupations, applies country calibrated automation risk, and surfaces live or cached labor market signals for four configured LMIC countries: Pakistan, Ghana, Kenya, and Bangladesh. The system is extensible to any country by adding a single JSON config entry. A user starting from zero formal credentials can complete the full flow from intake to downloadable profile in under 5 minutes on a mobile device. The policy dashboard gives program officers a cross country view of NEET rates, calibrated automation risk, and labor market context from real international data sources.

data.title: UNMAPPED - the bridge between real skills and real opportunity
data.summary: A protocol that matches country-specific labor market signals to user skill profiles presenting economic opportunities for the user based on a matchmaking algorithm
data.detail:
# UNMAPPED - the bridge between real skills and real opportunity

## Problem & Challenge

Have you met Amara? If not, then let me briefly introduce her: She is 22, lives outside Accra, and holds a secondary school certificate. She speaks three languages, has been running a phone repair business since she was 17, and taught herself basic coding from YouTube videos on a shared mobile connection.

Amara does not want to continue her phone repair business for the rest of her life. She feels like she wants to do more, perhaps expand her coding skills and pursue a more technical profession. But she does not know where to go from here. Platforms like LinkedIn are not helpful to her as she does not feel like her strengths are seen, especially since they come from experience outside of academic settings.

At the same time, the government of Ghana offers a coding bootcamp that aims to develop coding skills of young people living in the country. However, they barely get any sign-ups for this opportunity, and the government officials do not know where to find people like Amara who would be perfect for the program.

## Target Audience

Young people in developing countries like Amara with no traditional academic degree; governments and NGOs that want to develop the skills of young people like Amara

## Solution & Core Features

We want to make Amara skills more visible, increase her self-awareness and provide real economic opportunities that she might want to pursue. 

Amara signs up to our user interface from any device in any country, selects the language she wants the user interface to be in, and starts a conversation with our chatbot about her life. On the one hand, she starts reflecting on her journey, and on the other hand, the AI chatbot helps her to identify her core skills by translating her answers into ESCO skills. After, Amara can review her skills by deleting the ESCO skills she fully disagrees with and adding skills she thinks are missing for her profile.

Now, if she wants to get clear suggestions on what roles she might want to pursue, she initiates the process of matching. A skill profile is sent to our configuration layer in our architecture that in combination with up-to-date economic data calculates the best matches between Amara and job roles. As an output, she sees detailed job descriptions in her country in combination with the adjacent skills she needs to develop. 

Our configuration layer works with real country-specific data which is provided by governments and NGOs. The government of Ghana can upload its relevant data such as labour market statistics through our second interface in the format they desire in order to help people like Amara understand what roles currently exist and how they will develop, taking into account salaries, industry growth, and education. 

Next to supporting better matchmaking between young citizens and job roles, the NGOs and governments can access some information from the user profile, allowing them to send notifications to young people with certain skills, describing programs and apprenticeship opportunities. The government of Ghana does not know Amara personally but through our protocol, they can reach out to her and offer her the coding bootcamp.

## Unique Selling Proposition

We focus on the strengths of a person developed beyond academic settings, using them to match young people in developing countries to real economic opportunity through the support of governments and NGOs

## Implementation & Technology

Layer 1: User Interface for Amara

The first layer entails a frontend where Amara can sign up, chat about her journey, and see the final suggestions for jobs. The backend is an AI agent that chats with Amara, generates follow-up questions, and maps the responses to ESCO skills. We decided to use the format of a chatbot as the conversation becomes more dynamic, and the AI can truly identify the right skills by clarifying responses. The tool also becomes more accessible to Amara as she has more freedom to explain the skills stemming from different experiences like working in the phone repair shop. This layer matches Amara’s responses quite fast to a user profile using ESCO standards as we implemented a preprocessing step of the responses such that skills can be quickly identified. 

Layer 2: Configuration Layer

In the configuration layer, we implemented the logic behind the matchmaking that is triggered by Layer 1. The inputs for the algorithm in our prototype are the user skill profiles, the ESCO and ISCO-08 occupation database, and ILOSTAT databases that represent labour market data for specific countries. Since in Layer 3, we encourage the upload of new up-to-date datasets, the matchmaking will also take into account the new data to create matches between user skill profiles and occupations. This algorithm is deterministic and works very fast as we decided against using AI in order to provide results based on real data, avoid hallucinations, and generate fast answers.

Layer 3: User Interface for the government of Ghana

The third layer entails a separate interface for governments and NGOs where they also sign up and can upload their country-specific labour market data. In exchange, we offer them the opportunity to access some information from the user skill profiles, allowing them to contact the users through our anonymous network.

Database Usage

For our configuration layer, the databases that list occupations and skills according to a standard are ESCO and ISCO-08. As ESCO is seen as an extension to ISCO-08 and many country-specific economic datasets entail ISCO-08 references, we used both in our tool, mapping each ESCO occupation and skill to its ISCO-08 reference. With ESCO, we are able to provide concrete job suggestions and identify specific skills. For our country-specific economic data, we use ILOSTAT. For example, we use the dataset “Employment by Sex and Occupation” to understand what professions and how many employees are currently employed in a specific country. Furthermore, we added more datasets such as “Average monthly earning by Sex and Occupation” to include even more labour market signals and allow for improved matchmaking. All ILOSTAT dataset entries have an ISCO-08 reference.

## Results & Impact

Our solutions create an architecture that solves the two problems from above: Amara gets a clearer understanding of her skills and how they might benefit her future, providing guidance in her professional career. And the government of Ghana supports this process by providing up-to-date economic data while also being able to provide Amara with information about their training programs.

Two problems are solved with one protocol, and through our solution, we expect that the economy in developing countries will be boosted in the years to come as young people like Amara have a clearer sense of their professional opportunities, allowing them to more easily pursue the job roles in which they can use their strengths best.

Amara can find a profession more easily that makes her feel fulfilled and thus actively contribute to the economy, while the government of Ghana can reach the people they need for their training programs, helping to further develop the professional skills of young citizens.

The government of Ghana can upload its relevant data such as labour market statistics through our second interface in the format they desire in order to help people like Amara understand what roles currently exist and how they will develop, taking into account salaries, industry growth, and education. 

Next to supporting better matchmaking between young citizens and job roles, the NGOs and governments can access some information from the user profile, allowing them to send notifications to young people with certain skills, describing programs and apprenticeship opportunities. The government of Ghana does not know Amara personally but through our protocol, they can reach out to her and offer her the coding bootcamp.

jury_scope.notes: Temporary Event 5 live leaderboard display test

structured.implementation:
Layer 1: User Interface for Amara

The first layer entails a frontend where Amara can sign up, chat about her journey, and see the final suggestions for jobs. The backend is an AI agent that chats with Amara, generates follow-up questions, and maps the responses to ESCO skills. We decided to use the format of a chatbot as the conversation becomes more dynamic, and the AI can truly identify the right skills by clarifying responses. The tool also becomes more accessible to Amara as she has more freedom to explain the skills stemming from different experiences like working in the phone repair shop. This layer matches Amara’s responses quite fast to a user profile using ESCO standards as we implemented a preprocessing step of the responses such that skills can be quickly identified. 

Layer 2: Configuration Layer

In the configuration layer, we implemented the logic behind the matchmaking that is triggered by Layer 1. The inputs for the algorithm in our prototype are the user skill profiles, the ESCO and ISCO-08 occupation database, and ILOSTAT databases that represent labour market data for specific countries. Since in Layer 3, we encourage the upload of new up-to-date datasets, the matchmaking will also take into account the new data to create matches between user skill profiles and occupations. This algorithm is deterministic and works very fast as we decided against using AI in order to provide results based on real data, avoid hallucinations, and generate fast answers.

Layer 3: User Interface for the government of Ghana

The third layer entails a separate interface for governments and NGOs where they also sign up and can upload their country-specific labour market data. In exchange, we offer them the opportunity to access some information from the user skill profiles, allowing them to contact the users through our anonymous network.

Database Usage

For our configuration layer, the databases that list occupations and skills according to a standard are ESCO and ISCO-08. As ESCO is seen as an extension to ISCO-08 and many country-specific economic datasets entail ISCO-08 references, we used both in our tool, mapping each ESCO occupation and skill to its ISCO-08 reference. With ESCO, we are able to provide concrete job suggestions and identify specific skills. For our country-specific economic data, we use ILOSTAT. For example, we use the dataset “Employment by Sex and Occupation” to understand what professions and how many employees are currently employed in a specific country. Furthermore, we added more datasets such as “Average monthly earning by Sex and Occupation” to include even more labour market signals and allow for improved matchmaking. All ILOSTAT dataset entries have an ISCO-08 reference.
structured.targetAudience: Young people in developing countries like Amara with no traditional academic degree; governments and NGOs that want to develop the skills of young people like Amara

data.title: AI Scientist: From Hypothesis to Runnable Experiment Plan
data.summary: AI Scientist is an evidence-grounded research planning assistant that turns a scientific hypothesis into a fast literature novelty check and a runnable experiment plan with protocol, materials, budget, timeline, validation, and scientist feedback memory.
data.detail:
# AI Scientist: From Hypothesis to Runnable Experiment Plan

## Problem & Challenge

Turning a scientific idea into a runnable experiment plan is slow, manual, and inconsistent. Researchers must search prior literature, assess novelty, design a protocol, estimate cost and timeline, source materials, and define validation criteria. Small mistakes in concentrations, assumptions, or dependencies can make a plan unusable and waste days or weeks.

## Target Audience

Our primary users are scientists, PIs, research associates, CRO teams, and R&D groups that need to move quickly from a research question to an operationally realistic experiment plan. It is especially useful for teams exploring unfamiliar experiment types where prior experience is limited.

## Solution & Core Features

AI Scientist is an end-to-end scientific planning system that turns a plain-English hypothesis into a literature-backed, operationally realistic experiment plan. The workflow starts by parsing the hypothesis into structured scientific fields such as intervention, model system, endpoint, threshold, control, and mechanism. That structure is then used to route the request into the right experiment family, improve retrieval, and generate a more specific plan.

The system performs a fast literature QC step that classifies the idea as not found, similar work exists, or exact match found, and returns 1–3 relevant references with direct links, metadata, and short “why relevant” explanations. From there, it generates a structured experiment plan with grounded protocol steps, materials and supplier context, budget line items, dependency-aware timeline visualization, and explicit validation criteria.

To improve quality, we moved away from shallow one-shot generation and added several architectural layers: experiment-family routing, stronger literature retrieval and ranking, protocol grounding, material/supplier grounding, runnability checks, and decision-aware planning. The system now distinguishes grounded versus inferred steps, shows evidence more clearly, avoids wrong-domain fallbacks more safely, and supports an optional parsed-fields review step so scientists can refine extracted assumptions before generation.

A key feature is the scientist feedback loop. A scientist can leave structured corrections on sections like protocol, materials, budget, or timeline. Those corrections are stored with experiment-family, task, system-context, and tag metadata. On future similar runs, the system retrieves the most relevant past corrections and feeds them back into generation as guidance. This is a few-shot memory loop, not fine-tuning: the model is not retrained or updated at the weight level, but the system remembers expert corrections and reuses them at generation time so similar plans improve automatically.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Unlike a generic chat app, AI Scientist is built as a research-planning workflow rather than a text generator. It combines experiment-family routing, evidence-grounded planning, structured outputs, trust checks, and scientist feedback memory, so users get a plan they can inspect, critique, and improve over time instead of a one-off answer.

## Implementation & Technology

The app is built with Next.js and TypeScript using an App Router frontend plus API routes. It uses structured LLM generation for parsing and planning, a route-aware planner architecture for different experiment families, retrieval and QC scoring for literature checks, protocol and material grounding layers, SQLite-backed review memory, and UI components for evidence review, timeline visualization, and final plan export.

## Results & Impact

We built a working end-to-end application that takes a hypothesis, performs literature QC, and produces a complete experiment plan with protocol, materials, budget, timeline, and validation. We also added scientist feedback memory, runnability checks, clickable evidence access, and a cleaner 3-step flow, making the system much more credible for a demo and closer to a real research-planning assistant.
tags: SQLite feedback memory and runnability checks.

data.title: AgentWork
data.summary:
AgentWork is a Lightning-native labor protocol for autonomous AI agents. It lets agents request task access, bid on work, verify outputs, and unlock payment-gated workflows through L402-style access control. The core live proof shows an unpaid request returning HTTP 402 Payment Required and an L402 proof unlocking access with HTTP 200.

https://v0-agentwork.vercel.app/
data.detail:
# AgentWork

## Problem & Challenge

AI agents are becoming autonomous workers: they can browse, call APIs, complete tasks, and coordinate workflows. But today’s payment systems were built for humans, not agents. Credit cards require checkout pages, bank transfers are slow, API billing requires accounts and keys, and traditional rails cannot support tiny instant machine payments.

This creates a major bottleneck for the agent economy. If agents are going to work for each other, post tasks, access paid APIs, and receive compensation, they need payment-native infrastructure that works programmatically.

AgentWork addresses this gap by turning task access and labor coordination into a protocol-level payment flow. Instead of asking an agent to click a payment button, the system can return HTTP 402 Payment Required and unlock access only when payment proof is supplied.

## Solution & Core Features

AgentWork is built for developers, AI-agent platforms, automation builders, protocol teams, and marketplaces that want autonomous agents to perform paid work.

The primary users are:
- AI agents that need to access paid tasks or APIs
- builders creating agent marketplaces
- businesses that want to outsource repeatable digital work to agents
- protocol developers exploring L402 and Lightning-native machine payments
- future agent networks that need reputation, verification, and settlement infrastructure

In the long term, AgentWork targets the emerging agent economy: a world where software agents can earn, pay, and coordinate work without human checkout flows.

## Unique Selling Proposition

Most AI marketplace demos show a payment button. AgentWork is different because it demonstrates payment as a protocol layer.  The key differentiator is L402-style access control: an unpaid agent request receives HTTP 402 Payment Required, and a request with payment proof receives HTTP 200 Access Granted. This means agents can interact with paid services through HTTP itself, without human checkout pages, accounts, API keys, credit cards, or manual billing.  AgentWork is not just a task board. It combines task access, bidding, verification, reputation, pipeline execution, salary-stream concepts, and Lightning-native payment logic into one agent labor protocol. This makes it better suited for the future agent economy, where autonomous software needs to pay, earn, and coordinate work at machine speed.

## Implementation & Technology

AgentWork is implemented as a Next.js App Router application with TypeScript and deployed on Vercel. The backend uses API routes for the core protocol layer, including task lifecycle, agent bidding, completion and verification, agent stats, salary streams, pipeline execution, demo orchestration, MDK webhook handling, and L402 task access.

Supabase is used as the data foundation for protocol entities such as agents, tasks, bids, transactions, pipelines, and salary streams. The frontend prototype was built with a premium v0 interface to visualize the full product experience: task marketplace, live bids, Lightning feed, top agents, verification, salary mode, pipeline flow, and economy dashboard.

The strongest working technical proof is the deployed L402 endpoint. A POST request without authorization returns HTTP 402 with an L402 challenge. A POST request with demo L402 proof returns HTTP 200 and unlocks task access. MoneyDevKit was integrated for Lightning checkout infrastructure, with the MDK webhook route deployed and POST-only.

## Results & Impact

The project successfully demonstrates the core idea of machine-native labor access. The deployed L402 proof shows that an agent can be blocked with HTTP 402 Payment Required and then granted access with payment proof. This proves the central thesis of AgentWork: Lightning is not a decorative payment option, but a protocol-level access mechanism for autonomous agents.

Beyond the L402 proof, AgentWork includes a broad route-backed foundation for task posting, bidding, completion, verification, salary streams, pipeline execution, protocol metrics, and demo orchestration. The visual dashboard shows how these pieces come together into an AI labor market where agents compete, build reputation, complete tasks, and coordinate settlement states.

The value of AgentWork is that it points toward a future where agents can participate in digital labor markets without human checkout flows. Developers could use this model to build paid APIs, agent marketplaces, automated verification systems, and streaming compensation models for continuous AI work.
